```py
  dev_deps = data.get("dependency-groups", {}).get("dev", [])
  all_deps_list = project_deps + dev_deps
  parsed_deps = {}
  for dep in all_deps_list:
    name, constraint = parse_dependency_string(dep)
    parsed_deps[name.lower()] = constraint
  return parsed_deps
def find_best_compatible_version(releases: list[str], constraint_str: str) -> str:
  if constraint_str.startswith(" @") or "http" in constraint_str:
    return "Direct URL"
  specifier = SpecifierSet(constraint_str)
  valid_versions = []
  for r in releases:
    try:
      v = parse(r)
      if not v.is_prerelease and specifier.contains(v):
        valid_versions.append(v)
    except InvalidVersion:
      continue
  if not valid_versions:
    return "No Compatible Found"
  return str(max(valid_versions))
def analyze_gap(current_str: str, target_str: str, ignore_gap: bool = False) -> str:
  if ignore_gap or "http" in current_str:
    return ""
  try:
    clean_current = (
      current_str.replace("^", "")
      .replace("~", "")
      .replace(">=", "")
      .replace("==", "")
    )
    c = parse(clean_current)
    t = parse(target_str)
    if t.major > c.major:
      return "⚠️ Major Version Gap"
    if t.minor - c.minor >= 3:
      return "⚠️ Significant Lag (>3 minors)"
  except (InvalidVersion, AttributeError):
    pass
  return ""
async def fetch_pypi_version(
  session: aiohttp.ClientSession, package: str, constraint_str: str
) -> DependencyInfo:
  is_direct_url = "http" in constraint_str
  if is_direct_url:
    return DependencyInfo(
      name=package,
      current_constraint="Direct URL",
      target_version="Manual Check",
      needs_update=False,
      url="N/A",
      note="Source is not PyPI",
      is_pinned=False,
      is_direct_url=True,
    )
  url = PYPI_URL.format(package)
  try:
    async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as response:
      if response.status != 200:
        return DependencyInfo(
          package,
          constraint_str,
          "Not Found",
          False,
          url,
          "PyPI Error",
          False,
          False,
        )
      data = await response.json()
      project_url = (
        data["info"]["home_page"] or data["info"]["project_url"] or url
      )
      override_config = CONSTRAINT_OVERRIDES.get(package.lower())
      is_pinned = False
      note = ""
      gap_warning = ""
      if override_config:
        override_constraint = override_config["constraint"]
        all_releases = list(data["releases"].keys())
        target = find_best_compatible_version(all_releases, override_constraint)
        latest_real = data["info"]["version"]
        note = f"Pinned {override_constraint}: {override_config['reason']}"
        is_pinned = True
        gap_warning = analyze_gap(
          constraint_str,
          latest_real,
          ignore_gap=override_config.get("ignore_gap", False),
        )
        if gap_warning:
          target = f"{target} (Latest: {latest_real})"
      else:
        target = data["info"]["version"]
      clean_current = (
        constraint_str.replace("^", "")
        .replace("~", "")
        .replace(">=", "")
        .replace("==", "")
      )
      try:
        needs_update = parse(target.split()[0]) > parse(clean_current)
      except (InvalidVersion, IndexError):
        needs_update = False
      return DependencyInfo(
        name=package,
        current_constraint=constraint_str,
        target_version=target,
        needs_update=needs_update,
        url=project_url,
        note=note,
        is_pinned=is_pinned,
        is_direct_url=False,
        gap_warning=gap_warning,
      )
  except Exception as e:
    return DependencyInfo(
      package, constraint_str, "Error", False, url, str(e), False, False
    )
def generate_excel(results: list[DependencyInfo]) -> None:
  if not HAS_EXCEL:
    return
  wb = Workbook()
  ws = wb.active
  ws.title = "Dependency Audit"
  headers = [
    "Package",
    "Current Constraint",
    "Target Version",
    "Status",
    "Note",
    "URL",
  ]
  ws.append(headers)
  header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
  header_fill = PatternFill(
    start_color="4F81BD", end_color="4F81BD", fill_type="solid"
  )
  alert_font = Font(bold=True, color="C00000")
  warn_font = Font(bold=True, color="E26B0A")
  grey_font = Font(color="808080")
  for cell in ws[1]:
    cell.font = header_font
    cell.fill = header_fill
  for r in results:
    status_text = "OK"
    final_note = r.note
    if r.needs_update:
      status_text = "UPDATE NEEDED"
    elif r.is_pinned:
      status_text = "PINNED"
      if r.gap_warning:
        final_note = f"{r.note} | {r.gap_warning}"
    elif r.is_direct_url:
      status_text = "MANUAL"
    row = [
      r.name,
      r.current_constraint,
      r.target_version,
      status_text,
      final_note,
      r.url,
    ]
    ws.append(row)
    last_row = ws[ws.max_row]
    if r.needs_update:
      for cell in last_row:
        cell.font = alert_font
    elif r.gap_warning:
      for cell in last_row:
        cell.font = warn_font
    elif r.name == "en-core-web-sm":
      for cell in last_row:
        cell.font = grey_font
  for column_cells in ws.columns:
    length = max(len(str(cell.value)) for cell in column_cells)
    ws.column_dimensions[get_column_letter(column_cells[0].column)].width = (
      length + 2
    )
  wb.save(XLSX_REPORT_PATH)
  print(f"📊 Excel report generated at: {XLSX_REPORT_PATH}")
async def main() -> bool:
  deps = get_dependencies()
  print(f"🔍 Analyzing {len(deps)} dependencies (uv/PEP 621)...")
  async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    tasks = [
      fetch_pypi_version(session, name, const) for name, const in deps.items()
    ]
    results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
  spacy_needs_update = any(r.name == "spacy" and r.needs_update for r in results)
  processed_results = []
  for r in results:
    if r.name == "en-core-web-sm":
      new_note = r.note
      if spacy_needs_update:
        new_note = "⚠️ UPDATE URL (Spacy updated)"
      r = r._replace(note=new_note)
    processed_results.append(r)
  results = processed_results
  def sort_key(r: DependencyInfo):
    if r.name == "en-core-web-sm":
      return 5
    if r.needs_update:
      return 0
    if r.is_pinned and r.gap_warning:
      return 1
    if r.is_pinned:
      return 2
    if r.is_direct_url:
      return 3
    return 4
  results.sort(key=lambda x: (sort_key(x), x.name))
  updates_found = any(r.needs_update for r in results)
  pinned_items = [r for r in results if r.is_pinned]
  timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
  generate_excel(results)
  summary_lines = [
    f"# 📦 Dependency Audit - {timestamp}",
    "",
    "**Full analysis available in the attached Excel artifact.**",
    "",
    "### Summary",
    f"- Total Dependencies: {len(results)}",
    f"- Updates Available: {sum(1 for r in results if r.needs_update)}",
    f"- Pinned/Held Back: {len(pinned_items)}",
    "",
  ]
  if updates_found:
    summary_lines.append("### 🚀 Updates Recommended")
    for r in results:
      if r.needs_update:
        summary_lines.append(
          f"- **{r.name}**: `{r.current_constraint}` -> "
          f"**{r.target_version}** {r.note}"
        )
    summary_lines.append("")
  if pinned_items:
    summary_lines.append("### ⚓ Pinned Packages (Intentional)")
    for r in pinned_items:
      warning_icon = "⚠️ " if r.gap_warning else ""
      summary_lines.append(
        f"- **{r.name}**: {r.note}. Target: {r.target_version} "
        f"{warning_icon}{r.gap_warning}"
      )
    summary_lines.append("")
  spacy_model = next((r for r in results if r.name == "en-core-web-sm"), None)
  if spacy_model and "UPDATE URL" in spacy_model.note:
    summary_lines.append("### ⚠️ Manual Action Required")
    summary_lines.append(f"- **en-core-web-sm**: {spacy_model.note}")
  with open(SUMMARY_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("\n".join(summary_lines))
  print(f"📝 Summary generated at: {SUMMARY_PATH}")
  return updates_found
if __name__ == "__main__":
  try:
    asyncio.run(main())
  except KeyboardInterrupt:
    print("\nCancelled.")
```

### `scripts/evaluate_pdf_anki_dspy.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path
from app.core.dspy_compilation import (
  PdfAnkiFeedbackExample,
  compile_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact,
  evaluate_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact,
  load_jsonl_models,
)
from app.core.dspy_policy import PdfAnkiDSPyPromptArtifact
def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
  parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    description="Evaluate a PDF-Anki DSPy artifact against feedback JSONL."
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--input",
    type=Path,
    default=Path("config/dspy/examples/pdf_anki_feedback_trainset.jsonl"),
    help="JSONL feedback dataset path",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--artifact",
    type=Path,
    default=Path("config/dspy/pdf_anki.json"),
    help="Artifact JSON path",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--report",
    type=Path,
    default=None,
    help="Optional JSON report path",
  )
  return parser.parse_args()
def main() -> int:
  args = _parse_args()
  examples = load_jsonl_models(args.input, PdfAnkiFeedbackExample)
  current_artifact = PdfAnkiDSPyPromptArtifact.model_validate(
    json.loads(args.artifact.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
  )
  compiled_artifact = compile_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact(examples)
  current_metrics = evaluate_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact(examples, current_artifact)
  compiled_metrics = evaluate_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact(examples, compiled_artifact)
  report = {
    "dataset_examples": len(examples),
    "current_artifact": current_metrics.model_dump(),
    "compiled_candidate": compiled_metrics.model_dump(),
    "alignment_delta": round(
      compiled_metrics.alignment_score - current_metrics.alignment_score,
      4,
    ),
  }
  if args.report is not None:
    args.report.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    args.report.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
  print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
  return 0
if __name__ == "__main__":
  raise SystemExit(main())
```

### `scripts/prod_smoke_test.py`
```py
import sys
import time
import requests
PROD_URL = "https://api.tu-dominio.com"
HEADERS = {
  "Content-Type": "application/json",
  "X-Tenant-ID": "system-smoke-test",
  "X-Request-ID": f"smoke-{int(time.time())}",
}
def check_health():
  try:
    print(f"🩺 Checking Health at {PROD_URL}...")
    resp = requests.get(f"{PROD_URL}/api/v1/", timeout=5)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    data = resp.json()
    if data.get("database") != "connected":
      print("❌ Database reported disconnected!")
      sys.exit(1)
    print("✅ Health Check Passed.")
  except Exception as e:
    print(f"❌ Health Check Failed: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)
def check_inference():
  payload = {"prompt": "Sanity check for production deployment."}
  try:
    print("🧠 Checking AI Inference...")
    start = time.time()
    resp = requests.post(
      f"{PROD_URL}/api/v1/test-router",
      json=payload,
      headers=HEADERS,
      timeout=15,
    )
    resp.raise_for_status()
    latency = time.time() - start
    data = resp.json()
    if not data.get("response"):
      print("❌ Empty response from LLM!")
      sys.exit(1)
    print(
      f"✅ Inference Passed (Latency: {latency:.2f}s). "
      f"Model: {data.get('model_used')}"
    )
  except Exception as e:
    print(f"❌ Inference Failed: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)
if __name__ == "__main__":
  check_health()
  check_inference()
  print("🚀 PRODUCTION SMOKE TEST PASSED.")
```

### `scripts/dump_decrypted_user_rows.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import argparse
import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import sys
import uuid
from collections.abc import Callable
from datetime import date, datetime
from decimal import Decimal
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any
from sqlalchemy import text
_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
_src = _root / "src"
if str(_src) not in sys.path:
  sys.path.insert(0, str(_src))
os.environ.setdefault("MUTANT_UNDER_TEST", "fail")
from app.infrastructure.database import sessionmanager  # noqa: E402
from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.pdf_anki_ale import (  # noqa: E402
  decrypt_field as decrypt_pdf_anki_field,
)
from app.modules.receipt_parser.infrastructure.receipt_ale import (  # noqa: E402
  decrypt_field as decrypt_receipt_field,
)
_SAFE_IDENTIFIER_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-z_][a-z0-9_]{0,62}$")
def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
  parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    description=(
      "Dump matching table rows as JSON, decrypting encrypted string fields "
      "when possible."
    )
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "table",
    help="Database table name, for example receipt_parser_users",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "match_value",
    help="Value used in the WHERE filter, usually the user_id",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--match-column",
    default="user_id",
    help="Column used in the WHERE filter. Default: user_id",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--limit",
    type=int,
    default=100,
    help="Maximum number of rows to return. Default: 100",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--decryptor",
    choices=("auto", "receipt_parser", "pdf_anki", "none"),
    default="auto",
    help="Decryptor strategy. Default: auto",
  )
  return parser.parse_args()
def _validate_identifier(name: str) -> str:
  clean_name = str(name).strip()
  if not _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_RE.match(clean_name):
    raise ValueError(f"Unsafe SQL identifier rejected: {clean_name!r}")
  return clean_name
def _pick_decryptor(
  table: str,
  decryptor_name: str,
) -> Callable[[str | None], str | None]:
  if decryptor_name == "none":
    return lambda value: value
  if decryptor_name == "receipt_parser":
    return decrypt_receipt_field
  if decryptor_name == "pdf_anki":
    return decrypt_pdf_anki_field
  if table.startswith("pdf_anki") or table.startswith("pdf_"):
    return decrypt_pdf_anki_field
  return decrypt_receipt_field
def _decrypt_value(value: Any, decryptor: Callable[[str | None], str | None]) -> Any:
  if isinstance(value, str):
    return decryptor(value)
  if isinstance(value, dict):
    return {
      str(key): _decrypt_value(inner_value, decryptor)
      for key, inner_value in value.items()
    }
  if isinstance(value, list):
    return [_decrypt_value(item, decryptor) for item in value]
  if isinstance(value, tuple):
    return [_decrypt_value(item, decryptor) for item in value]
  return value
def _jsonable(value: Any) -> Any:
  if isinstance(value, dict):
    return {str(key): _jsonable(inner_value) for key, inner_value in value.items()}
  if isinstance(value, list):
    return [_jsonable(item) for item in value]
  if isinstance(value, tuple):
    return [_jsonable(item) for item in value]
  if isinstance(value, (datetime, date)):
    return value.isoformat()
  if isinstance(value, Decimal):
    return format(value, "f")
  if isinstance(value, uuid.UUID):
    return str(value)
  return value
async def _fetch_rows(
  *,
  table: str,
  match_column: str,
  match_value: str,
  limit: int,
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
  sessionmanager.initialize()
  query_text = (
    f"SELECT * FROM {table} "  # noqa: S608
    f"WHERE {match_column} = :match_value "
    "LIMIT :limit_value"
  )
  query = text(query_text)
  async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
    result = await session.execute(
      query,
      {"match_value": match_value, "limit_value": limit},
    )
    return [dict(row) for row in result.mappings().all()]
async def _async_main(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
  table = _validate_identifier(args.table)
  match_column = _validate_identifier(args.match_column)
  match_value = args.match_value.strip()
  limit = int(args.limit)
  if not match_value:
    print("match_value cannot be empty.", file=sys.stderr)
    return 1
  if limit <= 0:
    print("--limit must be a positive integer.", file=sys.stderr)
    return 1
  decryptor = _pick_decryptor(table, args.decryptor)
  try:
    rows = await _fetch_rows(
      table=table,
      match_column=match_column,
      match_value=match_value,
      limit=limit,
    )
  finally:
    await sessionmanager.close()
  decrypted_rows = [
    {
      column: _jsonable(_decrypt_value(value, decryptor))
      for column, value in row.items()
    }
    for row in rows
  ]
  payload = {
    "table": table,
    "match_column": match_column,
    "match_value": match_value,
    "row_count": len(decrypted_rows),
    "rows": decrypted_rows,
  }
  print(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
  return 0
def main() -> None:
  args = _parse_args()
  raise SystemExit(asyncio.run(_async_main(args)))
if __name__ == "__main__":
  main()
```

### `scripts/staging_warmup.py`
```py
import asyncio
import os
import sys
import time
import httpx
STAGING_URL = os.getenv("STAGING_URL", "http://localhost:8000")
API_KEY = os.getenv("API_KEY", "test-key")
async def run_warmup() -> None:
  print(f"🔥 Starting Warm-up Test against {STAGING_URL}...")
  headers = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {API_KEY}",
    "X-Tenant-ID": "ci-cd-pipeline",
  }
  payload = {"prompt": "Hello, this is a warmup request. Reply with 'OK'."}
  async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=15.0) as client:
    start_time = time.perf_counter()
    try:
      resp = await client.post(
        f"{STAGING_URL}/api/v1/test-router", json=payload, headers=headers
      )
      resp.raise_for_status()
    except (httpx.HTTPError, httpx.RequestError, httpx.HTTPStatusError) as e:
      print(f"❌ Cold start failed: {e}")
      sys.exit(1)
    cold_latency = time.perf_counter() - start_time
    print(f"❄️ Cold Start Latency: {cold_latency:.2f}s")
    if cold_latency > 10.0:
      print("❌ Cold start latency exceeded 10 seconds SLA.")
      sys.exit(1)
    latencies = []
    for _ in range(4):
      start_time = time.perf_counter()
      resp = await client.post(
        f"{STAGING_URL}/api/v1/test-router", json=payload, headers=headers
      )
      resp.raise_for_status()
      latencies.append(time.perf_counter() - start_time)
      await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
    avg_warm_latency = sum(latencies) / len(latencies)
    print(f"🔥 Average Warm Latency: {avg_warm_latency:.2f}s")
    if avg_warm_latency > 3.0:
      print("❌ Warm latency exceeded 3 seconds SLA.")
      sys.exit(1)
  print("✅ Warm-up & Cold Start SLAs met.")
if __name__ == "__main__":
  asyncio.run(run_warmup())
```

### `scripts/lancedb_delete_pdf_chunks_by_document_id.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import asyncio
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path
_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
_src = _root / "src"
if str(_src) not in sys.path:
  sys.path.insert(0, str(_src))
import lancedb  # noqa: E402  # type: ignore[import-untyped]
from app.core.config import settings  # noqa: E402
from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.lancedb_adapter import (  # noqa: E402
  PDF_ANKI_TABLE,
)
_UUID_RE = re.compile(
  r"^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$",
  re.IGNORECASE,
)
def _is_table_missing_error(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
  msg = str(exc).lower()
  return "not found" in msg or "does not exist" in msg
async def main() -> None:
  if len(sys.argv) != 2:
    print(
      "Usage: python scripts/lancedb_delete_pdf_chunks_by_document_id.py "
      "<document_uuid>",
      file=sys.stderr,
    )
    sys.exit(1)
  doc_id = sys.argv[1].strip()
  if not _UUID_RE.match(doc_id):
    print(
      "document_id must be a UUID (pdf_anki_documents.id).",
      file=sys.stderr,
    )
    sys.exit(1)
  uri = (settings.LANCEDB_URI or "").strip()
  if not uri:
    print("LANCEDB_URI is not set.", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)
  storage_opts = settings.lancedb_storage_options() or None
  conn = await lancedb.connect_async(uri, storage_options=storage_opts)
  try:
    try:
      tbl = await conn.open_table(PDF_ANKI_TABLE)
    except BaseException as exc:
      if _is_table_missing_error(exc):
        print(f"No table {PDF_ANKI_TABLE!r}; nothing to delete.")
        return
      raise
    await tbl.delete(where=f"document_id = '{doc_id}'")
    print(f"Deleted chunks for document_id={doc_id!r}.")
  finally:
    conn.close()
if __name__ == "__main__":
  asyncio.run(main())
```

### `scripts/compile_adaptive_router_dspy.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path
from app.core.dspy_compilation import (
  AdaptiveRouterCompileExample,
  compile_adaptive_router_policy,
  evaluate_adaptive_router_policy,
  load_jsonl_models,
)
def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
  parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    description="Compile the AdaptiveRouter DSPy pilot artifact from JSONL data."
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--input",
    type=Path,
    default=Path("config/dspy/examples/adaptive_router_trainset.jsonl"),
    help="JSONL training set path",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--eval-input",
    type=Path,
    default=None,
    help="Optional JSONL eval set path. Defaults to --input.",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--output",
    type=Path,
    default=Path("config/dspy/adaptive_router.json"),
    help="Output artifact path",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--max-keywords",
    type=int,
    default=12,
    help="Maximum complex keywords to keep in the artifact",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--max-queries",
    type=int,
    default=3,
    help="Maximum compiled query templates",
  )
  return parser.parse_args()
def main() -> int:
  args = _parse_args()
  trainset = load_jsonl_models(args.input, AdaptiveRouterCompileExample)
  eval_path = args.eval_input or args.input
  evalset = load_jsonl_models(eval_path, AdaptiveRouterCompileExample)
  artifact = compile_adaptive_router_policy(
    trainset,
    max_keywords=max(1, args.max_keywords),
    max_queries=max(1, args.max_queries),
  )
  metrics = evaluate_adaptive_router_policy(evalset, artifact)
  payload = artifact.model_dump(exclude_none=True)
  payload["evaluation"] = metrics.model_dump()
  args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  args.output.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
  print("AdaptiveRouter artifact compiled")
  print(f"  train examples: {len(trainset)}")
  print(f"  eval examples: {len(evalset)}")
  print(f"  complexity accuracy: {metrics.complexity_accuracy:.4f}")
  print(f"  query hint coverage: {metrics.query_hint_coverage:.4f}")
  print(f"  output: {args.output}")
  return 0
if __name__ == "__main__":
  raise SystemExit(main())
```

### `scripts/verify_observability.py`
```py
import asyncio
import os
import sys
import time
import uuid
import httpx
API_URL = os.getenv("API_URL", "http://localhost:8000")
LOKI_URL = os.getenv("LOKI_URL", "http://localhost:3100")
EXPECTED_LOG_JOB = "app"
async def verify_trace_flow():
  print(f"🔍 Starting Observability Smoke Test against {API_URL}...")
  unique_prompt = f"Observability Check {uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
  async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    try:
      start_time = time.time_ns()
      response = await client.post(
        f"{API_URL}/api/v1/test-router",
        json={"prompt": unique_prompt},
        timeout=10.0,
      )
      response.raise_for_status()
      print("✅ API Request Successful (200 OK)")
    except Exception as e:
      print(f"❌ API Request Failed: {e}")
      sys.exit(1)
    print("⏳ Waiting 5s for log ingestion...")
    await asyncio.sleep(5)
    loki_query = f'{{job="{EXPECTED_LOG_JOB}"}} |= "{unique_prompt}"'
    try:
      loki_resp = await client.get(
        f"{LOKI_URL}/loki/api/v1/query_range",
        params={"query": loki_query, "limit": 10, "start": start_time},
      )
      if loki_resp.status_code != 200:
        print(f"⚠️ Loki API Error: {loki_resp.status_code} - {loki_resp.text}")
        return
      data = loki_resp.json()
      results = data.get("data", {}).get("result", [])
      if not results:
        print("❌ FAILURE: Log entry not found in Loki.")
        print(f"   Query used: {loki_query}")
        sys.exit(1)
      log_entry_json = results[0]["values"][0][1]
      print(f"✅ Log found in Loki: {log_entry_json[:100]}...")
      if "trace_id" in log_entry_json:
        print("✅ Trace ID correlation verified.")
      else:
        print("❌ FAILURE: Trace ID missing in logs.")
        sys.exit(1)
    except Exception as e:
      print(f"⚠️ Could not verify Loki directly (Network/Auth issue?): {e}")
if __name__ == "__main__":
  asyncio.run(verify_trace_flow())
```

### `scripts/clear_pdf_anki_redis_user.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import asyncio
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
import redis.asyncio as redis
from sqlalchemy import select
_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
_src = _root / "src"
if str(_src) not in sys.path:
  sys.path.insert(0, str(_src))
from app.infrastructure.database import sessionmanager  # noqa: E402
from app.modules.pdf_anki.domain.entities import PdfDeck  # noqa: E402
async def _scan_keys(client: redis.Redis, pattern: str) -> list[str]:
  cursor = 0
  matches: list[str] = []
  while True:
    cursor, keys = await client.scan(cursor=cursor, match=pattern, count=200)
    matches.extend(str(key) for key in keys)
    if cursor == 0:
      return matches
async def _get_user_file_hashes(user_id: str) -> list[str]:
  sessionmanager.initialize()
  async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
    stmt = (
      select(PdfDeck.file_hash)
      .where(PdfDeck.user_id == user_id)
      .distinct()
      .order_by(PdfDeck.file_hash.asc())
    )
    rows = await session.execute(stmt)
    return [str(file_hash) for file_hash in rows.scalars().all() if file_hash]
async def main() -> None:
  if len(sys.argv) != 2:
    print(
      "Usage: python scripts/clear_pdf_anki_redis_user.py <user_id>",
      file=sys.stderr,
    )
    sys.exit(1)
  user_id = sys.argv[1].strip()
  if not user_id:
    print("User id cannot be empty.", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)
  try:
    file_hashes = await _get_user_file_hashes(user_id)
  finally:
    await sessionmanager.close()
  if not file_hashes:
    print(f"No pdf_anki decks found for user {user_id}.")
    return
  print(f"Found {len(file_hashes)} file_hash value(s) for user {user_id}.")
  redis_url = os.environ.get("REDIS_URL", "redis://localhost:6379/0")
  client = redis.from_url(redis_url, decode_responses=True)
  try:
    raw_keys = [f"pdf:raw:{file_hash}" for file_hash in file_hashes]
    deck_keys: list[str] = []
    for file_hash in file_hashes:
      pattern = f"pdf:deck:{file_hash}:*"
      keys = await _scan_keys(client, pattern)
      if keys:
        print(f"{pattern} -> {len(keys)} key(s)")
        deck_keys.extend(keys)
    unique_keys = sorted(set(raw_keys + deck_keys))
    if not unique_keys:
      print("No matching pdf_anki Redis keys found.")
      return
    deleted = await client.delete(*unique_keys)
    print(f"Deleted {deleted} pdf_anki Redis key(s).")
    for key in unique_keys:
      print(f" - {key}")
  finally:
    await client.aclose()
if __name__ == "__main__":
  asyncio.run(main())
```

### `scripts/summarize_mutants.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import argparse
import ast
import difflib
import json
import re
import subprocess
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path
_TIMEOUT_EXIT_CODES = (24, -24, 152)
_NO_TESTS_EXIT_CODES = (5, 33)
_NOT_CHECKED_EXIT_CODES = (None,)
_GATE_FAIL_STATUSES: tuple[str, ...] = (
  "survived",
  "suspicious",
  "timeout",
  "no tests",
  "not checked",
  "segfault",
  "check was interrupted by user",
)
UV_RUN = ["uv", "run", "--no-sync"]
_DEFAULT_JOBS = 4
_MAX_JOBS_WSL = 4
def _is_wsl() -> bool:
  try:
    with open("/proc/version", encoding="utf-8") as f:
      return "microsoft" in f.read().lower() or "wsl" in f.read().lower()
  except OSError:
    return False
def strip_ansi(text: str) -> str:
  ansi_escape = re.compile(r"(?:\x1B[@-_]|[\x80-\x9F])[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]")
  return ansi_escape.sub("", text)
def get_survivors(project_root: Path | None = None) -> list[str]:
  try:
    result = subprocess.run(
      [*UV_RUN, "mutmut", "results"],
      capture_output=True,
      text=True,
      check=False,
      timeout=60,
      cwd=str(project_root) if project_root else None,
    )
  except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
    print("Error: mutmut results command timed out.", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)
  if result.returncode != 0 and not result.stdout.strip():
    print(
      f"Error: mutmut results failed: {result.stderr or result.stdout}",
      file=sys.stderr,
    )
    sys.exit(1)
  lines = (result.stdout or "").splitlines()
  survivors: list[str] = []
  for line in lines:
    clean_line = strip_ansi(line).strip()
    if ": survived" in clean_line.lower():
      full_id = clean_line.split(":")[0].strip()
      if full_id and "__mutmut_" in full_id:
        survivors.append(full_id)
  return survivors
def get_timeouts_from_cache(project_root: Path) -> list[tuple[str, int]]:
  timeouts: list[tuple[str, int]] = []
  mutants_dir = project_root / "mutants"
  if not mutants_dir.is_dir():
    return timeouts
  for meta_path in mutants_dir.rglob("*.meta"):
    try:
      data = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
      continue
    exit_codes = data.get("exit_code_by_key") or {}
    for mut_id, code in exit_codes.items():
      if code in _TIMEOUT_EXIT_CODES:
        timeouts.append((mut_id, code))
  return timeouts
def get_not_checked_from_cache(project_root: Path) -> list[str]:
  not_checked: list[str] = []
  mutants_dir = project_root / "mutants"
  if not mutants_dir.is_dir():
    return not_checked
  for meta_path in mutants_dir.rglob("*.meta"):
    try:
      data = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
      continue
    exit_codes = data.get("exit_code_by_key") or {}
    for mut_id, code in exit_codes.items():
      if code in _NOT_CHECKED_EXIT_CODES:
        not_checked.append(mut_id)
  return not_checked
def get_no_tests_from_cache(
  project_root: Path,
) -> tuple[int, list[tuple[str, int]]]:
  no_tests_by_path: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
  mutants_dir = project_root / "mutants"
  if not mutants_dir.is_dir():
    return (0, [])
  for meta_path in mutants_dir.rglob("*.meta"):
    try:
      data = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
      continue
    exit_codes = data.get("exit_code_by_key") or {}
    count = sum(1 for c in exit_codes.values() if c in _NO_TESTS_EXIT_CODES)
    if count == 0:
      continue
    try:
      rel = meta_path.relative_to(mutants_dir)
      path_str = str(rel).replace("\\", "/")
      if path_str.endswith(".meta"):
        path_str = path_str[:-5]
      if path_str.startswith("src/") or "/src/" in path_str:
        if "/src/" in path_str:
          path_str = "src/" + path_str.split("/src/", 1)[1]
      else:
        path_str = "src/" + path_str
      no_tests_by_path[path_str] += count
    except ValueError:
      no_tests_by_path[str(meta_path)] += count
  total = sum(no_tests_by_path.values())
  by_file = sorted(no_tests_by_path.items(), key=lambda x: (-x[1], x[0]))
  return (total, by_file)
def tally_mutation_statuses(project_root: Path) -> dict[str, int]:
  from mutmut.__main__ import status_by_exit_code
  tallies: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
  mutants_dir = project_root / "mutants"
  if not mutants_dir.is_dir():
    return dict(tallies)
  for meta_path in mutants_dir.rglob("*.meta"):
    try:
      data = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
      continue
    for code in (data.get("exit_code_by_key") or {}).values():
      label = status_by_exit_code[code]
      tallies[label] += 1
  return dict(tallies)
def _normalize_path(raw: str) -> str:
  raw = raw.strip()
  for prefix in ("src/", "tests/", "mutants/src/", "mutants/tests/"):
    if prefix in raw:
      idx = raw.find(prefix)
      out = raw[idx:].split("\t")[0].strip()
      if out.startswith("mutants/"):
        out = out.replace("mutants/", "", 1)
      return out
  if "/" in raw:
    return raw.split("/")[-1] if raw else raw
  return raw
def _path_from_mutant_id(mut_id: str) -> str:
  try:
    first = mut_id.split("||")[0].strip()
    if not first:
      return "Unknown File"
    if ".x" in first:
      first = first.rsplit(".x", 1)[0]
    path = "src/" + first.replace(".", "/") + ".py"
    return path
  except Exception:
    return "Unknown File"
def parse_diff(diff_text: str, mut_id: str = "") -> tuple[str | None, int | None, str]:
  file_path: str | None = None
  line_num: int | None = None
  changes: list[str] = []
  for line in diff_text.splitlines():
    if line.startswith("--- "):
      match = re.match(r"^--- (?:[ab]/)?(.+)", line.strip())
      if match:
        file_path = _normalize_path(match.group(1))
    elif line.startswith("+++ "):
      if not file_path:
        match = re.match(r"^\+\+\+ (?:[ab]/)?(.+)", line.strip())
        if match:
          file_path = _normalize_path(match.group(1))
    elif line.startswith("@@ "):
      match = re.match(r"@@ -\d+(?:,\d+)? \+(\d+)(?:,\d+)?", line)
      if match:
        line_num = int(match.group(1))
    elif (line.startswith("-") and not line.startswith("---")) or (
      line.startswith("+") and not line.startswith("+++")
    ):
      changes.append(line[:80].strip())
  one_line = " | ".join(changes[:2]) if changes else "(parse error)"
  if file_path is None and mut_id:
    file_path = _path_from_mutant_id(mut_id)
    if file_path == "Unknown File":
      file_path = None
  return (file_path, line_num, one_line)
def _normalize_block_signature(block: str) -> str:
  normalized = re.sub(
    r"^(\s*(?:async\s+def|def)\s+)[^\(]+",
    r"\1<NAME>",
    block,
    count=1,
    flags=re.MULTILINE,
  )
  normalized = re.sub(r"\s+# pragma: no mutate\b", "", normalized)
  normalized = "\n".join(line.rstrip() for line in normalized.splitlines())
  return normalized.strip() + "\n"
def _extract_def_blocks(text: str) -> dict[str, str]:
  tree = ast.parse(text)
  blocks: dict[str, str] = {}
  for node in ast.walk(tree):
    if not isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):
      continue
    segment = ast.get_source_segment(text, node)
    if not segment:
      continue
    blocks[node.name] = segment
  return blocks
def _extract_source_blocks(source_path: Path) -> dict[str, tuple[int, str]]:
  text = source_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
  tree = ast.parse(text)
  blocks: dict[str, tuple[int, str]] = {}
  for node in ast.walk(tree):
    if not isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):
      continue
    segment = ast.get_source_segment(text, node)
    if not segment:
      continue
    normalized = _normalize_block_signature(segment)
    blocks.setdefault(normalized, (node.lineno, segment))
  return blocks
def _summarize_mutant_block(
  source_line: int,
  source_block: str,
  mutant_block: str,
) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
  src_lines = _normalize_block_signature(source_block).splitlines()
  mut_lines = _normalize_block_signature(mutant_block).splitlines()
  matcher = difflib.SequenceMatcher(a=src_lines, b=mut_lines)
  for tag, i1, i2, j1, j2 in matcher.get_opcodes():
    if tag == "equal":
      continue
    src_line_text = next(
      (line.strip() for line in src_lines[i1:i2] if line.strip()),
      "",
    )
    mut_line_text = next(
      (line.strip() for line in mut_lines[j1:j2] if line.strip()),
      "",
    )
    summary_parts: list[str] = []
    if src_line_text:
      summary_parts.append(f"- {src_line_text}")
    if mut_line_text:
      summary_parts.append(f"+ {mut_line_text}")
    summary = " | ".join(summary_parts) if summary_parts else "(parse error)"
    return (source_line + i1, summary)
  return (None, "(no textual diff found)")
class _MutantCacheIndex:
  def __init__(self) -> None:
    self._by_source_path: dict[str, dict[str, tuple[int, str, str]]] = {}
  def _build_for_path(
    self,
    project_root: Path,
    source_path_str: str,
  ) -> dict[str, tuple[int, str, str]]:
    source_path = project_root / source_path_str
    mutant_cache_path = project_root / "mutants" / source_path_str
    if not source_path.is_file() or not mutant_cache_path.is_file():
      return {}
    try:
      source_blocks = _extract_source_blocks(source_path)
      mutant_blocks = _extract_def_blocks(
        mutant_cache_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
      )
    except (OSError, SyntaxError, UnicodeDecodeError):
      return {}
    orig_by_encoded_name: dict[str, tuple[int, str]] = {}
    for name, block in mutant_blocks.items():
      if not name.endswith("__mutmut_orig"):
        continue
      normalized = _normalize_block_signature(block)
      source_info = source_blocks.get(normalized)
      if source_info is None:
        continue
      encoded_name = name[: -len("__mutmut_orig")]
      orig_by_encoded_name[encoded_name] = source_info
    index: dict[str, tuple[int, str, str]] = {}
    for name, block in mutant_blocks.items():
      mutant_match = re.match(r"^(.*)__mutmut_(\d+)$", name)
      if not mutant_match:
        continue
      encoded_name = mutant_match.group(1)
      source_info = orig_by_encoded_name.get(encoded_name)
      if source_info is None:
        continue
      source_line, source_block = source_info
      mutant_id = (
        source_path_str.removeprefix("src/")
        .removesuffix(".py")
        .replace("/", ".")
        + f".{name}"
      )
      index[mutant_id] = (source_line, source_block, block)
    return index
  def get(
    self,
    project_root: Path,
    mut_id: str,
  ) -> tuple[str | None, int | None, str] | None:
    source_path_str = _path_from_mutant_id(mut_id)
    if source_path_str == "Unknown File":
      return None
    if source_path_str not in self._by_source_path:
      self._by_source_path[source_path_str] = self._build_for_path(
        project_root, source_path_str
      )
    entry = self._by_source_path[source_path_str].get(mut_id)
    if entry is None:
      return None
    source_line, source_block, mutant_block = entry
    line, summary = _summarize_mutant_block(source_line, source_block, mutant_block)
    return (source_path_str, line, summary)
def fetch_one(mut_id: str, project_root: Path | None = None) -> tuple[str, str]:
  res = subprocess.run(
    [*UV_RUN, "mutmut", "show", mut_id],
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
    check=False,
    timeout=60,
    cwd=str(project_root) if project_root else None,
  )
  return (mut_id, strip_ansi(res.stdout or "").strip())
def main() -> None:
  import os as _os
  project_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
  if _os.getcwd() != str(project_root):
    _os.chdir(project_root)
  parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    description="Summarize surviving mutants (compact and fast)."
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "--full",
    action="store_true",
    help="Write full diff per mutant (slow, legacy format).",
  )
  parser.add_argument(
    "-j",
    "--jobs",
    type=int,
    default=_DEFAULT_JOBS,
    metavar="N",
    help=(
      "Parallel jobs for mutmut show (default "
      f"{_DEFAULT_JOBS}). In WSL a cap of "
      f"{_MAX_JOBS_WSL} is applied to avoid "
      "overloading the Cursor remote connection."
    ),
  )
  args = parser.parse_args()
  if _is_wsl() and args.jobs > _MAX_JOBS_WSL:
    print(
      (
        "⚠️ WSL detected: capping jobs from "
        f"{args.jobs} to {_MAX_JOBS_WSL} to avoid "
        "connection timeouts."
      ),
      flush=True,
    )
    args.jobs = _MAX_JOBS_WSL
  print("🔍 Analyzing mutmut cache...", flush=True)
  survivors = get_survivors(project_root)
  timeouts = get_timeouts_from_cache(project_root)
  not_checked = get_not_checked_from_cache(project_root)
  no_tests_total, no_tests_by_file = get_no_tests_from_cache(project_root)
  tallies = tally_mutation_statuses(project_root)
  meta_files = (
    list((project_root / "mutants").rglob("*.meta"))
    if (project_root / "mutants").is_dir()
    else []
  )
  report_path = project_root / "mutation_summary.md"
  if not survivors:
    body = "# 🧬 Surviving Mutants Summary\n\n**Total:** `0` mutants survived.\n\n"
    if not meta_files:
      body += (
        "❌ No `mutants/**/*.meta` found.\n\n"
        "This means `mutmut run` did not finish the generation/coverage "
        "phase that writes mutation metadata. Re-run "
        "`uv run --no-sync mutmut run` "
        "and check its exit code before running this summary. In memory "
        "constrained environments, try `MUTMUT_MAX_CHILDREN=1 make "
        "mutation`.\n"
      )
      report_path.write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
      print(
        "❌ No mutation metadata on disk. "
        "mutmut likely stopped during generation/coverage.",
        flush=True,
      )
      sys.exit(1)
    bad_counts = {s: tallies.get(s, 0) for s in _GATE_FAIL_STATUSES}
    gate_ok = all(count == 0 for count in bad_counts.values())
    if not gate_ok:
      bad_parts = [
        f"{k}={bad_counts[k]}" for k in _GATE_FAIL_STATUSES if bad_counts[k]
      ]
      print(f"❌ Quality gate failed: {', '.join(bad_parts)}", flush=True)
    if gate_ok:
      body += (
        "✅ Quality gate passed: 0 survivors, 0 timeouts, 0 suspicious, "
        "0 no_tests, 0 not_checked, 0 segfault.\n"
      )
    else:
      body += "✅ Listed survivors: 0 (see tallies below for other outcomes).\n\n"
      body += "### Outcome tallies\n\n| Status | Count |\n|--------|-------|\n"
      for label in sorted(tallies.keys()):
        body += f"| `{label}` | {tallies[label]} |\n"
      body += "\n"
    if timeouts:
      body += (
        "\n### ⏰ Timeout(s) (worker exit -24 / SIGXCPU)\n\n"
        "The following mutant(s) timed out (tests exceeded CPU limit). "
        "To get **0 timeouts**, keep pytest's mutation timeout below "
        "mutmut's SIGXCPU guard, speed up the tests that cover it, or "
        "add `# pragma: no mutate` only to non-logic orchestration "
        "lines that cannot be tested meaningfully.\n\n"
        "| Mutant ID | Exit code |\n|-----------|------------|\n"
      )
      for mut_id, code in timeouts:
        body += f"| `{mut_id}` | {code} |\n"
      body += (
        "\n**Fix:** Run a clean mutation pass after harness changes. "
        "For persistent cases, inspect "
        "`uv run --no-sync mutmut show <Mutant ID>` "
        "and isolate pure logic or mark only non-logic I/O lines.\n"
      )
      print(
        f"⏰ {len(timeouts)} timeout(s). See {report_path} for IDs.",
        flush=True,
      )
    if no_tests_total > 0:
      body += (
        "\n### 🫥 No-tests (uncovered lines)\n\n"
        f"**Total:** `{no_tests_total}` mutant(s) in code not covered by "
        "any test. Quality gate requires **0 no_tests**.\n\n"
        "| File | No-tests count |\n|------|----------------|\n"
      )
      for path, count in no_tests_by_file:
        body += f"| `{path}` | {count} |\n"
      body += (
        "\n**Fix:** Run with `mutate_only_covered_lines = true`, then "
        "add tests for any intentionally mutable line that remains "
        "uncovered.\n"
      )
      print(
        f"🫥 {no_tests_total} no_tests (uncovered). See {report_path}.",
        flush=True,
      )
    if not_checked:
      body += (
        "\n### ❔ Not checked\n\n"
        f"**Total:** `{len(not_checked)}` mutant(s) were generated but "
        "not executed. This usually means the run was interrupted or "
        "aborted after earlier infrastructure failures.\n\n"
        "| Mutant ID |\n|-----------|\n"
      )
      for mut_id in not_checked:
        body += f"| `{mut_id}` |\n"
      print(
        f"❔ {len(not_checked)} not_checked mutant(s). See {report_path}.",
        flush=True,
      )
    sus = tallies.get("suspicious", 0)
    if sus and not gate_ok:
      print(
        f"🤔 {sus} suspicious mutant(s). See outcome tallies in {report_path}.",
        flush=True,
      )
    if gate_ok:
      print(
        "✅ Quality Gate Passed: 0 survivors, 0 timeouts, 0 no_tests, "
        "0 suspicious, 0 not_checked.",
        flush=True,
      )
    report_path.write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
    sys.exit(0 if gate_ok else 1)
  n = len(survivors)
  if timeouts:
    print(
      f"⏰ {len(timeouts)} timeout(s) (see report). "
      "Fix the harness/tests or mark only non-logic I/O lines.",
      flush=True,
    )
  print(f"⚠️ Found {n} surviving mutants. Building summary from cache...")
  if args.full:
    with report_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
      f.write("# Surviving Mutants Report (Full Diffs)\n\n")
      for i, mut_id in enumerate(survivors):
        if (i + 1) % 50 == 0:
          print(f"  {i + 1}/{n}...", flush=True)
        _, diff_text = fetch_one(mut_id, project_root)
        diff_text = re.sub(r"--- .*/src/", "--- src/", diff_text)
        diff_text = re.sub(r"\+\+\+ .*/src/", "+++ src/", diff_text)
        f.write(f"## Mutant: `{mut_id}`\n```python\n{diff_text}\n```\n\n")
    print(f"📄 Full report written to {report_path}")
    sys.exit(1)
  by_file: dict[str, list[tuple[str, int | None, str]]] = defaultdict(list)
  for done, mut_id in enumerate(survivors, start=1):
    if done % 50 == 0:
      print(f"  Processed {done}/{n}...", flush=True)
    path = _path_from_mutant_id(mut_id)
    if path == "Unknown File":
      path = "Unknown File"
    by_file[path].append(
      (
        mut_id,
        None,
        "compact summary: use `uv run --no-sync mutmut show <id>` "
        "for exact diff",
      )
    )
  for path in by_file:
    by_file[path].sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] or 0, x[0]))
  with report_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("# 🧬 Surviving Mutants Summary\n\n")
    f.write(f"**Total:** `{n}` mutants survived across `{len(by_file)}` files.\n\n")
    for path in sorted(by_file.keys()):
      entries = by_file[path]
      f.write(f"### 📄 `{path}` ({len(entries)} survivors)\n\n")
      f.write("| Mutant ID | Line | Change Summary |\n")
      f.write("|-----------|------|----------------|\n")
      for mut_id, line, summary in entries:
        line_s = str(line) if line is not None else "?"
        summary_esc = summary.replace("|", "\\|")[:120]
        f.write(f"| `{mut_id}` | {line_s} | `{summary_esc}` |\n")
      f.write("\n")
    if timeouts:
      f.write(
        "### ⏰ Timeout(s) (worker exit -24 / SIGXCPU)\n\n"
        "These mutants timed out before pytest could classify them as "
        "killed or survived. Keep pytest's mutation timeout below "
        "mutmut's SIGXCPU guard, speed up the selected tests, or mark "
        "only non-logic I/O lines with `# pragma: no mutate`.\n\n"
        "| Mutant ID | Exit code |\n|-----------|------------|\n"
      )
      for mut_id, code in timeouts:
        f.write(f"| `{mut_id}` | {code} |\n")
      f.write("\n")
    if no_tests_total > 0:
      f.write(
        "### 🫥 No-tests (uncovered lines)\n\n"
        f"**Total:** `{no_tests_total}` mutant(s) were generated for "
        "code that no selected test executed. Enable covered-line "
        "mutation or add tests before reviewing survivors.\n\n"
        "| File | No-tests count |\n|------|----------------|\n"
      )
      for path, count in no_tests_by_file:
        f.write(f"| `{path}` | {count} |\n")
      f.write("\n")
    if not_checked:
      f.write(
        "### ❔ Not checked\n\n"
        f"**Total:** `{len(not_checked)}` mutant(s) were generated but "
        "not executed. Re-run mutation after clearing timeouts/no-tests "
        "to produce a complete survivor list.\n\n"
        "| Mutant ID |\n|-----------|\n"
      )
      for mut_id in not_checked:
        f.write(f"| `{mut_id}` |\n")
      f.write("\n")
  print(f"\n📄 Compact report written to {report_path}")
  bad_parts = [f"{n} survived"]
  if timeouts:
    bad_parts.append(f"{len(timeouts)} timeout")
  if no_tests_total > 0:
    bad_parts.append(f"{no_tests_total} no_tests")
  if not_checked:
    bad_parts.append(f"{len(not_checked)} not_checked")
  print(f"❌ Quality Gate Failed: {', '.join(bad_parts)}. Review the summary.")
  sys.exit(1)
if __name__ == "__main__":
  main()
```

### `scripts/fix_timeouts.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import json
import re
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
def get_timeouts_from_cache(project_root: Path) -> list[tuple[str, int]]:
  timeout_exit_codes = (24, -24, 152)
  timeouts: list[tuple[str, int]] = []
  mutants_dir = project_root / "mutants"
  if not mutants_dir.is_dir():
    return timeouts
  for meta_path in mutants_dir.rglob("*.meta"):
    try:
      data = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
      continue
    exit_codes = data.get("exit_code_by_key") or {}
    for mut_id, code in exit_codes.items():
      if code in timeout_exit_codes:
        timeouts.append((mut_id, code))
  return timeouts
def strip_ansi(text: str) -> str:
  ansi_escape = re.compile(r"(?:\x1B[@-_]|[\x80-\x9F])[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]")
  return ansi_escape.sub("", text)
def get_mutant_diff(mut_id: str, project_root: Path) -> str:
  res = subprocess.run(
    ["uv", "run", "mutmut", "show", mut_id],
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
    check=False,
    timeout=60,
    cwd=str(project_root),
  )
  return strip_ansi(res.stdout or "").strip()
def extract_line_from_diff(diff_text: str) -> int | None:
  for line in diff_text.splitlines():
    if line.startswith("@@ "):
      match = re.match(r"@@ -\d+(?:,\d+)? \+(\d+)(?:,\d+)?", line)
      if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
  return None
def main():
  project_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
  timeouts = get_timeouts_from_cache(project_root)
  if not timeouts:
    print("✅ No timeouts found!")
    return
  print(f"⏰ Found {len(timeouts)} timeout(s). Analyzing...")
  by_file: dict[str, list[tuple[str, int, int | None]]] = {}
  for i, (mut_id, code) in enumerate(timeouts, 1):
    if i % 100 == 0:
      print(f"  Analyzed {i}/{len(timeouts)}...")
    diff = get_mutant_diff(mut_id, project_root)
    line_num = extract_line_from_diff(diff)
    parts = mut_id.split("||")
    if parts:
      module_path = parts[0].strip()
      file_path = "src/" + module_path.replace(".", "/") + ".py"
      if file_path not in by_file:
        by_file[file_path] = []
      by_file[file_path].append((mut_id, code, line_num))
  print("\nTimeouts by file:")
  for file_path in sorted(by_file.keys()):
    entries = by_file[file_path]
    print(f"  {file_path}: {len(entries)} timeout(s)")
    for mut_id, _code, line_num in sorted(entries, key=lambda x: x[2] or 0):
      print(f"    - Line {line_num}: {mut_id[:60]}...")
if __name__ == "__main__":
  main()
```

### `scripts/run_mutation.py`
```py
import argparse
import os
import re
import shlex
import shutil
import signal
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
PYPROJECT_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / "pyproject.toml"
BACKUP_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / "pyproject.toml.bak"
UV_RUN = ["uv", "run", "--no-sync"]
DO_NOT_MUTATE_CORE_ONLY = [
  "src/app/__init__.py",
  "src/app/adapters/*",
  "src/app/domain/*",
  "src/app/infrastructure/*",
  "src/app/middleware/*",
  "src/app/modules/*",
  "src/app/security/*",
  "src/app/shared/*",
  "src/app/config.py",
  "src/app/main.py",
  "src/app/dependencies.py",
  "src/app/logging_conf.py",
  "src/app/worker.py",
  "src/app/core/__init__.py",
  "src/app/core/api_bearer_auth.py",
  "src/app/core/api_cookie_auth.py",
  "src/app/core/auth_modules.py",
  "src/app/core/config.py",
  "src/app/core/context.py",
  "src/app/core/database.py",
  "src/app/core/dlq_recovery.py",
  "src/app/core/dynamic_config.py",
  "src/app/core/exceptions.py",
  "src/app/core/finops.py",
  "src/app/core/finops_logger.py",
  "src/app/core/health.py",
  "src/app/core/jobs.py",
  "src/app/core/redis.py",
  "src/app/core/reranker.py",
  "src/app/core/retrieval.py",
  "src/app/core/router.py",
  "src/app/core/runtime_tunable_config.py",
  "src/app/core/semantic_cache.py",
  "src/app/core/stream_manager.py",
  "src/app/core/support_tickets.py",
  "src/app/core/telemetry.py",
  "src/app/core/token_manager.py",
  "src/app/core/tracing.py",
  "src/app/core/vector_store.py",
  "src/app/core/concurrency.py",
]
def replace_assignment(content: str, key: str, value: str) -> str:
  pattern = re.compile(rf"(?m)^{re.escape(key)}\s*=\s*\[[^\n]*\]\s*$")
  updated, count = pattern.subn(f"{key} = {value}", content, count=1)
  if count != 1:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Could not replace {key} in pyproject.toml.")
  return updated
def replace_array(content: str, key: str, values: list[str]) -> str:
  array_text = f"{key} = [\n"
  array_text += "".join(f'    "{value}",\n' for value in values)
  array_text += "]\n"
  pattern = re.compile(rf"(?ms)^{re.escape(key)}\s*=\s*\[.*?^\]\n?")
  updated, count = pattern.subn(array_text, content, count=1)
  if count != 1:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Could not replace {key} in pyproject.toml.")
  return updated
def restore_backup(signum: int | None = None, frame: object | None = None) -> None:
  if BACKUP_PATH.exists():
    shutil.copy(BACKUP_PATH, PYPROJECT_PATH)
    BACKUP_PATH.unlink()
    print("\n🧹 Restored pyproject.toml from backup.")
  if signum is not None:
    print(f"⚠️ Process interrupted by signal {signum}. Exiting.")
    sys.exit(1)
def main() -> None:
  signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, restore_backup)
  signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, restore_backup)
  parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run mutmut with dynamic config.")
  parser.add_argument("target_path", help="Path to mutate (e.g., src/app/core)")
  args = parser.parse_args()
  if not PYPROJECT_PATH.exists():
    print(f"❌ Error: {PYPROJECT_PATH} not found.")
    sys.exit(1)
  restore_backup()
  shutil.copy(PYPROJECT_PATH, BACKUP_PATH)
  try:
    content = PYPROJECT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    if args.target_path == "src/app/core":
      content = replace_assignment(content, "paths_to_mutate", '["src/app"]')
      content = replace_array(content, "do_not_mutate", DO_NOT_MUTATE_CORE_ONLY)
      print(
        "🔧 Temporarily updating pyproject.toml to mutate: src/app (core only)"
      )
    else:
      content = replace_assignment(
        content,
        "paths_to_mutate",
        f'["{args.target_path}"]',
      )
      print(
        f"🔧 Temporarily updating pyproject.toml to mutate: {args.target_path}"
      )
    PYPROJECT_PATH.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    shutil.rmtree(PROJECT_ROOT / ".mutmut-cache", ignore_errors=True)
    shutil.rmtree(PROJECT_ROOT / "mutants", ignore_errors=True)
    needs_app_mirror = (
      args.target_path != "src/app/core"
      and args.target_path != "src/app"
      and args.target_path.startswith("src/app/")
    )
    if needs_app_mirror:
      mutants_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / "mutants"
      shutil.rmtree(mutants_dir, ignore_errors=True)
      mutants_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
      shutil.copytree(
        PROJECT_ROOT / "src" / "app",
        mutants_dir / "src" / "app",
        dirs_exist_ok=True,
      )
      print("📁 Seeded mutants/src/app from src/app for scoped mutation imports.")
    print("🚀 Running mutmut...")
    max_children = os.environ.get("MUTMUT_MAX_CHILDREN", "2")
    if sys.platform == "linux":
      cmd: list[str] = [
        "bash",
        "-c",
        "ulimit -t unlimited 2>/dev/null; "
        "uv run --no-sync mutmut run --max-children "
        f"{shlex.quote(max_children)}",
      ]
    else:
      cmd = [*UV_RUN, "mutmut", "run", "--max-children", max_children]
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=PROJECT_ROOT, env=os.environ.copy())
    if result.returncode != 0:
      print("⚠️ mutmut finished with non-zero exit code.")
  finally:
    restore_backup()
if __name__ == "__main__":
  main()
```

### `scripts/decrypt.py`
```py
import base64
import hashlib
from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
encrypted_text = "gAAAAABp7K5rHvCaDPVdNUDJulvgI8s2X7jQluKcT45TAJqHFDF5y2yunZlC8CP3B8"
"yhtmMYBt_0-Jwmyf6cZodr4NhrUM25-Q=="
secret_key = "dM4ftweBvGcyk_dZM0AbqmRfV99D9Z5tkgnRqvTZxnA"  # noqa: S105
derived_key_salt = b"pdf-anki-app-layer-encryption"
material = secret_key.encode("utf-8") + derived_key_salt
digest = hashlib.sha256(material).digest()
fernet_key = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(digest)
print(f"Clave derivada: {fernet_key}")
print(f"Texto cifrado: {encrypted_text}\n")
try:
  cipher = Fernet(fernet_key)
  decrypted = cipher.decrypt(encrypted_text.encode())
  print("✓ Desencriptado exitosamente:")
  print(f"\n{decrypted.decode()}\n")
except Exception as e:
  print(f"✗ Error al desencriptar: {e}")
```
