### `src/app/main.py`
```py
import asyncio
import os
from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from typing import Any
import litellm
import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI, Response
from fastapi.openapi.utils import get_openapi
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from prometheus_client import CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST, generate_latest
from app.adapters.admin_adapter import router as admin_router
from app.adapters.auth_adapter import router as auth_router
from app.adapters.billing_adapter import router as billing_router
from app.adapters.graphql_adapter import router as graphql_router
from app.adapters.rest_adapter import router as rest_router
from app.adapters.support_adapter import router as support_router
from app.adapters.websocket_adapter import router as websocket_router
from app.config import settings
from app.core.concurrency import cpu_executor
from app.core.finops import BudgetManager
from app.core.telemetry import router as telemetry_router
from app.dependencies import get_background_runner, get_llm_provider, get_reranker
from app.infrastructure.cache import redis_cache
from app.infrastructure.circuit_breaker import CircuitBreakerFactory
from app.infrastructure.database import init_db, sessionmanager
from app.infrastructure.finops_callback import FinOpsCallback
from app.infrastructure.monitoring import configure_cpu_bound_executor_metrics
from app.infrastructure.observability import setup_observability
from app.infrastructure.queue import QueueManager
from app.infrastructure.support_attachment_storage import (
  resolve_support_attachment_local_dir,
)
from app.logging_conf import configure_logging
from app.middleware.audit import AuditMiddleware
from app.middleware.context import ContextMiddleware
from app.middleware.cors import add_cors_middleware
from app.middleware.exception_handler import global_exception_handler
from app.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware
from app.middleware.security import SecurityHeadersMiddleware
from app.modules.audio_notes.router import router as audio_router
from app.modules.dlp_proxy.router import router as dlp_proxy_router
from app.modules.genui.router import router as genui_router
from app.modules.pdf_anki.router import router as pdf_anki_router
from app.modules.receipt_parser.router import router as receipts_router
from app.security.pii_scrubber import PIIScrubber
redis_cache.register_lua_script(RateLimitMiddleware.LUA_TOKEN_BUCKET)
redis_cache.register_lua_script(BudgetManager.LUA_RESERVE)
redis_cache.register_lua_script(BudgetManager.LUA_COMMIT)
configure_logging(json_format=not settings.DEBUG)
logger = structlog.get_logger()
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None]:
  logger.info(
    "Startup: Initializing resources...",
    version=settings.VERSION,
    environment="testing" if settings.TESTING else "production",
  )
  configure_cpu_bound_executor_metrics()
  cpu_executor.initialize()
  finops_logger = FinOpsCallback()
  callbacks: list[Any] = [finops_logger]
  if os.getenv("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY") and os.getenv("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"):
    callbacks.append("langfuse")
    logger.info("Langfuse AI Observability enabled.")
  litellm.callbacks = callbacks
  litellm.set_verbose = False  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
  if not settings.TESTING or settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
    await redis_cache.initialize()
    CircuitBreakerFactory.initialize(redis_cache)
    sessionmanager.initialize()
    await init_db()
    await QueueManager.get_pool()
    if not settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
      logger.info("Warming up AI Models (LLM, Reranker & PII)...")
      reranker = get_reranker()
      llm_provider = get_llm_provider()
      await asyncio.gather(
        reranker.warmup(),
        llm_provider.warmup(),
        PIIScrubber.warmup(),
      )
    else:
      logger.info("Contract Testing Mode: Skipping AI Model Warmup.")
  yield
  logger.info("Shutdown: Cleaning up resources...")
  runner = get_background_runner()
  await runner.shutdown()
  cpu_executor.shutdown()
  if not settings.TESTING or settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
    try:
      await CircuitBreakerFactory.cleanup()
      await QueueManager.close()
      await redis_cache.close()
      await sessionmanager.close()
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error("shutdown_error", error=str(e))
app = FastAPI(
  title=settings.PROJECT_NAME,
  version=settings.VERSION,
  lifespan=lifespan,
)
support_attachment_root = resolve_support_attachment_local_dir()
app.mount(
  "/support-attachments",
  StaticFiles(directory=str(support_attachment_root), check_dir=False),
  name="support-attachments",
)
app.add_middleware(SecurityHeadersMiddleware)
app.add_middleware(ContextMiddleware)
if not settings.TESTING:
  app.add_middleware(AuditMiddleware)
app.add_exception_handler(Exception, global_exception_handler)
add_cors_middleware(app)
if not settings.TESTING:
  app.add_middleware(
    RateLimitMiddleware,
    cache=redis_cache,
    default_rpm=60,
    default_tpm=10000,
    window_seconds=60,
  )
  setup_observability(app)
app.include_router(graphql_router)
app.include_router(websocket_router)
app.include_router(admin_router)
app.include_router(rest_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(billing_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(auth_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(support_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(telemetry_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(audio_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(pdf_anki_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(receipts_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(genui_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
app.include_router(dlp_proxy_router, prefix=settings.API_V1_STR)
def _custom_openapi() -> dict[str, Any]:
  if app.openapi_schema is not None:
    return app.openapi_schema
  openapi_schema = get_openapi(
    title=app.title,
    version=app.version,
    openapi_version=app.openapi_version,
    description=app.description,
    routes=app.routes,
  )
  openapi_schema.setdefault("components", {})
  openapi_schema["components"].setdefault("securitySchemes", {})
  openapi_schema["components"]["securitySchemes"]["BearerAuth"] = {
    "type": "http",
    "scheme": "bearer",
    "bearerFormat": "JWT",
    "description": (
      "JWT de administrador (scopes: admin, read, write). Sin prefijo 'Bearer'."
    ),
  }
  for path, path_item in openapi_schema.get("paths", {}).items():
    if path.startswith("/admin"):
      for _key, operation in path_item.items():
        if isinstance(operation, dict) and "summary" in operation:
          operation["security"] = [{"BearerAuth": []}]
  app.openapi_schema = openapi_schema
  return app.openapi_schema
app.openapi = _custom_openapi  # type: ignore[method-assign]
@app.get("/", include_in_schema=False)
async def root() -> RedirectResponse:
  return RedirectResponse(url="/docs", status_code=302)
@app.get(
  "/metrics",
  response_class=Response,
  responses={
    200: {
      "content": {CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST: {}},
      "description": "Prometheus Metrics",
    }
  },
)
async def metrics() -> Response:
  return Response(content=generate_latest(), media_type=CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST)
```

### `src/app/logging_conf.py`
```py
import atexit
import logging
import logging.handlers
import os
import queue
import sys
from collections.abc import Callable
from copy import copy
from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
from typing import Any, cast
import redis
import structlog
from opentelemetry import trace
def add_opentelemetry_trace_id(
  logger: Any, method_name: str, event_dict: dict[str, Any]
) -> dict[str, Any]:
  span = trace.get_current_span()
  ctx = span.get_span_context()
  if ctx.is_valid:
    event_dict["trace_id"] = format(ctx.trace_id, "032x")
    event_dict["span_id"] = format(ctx.span_id, "016x")
  return event_dict
class DailyCriticalEmailLimiter:
  def __init__(
    self,
    redis_url: str,
    max_emails_per_day: int,
    key_prefix: str = "critical_email_quota",
  ) -> None:
    self._redis_url = redis_url
    self._max_emails_per_day = max_emails_per_day
    self._key_prefix = key_prefix
    self._client: redis.Redis | None = None
  def _get_client(self) -> redis.Redis:
    if self._client is None:
      self._client = redis.Redis.from_url(
        self._redis_url,
        encoding="utf-8",
        decode_responses=True,
        socket_timeout=1.0,
        socket_connect_timeout=1.0,
      )
    return self._client
  @staticmethod
  def _utc_today() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(UTC)
  def try_acquire(self) -> tuple[bool, int, str]:
    now = self._utc_today()
    date_str = now.date().isoformat()
    key = f"{self._key_prefix}:{date_str}"
    client = self._get_client()
    count = cast(int, client.incr(key))
    if count == 1:
      next_midnight = datetime.combine(
        now.date() + timedelta(days=1),
        datetime.min.time(),
        tzinfo=UTC,
      )
      client.expireat(key, next_midnight)
    return count <= self._max_emails_per_day, count, date_str
class RateLimitedSMTPHandler(logging.Handler):
  def __init__(
    self,
    smtp_handler: logging.Handler,
    limiter: DailyCriticalEmailLimiter,
    max_emails_per_day: int,
  ) -> None:
    super().__init__(level=logging.CRITICAL)
    self.smtp_handler = smtp_handler
    self.limiter = limiter
    self.max_emails_per_day = max_emails_per_day
  def emit(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> None:
    try:
      should_send, current_count, date_str = self.limiter.try_acquire()
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      print(
        "⚠️ WARNING: Failed to evaluate CRITICAL email quota in Redis. "
        f"Falling back to sending email. Error: {e}",
        file=sys.stderr,
      )
      self.smtp_handler.emit(record)
      return
    if not should_send:
      return
    record_to_send = copy(record)
    if current_count == self.max_emails_per_day:
      quota_notice = (
        "EMAIL QUOTA NOTICE: This is the last CRITICAL alert email for "
        f"{date_str}. The daily limit of {self.max_emails_per_day} emails "
        "has been reached; further CRITICAL emails will be suppressed "
        f"until 00:00 UTC on the next day after {date_str}."
      )
      record_to_send.msg = f"{record.getMessage()}\n\n{quota_notice}"
      record_to_send.args = ()
      record_to_send.email_quota_notice = quota_notice
    self.smtp_handler.emit(record_to_send)
def configure_logging(json_format: bool = False) -> None:
  under_mutation_mode = os.getenv("MUTANT_UNDER_TEST") in {"stats", "fail"}
  processor_t = Callable[[Any, str, Any], Any]
  def _no_redactor(
    _logger: Any, _method_name: str, event_dict: dict[str, Any]
  ) -> dict[str, Any]:
    return event_dict
  redactor: processor_t
  if under_mutation_mode:
    redactor = _no_redactor
  else:
    from app.security.redaction import structlog_secret_redactor
    redactor = structlog_secret_redactor
  shared_processors: list[Any] = [
    structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,
    add_opentelemetry_trace_id,
    structlog.processors.add_log_level,
    structlog.processors.StackInfoRenderer(),
    structlog.dev.set_exc_info,
    structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso"),
    redactor,
  ]
  renderer: Any
  if json_format:
    renderer = structlog.processors.JSONRenderer()
  else:
    renderer = structlog.dev.ConsoleRenderer()
  email_formatter = logging.Formatter("%(message)s")
  structlog_processors: list[Any] = [
    *shared_processors,
    structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.wrap_for_formatter,
  ]
  handlers: list[logging.Handler] = [logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)]
  log_path = os.getenv("LOG_FILE_PATH")
  if log_path:
    try:
      os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(log_path), exist_ok=True)
      file_handler = logging.FileHandler(log_path)
      handlers.append(file_handler)
    except OSError as e:
      print(
        f"⚠️ WARNING: Failed to setup log file at {log_path}. "
        f"Logs will only go to stdout. Error: {e}",
        file=sys.stderr,
      )
  critical_email_enabled = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_ENABLED", "false").lower() in {
    "1",
    "true",
    "yes",
    "on",
  }
  if critical_email_enabled:
    try:
      smtp_host = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_HOST", "localhost")
      smtp_port = int(os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_PORT", "25"))
      smtp_timeout = float(
        os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "3.0")
      )
      from_addr = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_FROM", "backend-alerts@localhost")
      to_addr = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_TO", "contacto@jmmia.com")
      subject_prefix = os.getenv(
        "CRITICAL_EMAIL_SUBJECT_PREFIX", "[Backend CRITICAL]"
      )
      username = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_USERNAME")
      password = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_PASSWORD")
      redis_url = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_REDIS_URL", os.getenv("REDIS_URL"))
      max_emails_per_day = int(os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_MAX_PER_DAY", "480"))
      quota_key_prefix = os.getenv(
        "CRITICAL_EMAIL_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX", "critical_email_quota"
      )
      use_tls = os.getenv("CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_USE_TLS", "false").lower() in {
        "1",
        "true",
        "yes",
        "on",
      }
      secure: tuple[()] | None = () if use_tls else None
      credentials: tuple[str, str] | None = None
      if username and password:
        credentials = (username, password)
      else:
        print(
          "⚠️ WARNING: CRITICAL_EMAIL_SMTP_USERNAME or PASSWORD is missing. "
          "SMTP authentication will likely fail.",
          file=sys.stderr,
        )
      email_handler = logging.handlers.SMTPHandler(
        mailhost=(smtp_host, smtp_port),
        fromaddr=from_addr,
        toaddrs=[to_addr],
        subject=f"{subject_prefix} Evento CRITICAL en backend",
        credentials=credentials,
        secure=secure,
        timeout=smtp_timeout,
      )
      email_handler.setLevel(logging.CRITICAL)
      email_handler.setFormatter(email_formatter)
      rate_limited_email_handler: logging.Handler = email_handler
      if redis_url:
        limiter = DailyCriticalEmailLimiter(
          redis_url=redis_url,
          max_emails_per_day=max_emails_per_day,
          key_prefix=quota_key_prefix,
        )
        rate_limited_email_handler = RateLimitedSMTPHandler(
          smtp_handler=email_handler,
          limiter=limiter,
          max_emails_per_day=max_emails_per_day,
        )
      log_queue: queue.Queue[Any] = queue.Queue(-1)
      queue_handler = logging.handlers.QueueHandler(log_queue)
      queue_handler.setLevel(logging.CRITICAL)
      handlers.append(queue_handler)
      listener = logging.handlers.QueueListener(
        log_queue, rate_limited_email_handler, respect_handler_level=True
      )
      listener.start()
      queue_handler.listener = listener
      atexit.register(listener.stop)
    except (OSError, ValueError) as e:
      print(
        "⚠️ WARNING: Failed to setup CRITICAL email handler. "
        f"Critical logs will not be emailed. Error: {e}",
        file=sys.stderr,
      )
  console_formatter = structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter(
    processor=renderer,
    foreign_pre_chain=shared_processors,
  )
  for handler in handlers:
    if not isinstance(handler, logging.handlers.QueueHandler):
      handler.setFormatter(console_formatter)
  structlog.configure(
    processors=structlog_processors,
    logger_factory=structlog.stdlib.LoggerFactory(),
    wrapper_class=structlog.make_filtering_bound_logger(logging.INFO),
    cache_logger_on_first_use=True,
  )
  uvicorn_log = logging.getLogger("uvicorn.access")
  uvicorn_log.setLevel(logging.WARNING)
  litellm_logger_names = [
    "litellm",
    "litellm.utils",
    "litellm._logging",
    "LiteLLM",
    "LiteLLM.utils",
    "LiteLLM._logging",
  ]
  for name in litellm_logger_names:
    _log = logging.getLogger(name)
    _log.setLevel(logging.WARNING)
    _log.propagate = False
    if not _log.handlers:
      handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
      handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter("%(message)s"))
      _log.addHandler(handler)
  root_logger = logging.getLogger()
  root_logger.handlers = handlers
  root_logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
```

### `src/app/worker.py`
```py
import asyncio
import base64
import binascii
import json
import logging
import os

logger = structlog.get_logger()
class CircuitBreakerDeferError(Exception):
  pass
tracer = trace.get_tracer(__name__)
STREAM_KEY = "stream:interaction_logs"
DLQ_KEY = "stream:interaction_logs:dlq"
GROUP_NAME = "group:log_persisters"
CONSUMER_NAME = f"consumer:{socket.gethostname()}:{uuid4().hex[:6]}"
WORKER_METRICS_PORT = 8001
WORKER_HEALTH_KEY = "worker:health_heartbeat"
BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 120
DB_CB_KEY = "db:persistence"
_SINGLE_INSERT_SEMAPHORE: asyncio.Semaphore | None = None
def _get_single_insert_semaphore() -> asyncio.Semaphore:
  global _SINGLE_INSERT_SEMAPHORE
  if _SINGLE_INSERT_SEMAPHORE is None:
    _SINGLE_INSERT_SEMAPHORE = asyncio.Semaphore(10)
  return _SINGLE_INSERT_SEMAPHORE
def _coerce_pdf_cache_payload(value: bytes | str) -> bytes:
  if isinstance(value, bytes):
    return value
  compact = value.strip().replace("\n", "").replace("\r", "")
  if not compact:
    return b""
  try:
    pad = (-len(compact)) % 4
    if pad:
      compact += "=" * pad
    return base64.b64decode(compact.encode("ascii"), validate=True)
  except (ValueError, binascii.Error):
    return compact.encode("utf-8")
class FeedbackUpdateValues(TypedDict, total=False):
  score: float
  user_correction: str | None
  status: KnowledgeStatus
  embedding: list[float]
async def maintain_db_partitions(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  logger.info("Maintenance: Attempting to acquire lock for partition check...")
  try:
    async with redis_cache.lock(MAINTENANCE_LOCK_KEY, ttl=60):
      logger.info("Maintenance: Lock acquired. Checking database partitions...")
      async with sessionmanager.connect() as conn:
        await ensure_partitions(conn)
      PARTITION_MAINTENANCE_STATUS.set(1)
      logger.info("Maintenance: Partition check completed successfully.")
  except TimeoutError:
    logger.info(
      "Maintenance: Lock busy. Skipping partition check "
      "(handled by another worker/api)."
    )
  except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
    logger.error("partition_maintenance_failed", error=str(e))
    PARTITION_MAINTENANCE_STATUS.set(0)
async def worker_health_check(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  try:
    await redis_cache.set(WORKER_HEALTH_KEY, datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(), ttl=60)
  except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
    logger.warning("worker_health_check_failed", error=str(e))
async def startup(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  configure_logging(json_format=not settings.DEBUG)
  setup_core_observability(service_name_override="default-app-worker")
  configure_cpu_bound_executor_metrics()
  try:
    start_http_server(WORKER_METRICS_PORT)
    logger.info("worker_metrics_exposed", port=WORKER_METRICS_PORT)
  except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
    logger.error("worker_metrics_start_failed", error=str(e))
  cpu_executor.initialize()
  sessionmanager.initialize()
  await redis_cache.initialize()
  CircuitBreakerFactory.initialize(redis_cache)
  CircuitBreakerFactory.get_breaker(DB_CB_KEY)
  await redis_cache.ensure_group(STREAM_KEY, GROUP_NAME)
  await maintain_db_partitions(ctx)
  logger.info("worker_started", consumer=CONSUMER_NAME)
async def shutdown(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  await CircuitBreakerFactory.cleanup()
  await redis_cache.close()
  await sessionmanager.close()
  cpu_executor.shutdown()
  logger.info("Worker shutdown.")
async def _move_to_dlq(entry_id: str, data: dict[str, Any], error: str) -> None:
  try:
    dlq_payload = {
      "original_stream_id": entry_id,
      "error": error,
      "failed_at": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
      "original_payload": data,
    }
    await redis_cache.xadd(DLQ_KEY, dlq_payload)
    DLQ_EVENTS_TOTAL.labels(reason="persistence_failure").inc()
    logger.warning(
      "Message moved to DLQ",
      stream_id=entry_id,
      dlq_stream=DLQ_KEY,
      error=error,
    )
  except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
    logger.critical(
      "CRITICAL:Failed to move message to DLQ. Dropping message to unblock queue",
      stream_id=entry_id,
      original_error=error,
      dlq_error=str(e),
      payload_dump=data,
    )
async def _persist_batch_optimistic(log_dicts: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
  with BATCH_PERSISTENCE_SECONDS.time():
    async with sessionmanager.transaction() as db_session:
      stmt = insert(FeedbackLog).values(log_dicts)
      stmt = stmt.on_conflict_do_nothing(index_elements=["id", "created_at"])
      await db_session.execute(stmt)
async def _persist_single_core(
  entry_id: str,
  log_data: dict[str, Any],
  raw_data: dict[str, Any],
  semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
) -> bool:
  async with semaphore:
    breaker = CircuitBreakerFactory.get_breaker(DB_CB_KEY)
    async def _do_insert() -> None:
      async with sessionmanager.transaction() as db_session:
        stmt = insert(FeedbackLog).values([log_data])
        stmt = stmt.on_conflict_do_nothing(index_elements=["id", "created_at"])
        await db_session.execute(stmt)
    try:
      if isinstance(breaker, CircuitBreaker):
        await breaker.call(_do_insert)
      else:
        await _do_insert()
      LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="success_fallback").inc()
      return True
    except CircuitBreakerOpenError:
      logger.warning(
        "single_insert_circuit_open",
        entry_id=entry_id,
        action="moving_to_dlq",
      )
      await _move_to_dlq(entry_id, raw_data, "Circuit Breaker OPEN")
      LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="db_error").inc()
      return True  # Return True to ack the message since it's safely in DLQ
    except (IntegrityError, DataError) as e:
      logger.error("single_insert_data_error", entry_id=entry_id, error=str(e))
      await _move_to_dlq(entry_id, raw_data, str(e))
      LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="validation_error").inc()
      return True
    except OperationalError as e:
      logger.error(
        "single_insert_operational_error",
        entry_id=entry_id,
        error=str(e),
      )
      LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="db_error").inc()
      return False
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error("single_insert_failed", entry_id=entry_id, error=str(e))
      await _move_to_dlq(entry_id, raw_data, str(e))
      LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="db_error").inc()
      return True
async def _persist_single_safe(
  entry_id: str, log_data: dict[str, Any], raw_data: dict[str, Any]
) -> bool:
  return await _persist_single_core(
    entry_id=entry_id,
    log_data=log_data,
    raw_data=raw_data,
    semaphore=_get_single_insert_semaphore(),
  )
async def _process_batch(
  messages: list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]],
) -> None:
  if not messages:
    return
  try:
    await asyncio.wait_for(
      _process_batch_inner(messages),
      timeout=BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    )
  except TimeoutError:
    logger.critical(
      "batch_processing_timeout",
      batch_size=len(messages),
      timeout_seconds=BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    )
async def _process_batch_inner(
  messages: list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]],
) -> None:
  entry_ids: list[str] = []
  valid_logs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
  raw_map: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
  id_to_stream_map: dict[UUID, str] = {}
  invalid_entry_ids: list[str] = []
  for entry_id, data in messages:
    entry_ids.append(entry_id)
    raw_map[entry_id] = data
    trace_id = data.get("trace_id")
    tenant_id = data.get("tenant_id")
    with structlog.contextvars.bound_contextvars(
      message_id=entry_id, trace_id=trace_id, tenant_id=tenant_id
    ):
      try:
        log_entry = InteractionLogEvent.model_validate(data)
        dumped = log_entry.model_dump()
        valid_logs.append(dumped)
        id_to_stream_map[log_entry.id] = entry_id
      except ValidationError as e:
        logger.error("validation_failed_moving_to_dlq", error=str(e))
        await _move_to_dlq(entry_id, data, str(e))
        DLQ_EVENTS_TOTAL.labels(reason="validation").inc()
        LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="validation_error").inc()
        invalid_entry_ids.append(entry_id)
        continue
  if invalid_entry_ids:
    await redis_cache.xack(STREAM_KEY, GROUP_NAME, invalid_entry_ids)
  if not valid_logs:
    return
  breaker = CircuitBreakerFactory.get_breaker(DB_CB_KEY)
  async def _run_batch_once() -> None:
    await _persist_batch_optimistic(valid_logs)
  async def _attempt_batch() -> None:
    if isinstance(breaker, CircuitBreaker):
      await breaker.call(_run_batch_once)
    else:
      await _run_batch_once()
  max_sqlalchemy_attempts = 3
  attempt = 0
  fallback_to_single = False
  while True:
    try:
      await _attempt_batch()
      valid_entry_ids = [id_to_stream_map[log["id"]] for log in valid_logs]
      if valid_entry_ids:
        await redis_cache.xack(STREAM_KEY, GROUP_NAME, valid_entry_ids)
      LOGS_PROCESSED_TOTAL.labels(status="success").inc(len(valid_logs))
      logger.info("batch_persisted", count=len(valid_logs))
      return
    except CircuitBreakerOpenError:
      logger.warning(
        "DB Circuit Breaker OPEN. Skipping batch persistence (Backpressure)."
      )
      return
    except (IntegrityError, DataError) as e:
      logger.warning(
        "batch_persist_data_error",
        error=str(e),
        action="falling_back_to_single_inserts",
      )
      fallback_to_single = True
      break
    except OperationalError as e:
      attempt += 1
      if attempt < max_sqlalchemy_attempts:
        logger.warning(
          "batch_persist_operational_error_retrying",
          attempt=attempt,
          max_attempts=max_sqlalchemy_attempts,
          error=str(e),
        )
        await asyncio.sleep(1.0 * attempt)
        continue
      logger.error(
        "batch_persist_operational_error_giving_up",
        attempts=attempt,
        error=str(e),
      )
      return
    except SQLAlchemyError as e:
      attempt += 1
      if attempt < max_sqlalchemy_attempts:
        logger.warning(
          "batch_persist_sqlalchemy_error_retrying",
          attempt=attempt,
          max_attempts=max_sqlalchemy_attempts,
          error=str(e),
        )
        await asyncio.sleep(1.0 * attempt)
        continue
      logger.error(
        "batch_persist_sqlalchemy_error_giving_up",
        attempts=attempt,
        error=str(e),
      )
      fallback_to_single = True
      break
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error(
        "batch_persistence_failed_fallback",
        error=str(e),
      )
      fallback_to_single = True
      break
  if fallback_to_single:
    tasks = []
    stream_ids_in_fallback = []
    for log_data in valid_logs:
      log_id = log_data["id"]
      stream_id = id_to_stream_map.get(log_id)
      if stream_id:
        raw_data = raw_map[stream_id]
        tasks.append(
          _persist_single_safe(
            entry_id=stream_id,
            log_data=log_data,
            raw_data=raw_data,
          )
        )
        stream_ids_in_fallback.append(stream_id)
    if tasks:
      results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
      fallback_ids_to_ack: list[str] = []
      for stream_id, result in zip(stream_ids_in_fallback, results, strict=True):
        if isinstance(result, bool) and result is True:
          fallback_ids_to_ack.append(stream_id)
        elif isinstance(result, Exception):
          logger.error(
            "single_insert_task_crashed",
            stream_id=stream_id,
            error=str(result),
          )
      if fallback_ids_to_ack:
        await redis_cache.xack(
          STREAM_KEY,
          GROUP_NAME,
          fallback_ids_to_ack,
        )
async def flush_interaction_logs(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  breaker = CircuitBreakerFactory.get_breaker(DB_CB_KEY)
  try:
    state = await breaker._get_state()
    if state == CircuitState.OPEN:
      logger.warning("DB Circuit Breaker OPEN. Pausing log consumption.")
      return
  except (OSError, TimeoutError, ConnectionError, RuntimeError) as e:
    logger.debug(
      "Circuit breaker state check failed, proceeding with consumption.",
      error=str(e),
    )
  batch_size = 50
  pending_messages = await redis_cache.xreadgroup(
    STREAM_KEY, GROUP_NAME, CONSUMER_NAME, batch_size, start_id="0"
  )
  if pending_messages:
    logger.warning(
      f"Recovering {len(pending_messages)} pending messages from PEL..."
    )
    await _process_batch(pending_messages)
  new_messages = await redis_cache.xreadgroup(
    STREAM_KEY, GROUP_NAME, CONSUMER_NAME, batch_size, start_id=">"
  )
  if new_messages:
    logger.info("processing_new_logs", count=len(new_messages))
    await _process_batch(new_messages)
async def _get_or_create_embedding(correction: str, request_id: str) -> list[float]:
  cache_key = f"worker:embedding:{request_id}"
  cached = await redis_cache.get(cache_key)
  if cached:
    logger.info(
      "Using cached embedding from previous attempt", request_id=request_id
    )
    return cast(list[float], cached)
  llm_provider = get_llm_provider()
  new_embedding = await llm_provider.embed(correction)
  await redis_cache.set(cache_key, new_embedding, ttl=3600)
  return new_embedding
@retry(
  stop=stop_after_attempt(3),
  wait=wait_exponential_jitter(initial=0.5, max=5),
  retry=retry_if_exception_type(
    (SQLAlchemyError, ConnectionError, TimeoutError, OSError)
  ),
  before_sleep=before_sleep_log(logger, logging.WARNING),
)
async def process_feedback_task(
  ctx: dict[Any, Any],
  request_id: UUID,
  score: float,
  correction: str | None,
  trace_context: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> None:
  job_id = ctx.get("job_id", str(uuid4()))
  structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(job_id=job_id, request_id=str(request_id))
  otel_ctx = extract_trace_context(trace_context)
  with tracer.start_as_current_span(
    "worker.process_feedback", context=otel_ctx
  ) as span:
    span.set_attribute("job.id", job_id)
    span.set_attribute("feedback.request_id", str(request_id))
    span.set_attribute("feedback.score", score)
    logger.info("processing_feedback_job", request_id=str(request_id))
    breaker = CircuitBreakerFactory.get_breaker(DB_CB_KEY)
    if await breaker._get_state() == CircuitState.OPEN:
      logger.warning("DB Circuit Breaker OPEN. Deferring feedback task.")
      raise CircuitBreakerDeferError("DB Circuit Breaker Open - Deferring")
    try:
      feedback_data: dict[str, Any] | None = None
      async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
        stmt = select(
          FeedbackLog.tenant_id,
          FeedbackLog.user_prompt,
          FeedbackLog.trace_id,
          FeedbackLog.user_correction,
          FeedbackLog.score,
        ).where(FeedbackLog.request_id == request_id)
        result = await session.execute(stmt)
        row = result.first()
        if row:
          feedback_data = {
            "tenant_id": row.tenant_id,
            "user_prompt": row.user_prompt,
            "trace_id": row.trace_id,
            "existing_correction": row.user_correction,
            "existing_score": row.score,
          }
      if not feedback_data:
        logger.warning("feedback_entry_not_found", request_id=str(request_id))
        FEEDBACK_TASKS_TOTAL.labels(status="not_found").inc()
        span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, "Not Found"))
        return
      existing_correction = feedback_data["existing_correction"]
      existing_score = feedback_data["existing_score"]
      if existing_correction == correction and existing_score == score:
        logger.info(
          "feedback_already_processed_idempotent", request_id=str(request_id)
        )
        FEEDBACK_TASKS_TOTAL.labels(status="success").inc()
        span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.OK))
        return
      tenant_id = feedback_data["tenant_id"]
      user_prompt = feedback_data["user_prompt"]
      db_trace_id = feedback_data.get("trace_id")
      if tenant_id:
        structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(tenant_id=tenant_id)
      if db_trace_id:
        structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(original_trace_id=db_trace_id)
      new_embedding: list[float] | None = None
      emb_cost: float = 0.0
      budget_manager = BudgetManager(redis_cache)
      token_manager = get_token_manager()
      cost_calculator = get_cost_calculator()
      needs_embedding = bool(correction and correction != existing_correction)
      if needs_embedding and correction is not None:
        try:
          logger.info("Generating embedding for correction...")
          embedding_model = settings.EMBEDDING_MODEL
          token_count = await token_manager.count_tokens(
            correction, embedding_model
          )
          emb_cost = cost_calculator.calculate_cost(
            embedding_model, token_count, 0
          )
          if tenant_id and not await budget_manager.can_afford(
            tenant_id, emb_cost
          ):
            logger.warning(
              "Tenant cannot afford embedding generation. "
              "Skipping vector update.",
              cost=emb_cost,
            )
            emb_cost = 0.0
          else:
            new_embedding = await _get_or_create_embedding(
              correction, str(request_id)
            )
            if tenant_id and emb_cost > 0:
              await budget_manager.consume_budget(
                tenant_id, emb_cost, idempotency_key=job_id
              )
            cache_service = SemanticCacheService(redis_cache)
            await cache_service.invalidate_exact_match(user_prompt)
        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
          logger.error(
            f"Embedding generation failed for feedback: {e}. "
            "Proceeding with text-only update."
          )
          span.record_exception(e)
          new_embedding = None
          emb_cost = 0.0
      async def _execute_update() -> None:
        async with sessionmanager.transaction() as db_session:
          update_values: FeedbackUpdateValues = {
            "score": score,
            "user_correction": correction,
          }
          if score == 1.0:
            update_values["status"] = KnowledgeStatus.APPROVED
          if new_embedding:
            update_values["embedding"] = new_embedding
            update_stmt = (
              update(FeedbackLog)
              .where(FeedbackLog.request_id == request_id)
              .values(
                cost_usd=FeedbackLog.cost_usd + emb_cost,
                **update_values,
              )
            )
          else:
            update_stmt = (
              update(FeedbackLog)
              .where(FeedbackLog.request_id == request_id)
              .values(**update_values)
            )
          await db_session.execute(update_stmt)
      await breaker.call(_execute_update)
      logger.info(
        "feedback_updated",
        request_id=str(request_id),
        embedding_updated=bool(new_embedding),
      )
      FEEDBACK_TASKS_TOTAL.labels(status="success").inc()
      span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.OK))
    except Exception as e:
      logger.error(
        "feedback_persistence_failed",
        request_id=str(request_id),
        error=str(e),
      )
      FEEDBACK_TASKS_TOTAL.labels(status="db_error").inc()
      span.record_exception(e)
      span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR))
      raise
    finally:
      structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars()
async def process_pdf_task(
  ctx: dict[Any, Any],
  document_id: str,
  file_hash: str,
  user_tier: str,
  user_id: str,
  title: str,
  payg_unified_model_id: str | None = None,
  pdf_page_count: int = 0,
  trace_context: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> None:
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.application.pdf_deck_cache import pdf_deck_redis_key
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.application.pdf_phase_logging import run_pdf_phase
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.document_repository import (
    DocumentRepository,
  )
  job_id = ctx.get("job_id", str(uuid4()))
  structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(
    job_id=job_id,
    document_id=document_id,
    file_hash=file_hash,
    user_id=user_id,
  )
  otel_ctx = extract_trace_context(trace_context)
  span = tracer.start_span("worker.process_pdf", context=otel_ctx)
  token = otel_context.attach(trace.set_span_in_context(span, context=otel_ctx))
  span_closed = False
  def _end_pdf_span() -> None:
    nonlocal span_closed
    if span_closed:
      return
    span_closed = True
    otel_context.detach(token)
    span.end()
  span.set_attribute("job.id", job_id)
  span.set_attribute("document.id", document_id)
  span.set_attribute("user.tier", user_tier)
  span.set_attribute("pdf_anki.file_hash_prefix", file_hash[:12])
  span.set_attribute("pdf_anki.pdf_page_count", pdf_page_count)
  raw_key = f"pdf:raw:{file_hash}"
  deck_key = pdf_deck_redis_key(file_hash)
  try:
    cached_raw = await run_pdf_phase(
      phase="pdf_raw_fetch",
      dependency="redis",
      document_id=document_id,
      worker_user_id=user_id,
      worker_job_id=str(job_id),
      coro_factory=lambda: redis_cache.get_bytes(raw_key),
    )
  except Exception as redis_exc:  # noqa: BLE001
    logger.error(
      "pdf_raw_fetch_unhandled",
      document_id=document_id,
      user_id=user_id,
      job_id=str(job_id),
      error=str(redis_exc),
    )
    try:
      async with sessionmanager.session() as u_session:
        u_repo = DocumentRepository(u_session)
        await u_repo.update_state(
          document_id,
          PdfDocumentState.FAILED,
          error_message="Redis error while loading PDF bytes from cache.",
        )
        await u_session.commit()
    except Exception as inner_exc:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error(
        "process_pdf_task_failure_state_failed",
        document_id=document_id,
        error=str(inner_exc),
      )
    span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, "Redis Fetch Failed"))
    _end_pdf_span()
    return
  if not cached_raw:
    logger.warning(
      "pdf_raw_missing",
      document_id=document_id,
      user_id=user_id,
      job_id=str(job_id),
    )
    async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
      repo = DocumentRepository(session)
      await repo.update_state(
        document_id,
        PdfDocumentState.FAILED,
        error_message="PDF bytes not found in cache (expired or missing).",
      )
      await session.commit()
    span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, "PDF Missing in Cache"))
    _end_pdf_span()
    return
  raw_pdf_payload = _coerce_pdf_cache_payload(cached_raw)
  try:
    pdf_bytes = decrypt_bytes(raw_pdf_payload)
  except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
    pdf_bytes = raw_pdf_payload
  async def _cache_get(key: str) -> Any:
    return await redis_cache.get(deck_key if key == file_hash else key)
  async def _cache_set(key: str, deck: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
    if key == file_hash:
      plaintext = json.dumps(deck).encode("utf-8")
      ciphertext = encrypt_bytes(plaintext)
      encoded = base64.b64encode(ciphertext).decode("ascii")
      await redis_cache.set(deck_key, encoded, ttl=7 * 86400)
  logger.info(
    "process_pdf_task_start",
    document_id=document_id,
    user_id=user_id,
    job_id=str(job_id),
    user_tier=user_tier,
    file_hash=file_hash,
  )
  try:
    await _process_pdf_task_pipeline(
      document_id=document_id,
      file_hash=file_hash,
      user_tier=user_tier,
      user_id=user_id,
      title=title,
      raw_key=raw_key,
      pdf_bytes=pdf_bytes,
      job_id=str(job_id),
      _cache_get=_cache_get,
      _cache_set=_cache_set,
      payg_upload_unified_model_id=payg_unified_model_id,
      pdf_page_count=pdf_page_count,
    )
    span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.OK))
  except Exception as exc:
    logger.exception(
      "process_pdf_task_unhandled",
      document_id=document_id,
      user_id=user_id,
      job_id=str(job_id),
      error=str(exc),
    )
    span.record_exception(exc)
    span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(exc)))
    try:
      async with sessionmanager.session() as u_session:
        u_repo = DocumentRepository(u_session)
        await u_repo.update_state(
          document_id,
          PdfDocumentState.FAILED,
          error_message=f"Worker error: {exc}",
        )
        await u_session.commit()
    except Exception as inner_exc:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error(
        "process_pdf_task_failure_state_failed",
        document_id=document_id,
        error=str(inner_exc),
      )
  finally:
    _end_pdf_span()
async def _process_pdf_task_pipeline(
  *,
  document_id: str,
  file_hash: str,
  user_tier: str,
  user_id: str,
  title: str,
  raw_key: str,
  pdf_bytes: bytes,
  job_id: str,
  _cache_get: Any,
  _cache_set: Any,
  payg_upload_unified_model_id: str | None = None,
  pdf_page_count: int = 0,
) -> None:
  from app.domain.billing import WalletTransaction, WalletTransactionType
  from app.infrastructure.billing_repository import (
    SqlAlchemyWalletTransactionRepository,
  )
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.application.process_pdf import process_pdf_use_case
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.domain.entities import PdfDeckStatus
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.document_repository import (
    DocumentRepository,
  )
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.embedding_adapter import (
    PdfEmbeddingAdapter,
  )
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.flashcard_generator import (
    FlashcardGeneratorAdapter,
  )
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.lancedb_adapter import LanceDBAdapter
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.llamaparse_adapter import (
    LlamaParseAdapter,
  )
  from app.modules.pdf_anki.infrastructure.users_repository import (
    PdfAnkiUserRepository,
  )
  _key = getattr(settings, "LLAMAPARSE_API_KEY", None)
  pdf2anki_key = os.getenv("LLAMAPARSE_PDF2ANKI_API_KEY")
  if pdf2anki_key:
    llamaparse_key = pdf2anki_key
  else:
    llamaparse_key = (
      _key.get_secret_value()
      if hasattr(_key, "get_secret_value") and _key
      else ""
    )
  parser = LlamaParseAdapter(api_key=llamaparse_key)
  vector_store = LanceDBAdapter()
  llm = get_llm_provider()
  embedder = PdfEmbeddingAdapter(llm)
  flashcard_gen = FlashcardGeneratorAdapter(llm)
  async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
    repo = DocumentRepository(session)
    wallet_tx_repo = SqlAlchemyWalletTransactionRepository(session)
    payg_stored: str | None = None
    pdf_payg_unlocked = False
    wallet_repo: PdfAnkiUserRepository | None = None
    pdf_db_tier: str | None = None
    effective_user_tier = user_tier
    if user_id and str(user_id).strip().lower() != "anonymous":
      pref_repo = PdfAnkiUserRepository(session)
      wallet_repo = pref_repo
      pref_row = await pref_repo.get_user_by_id(user_id)
      if pref_row is not None:
        pdf_payg_unlocked = getattr(pref_row, "payg", None) is True
        raw_act = getattr(pref_row, "active_unified_pdf_model", None)
        payg_stored = str(raw_act).strip() if raw_act else None
        raw_tier = getattr(pref_row, "tier", None)
        if raw_tier is not None and str(raw_tier).strip():
          pdf_db_tier = str(raw_tier).strip().lower()
          effective_user_tier = pdf_db_tier
    await repo.update_state(document_id, PdfDocumentState.PROCESSING)
    await session.commit()
    from app.infrastructure.groq_free_tier_completion import (
      GROQ_FREE_TIER_BUSY_STATUS_MESSAGE,
      GROQ_FREE_TIER_TPD_ALL_KEYS_STATUS_MESSAGE,
    )
    congestion_notice_sent = False
    tpd_all_keys_notice_sent = False
    async def _groq_congestion_commit() -> None:
      nonlocal congestion_notice_sent
      if congestion_notice_sent:
        return
      congestion_notice_sent = True
      async def _persist_busy_message() -> None:
        await repo.update_state(
          document_id,
          PdfDocumentState.PROCESSING,
          error_message=GROQ_FREE_TIER_BUSY_STATUS_MESSAGE,
        )
        await session.commit()
      await asyncio.shield(_persist_busy_message())
    async def _groq_tpd_all_keys_commit() -> None:
      nonlocal tpd_all_keys_notice_sent
      if tpd_all_keys_notice_sent:
        return
      tpd_all_keys_notice_sent = True
      async def _persist_tpd_message() -> None:
        await repo.update_state(
          document_id,
          PdfDocumentState.PROCESSING,
          error_message=GROQ_FREE_TIER_TPD_ALL_KEYS_STATUS_MESSAGE,
        )
        await session.commit()
      await asyncio.shield(_persist_tpd_message())
    result = await process_pdf_use_case(
      document_id=document_id,
      file_hash=file_hash,
      pdf_bytes=pdf_bytes,
      filename=title or "document.pdf",
      parser=parser,
      vector_store=vector_store,
      document_repo=repo,
      embedder=embedder,
      flashcard_gen=flashcard_gen,
      user_tier=effective_user_tier,
      cache_get=_cache_get,
      cache_set=_cache_set,
      worker_user_id=user_id,
      worker_job_id=job_id,
      llm=llm,
      feedback_db_session=session,
      groq_congestion_commit_hook=_groq_congestion_commit,
      groq_tpd_all_keys_commit_hook=_groq_tpd_all_keys_commit,
      payg_upload_unified_model_id=payg_upload_unified_model_id,
      payg_stored_unified_model_id=payg_stored,
      pdf_anki_balance_payg_unlocked=pdf_payg_unlocked,
      pdf_anki_db_tier=pdf_db_tier,
      pdf_anki_wallet=wallet_repo,
      pdf_page_count=pdf_page_count,
    )
    status_value = str(result.get("status", "completed"))
    if status_value == "failed":
      await session.commit()
      logger.error(
        "process_pdf_use_case_failed",
        document_id=document_id,
        user_id=user_id,
        job_id=job_id,
        error=str(result.get("error_message", "")),
      )
      return
    deck_status = (
      PdfDeckStatus.COMPLETED_PARTIAL
      if status_value == "completed_partial"
      else PdfDeckStatus.COMPLETED
    )
    charged_raw = result.get("payg_wallet_charged_usd")
    balance_raw = result.get("payg_wallet_balance_usd")
    if user_id and charged_raw is not None and balance_raw is not None:
      await wallet_tx_repo.insert_transaction(
        WalletTransaction(
          tenant_id=user_id,
          module="pdf_anki",
          transaction_type=WalletTransactionType.DEBIT,
          amount_usd=Decimal(str(charged_raw)),
          balance_after_usd=Decimal(str(balance_raw)),
          reference_id=document_id,
          description=("PAYG wallet debit for pdf_anki document processing"),
        )
      )
    deck_cards = list(result.get("deck", []))
    try:
      await repo.create_deck_with_flashcards(
        user_id=user_id,
        document_id=document_id,
        file_hash=file_hash,
        title=title,
        status=deck_status,
        flashcards=deck_cards,
      )
      await (
        session.commit()
      )  # AHORA SE CONFIRMA EL ESTADO Y LAS FLASHCARDS JUNTOS
    except Exception as persist_exc:
      await session.rollback()
      logger.exception(
        "pdf_deck_persist_failed",
        document_id=document_id,
        user_id=user_id,
        job_id=job_id,
        error=str(persist_exc),
      )
      async with sessionmanager.session() as fail_session:
        fail_repo = DocumentRepository(fail_session)
        await fail_repo.update_state(
          document_id,
          PdfDocumentState.FAILED,
          error_message=f"Deck persistence failed: {persist_exc}",
        )
        await fail_session.commit()
      return
    if wallet_repo is not None and user_id and pdf_page_count > 0:
      try:
        await wallet_repo.increment_monthly_pages_usage(
          user_id,
          pages=pdf_page_count,
          window_key=wallet_repo.monthly_window_key(),
        )
        await session.commit()
      except Exception as usage_exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        logger.warning(
          "pdf_anki_monthly_page_usage_increment_failed",
          document_id=document_id,
          user_id=user_id,
          job_id=job_id,
          pages=pdf_page_count,
          error=str(usage_exc),
        )
  await redis_cache.delete(raw_key)
  logger.info(
    "process_pdf_done",
    document_id=document_id,
    user_id=user_id,
    job_id=job_id,
    status=result.get("status"),
    payg_wallet_charged_usd=result.get("payg_wallet_charged_usd"),
    payg_wallet_balance_usd=result.get("payg_wallet_balance_usd"),
  )
async def maintain_pdf_data_lifecycle(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  logger.info("pdf_anki_lifecycle_cron_start")
  adapter = LanceDBAdapter()
  async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
    await prune_expired_pdf_data_use_case(session=session, vector_store=adapter)
    await session.commit()
  logger.info("pdf_anki_lifecycle_cron_done")
async def maintain_audio_notes_data_lifecycle(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  logger.info("audio_notes_lifecycle_cron_start")
  async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
    expired_user_ids = await prune_expired_audio_notes_data_use_case(
      session=session
    )
    await session.commit()
  for user_id in expired_user_ids:
    await redis_cache.delete(f"cache:rules_count:{user_id}")
    await redis_cache.delete(f"glossary:{user_id}")
  logger.info("audio_notes_lifecycle_cron_done")
async def maintain_receipt_parser_data_lifecycle(ctx: dict[Any, Any]) -> None:
  logger.info("receipt_parser_lifecycle_cron_start")
  async with sessionmanager.session() as session:
    await prune_expired_receipt_parser_data_use_case(session=session)
    await session.commit()
  logger.info("receipt_parser_lifecycle_cron_done")
class WorkerSettings:
  functions: ClassVar[list[Any]] = [
    process_feedback_task,
    func(
      process_pdf_task,
      max_tries=1,
      timeout=float(settings.PDF_PROCESS_JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS),
    ),
    flush_interaction_logs,
    maintain_db_partitions,
    worker_health_check,
    maintain_pdf_data_lifecycle,
    maintain_audio_notes_data_lifecycle,
    maintain_receipt_parser_data_lifecycle,
  ]
  on_startup = startup
  on_shutdown = shutdown
  redis_settings = RedisSettings.from_dsn(settings.REDIS_URL)
  cron_jobs: ClassVar[list[Any]] = (
    []
    if os.getenv("MUTANT_UNDER_TEST")
    else [
      cron(flush_interaction_logs, minute=None, second=0),  # every minute
      cron(maintain_db_partitions, hour=0, minute=0),  # daily at midnight
      cron(maintain_pdf_data_lifecycle, hour=1, minute=0),
      cron(maintain_audio_notes_data_lifecycle, hour=1, minute=15),
      cron(maintain_receipt_parser_data_lifecycle, hour=1, minute=30),
      cron(worker_health_check, minute=None, second=30),  # every minute at :30
    ]
  )
```

```py
from app.core.health import HealthService

from app.core.query_expansion import QueryExpander

  LLMProvider,

  TokenManagerPort,
)

from app.infrastructure.database import get_db, sessionmanager
from app.infrastructure.dspy_artifacts import get_adaptive_router_dspy_policy

from app.infrastructure.logging_repository import ResilientInteractionLogger

from app.infrastructure.reranker_adapter import ResilientReranker

@lru_cache
def get_llm_provider() -> LLMProvider:
  if settings.MOCK_LLM:
    return MockLLMProvider()
  return LiteLLMAdapter()
@lru_cache
def get_background_runner() -> BackgroundRunner:
  return AsyncioBackgroundRunner()
@lru_cache
def get_cache_port() -> CachePort:
  return redis_cache
@lru_cache
def get_database_port() -> DatabasePort:
  return sessionmanager
@lru_cache
def get_event_bus_port() -> EventBusPort:
  return redis_cache
@lru_cache
def get_support_attachment_storage_port() -> SupportAttachmentStoragePort:

@lru_cache
def get_singleflight() -> AsyncSingleFlight:

def get_prompt_repository() -> PromptRepositoryPort:

@lru_cache
def get_queue_manager() -> type[QueueManager]:

@lru_cache
def get_security_service() -> SecurityPort:
  return SecurityAdapter()
@lru_cache
def get_prompt_manager(
  cache: Annotated[CachePort, Depends(get_cache_port)],
  repo: Annotated[PromptRepositoryPort, Depends(get_prompt_repository)],
) -> PromptManager:
  return PromptManager(cache=cache, repo=repo)
@lru_cache
def get_reranker() -> RerankerPort:
  if settings.MOCK_LLM:
    return MockReranker()
  return ResilientReranker(concurrency=settings.RERANKER_CONCURRENCY)
@lru_cache
def get_budget_manager(
  cache: Annotated[CachePort, Depends(get_cache_port)],
) -> BudgetManager:
  from prometheus_client import REGISTRY, Counter
  try:
    budget_counter: Counter = Counter(
      "budget_exceeded_total",
      "Times tenants exceeded daily budget",
      ["tenant_id"],
    )
  except ValueError as exc:
    existing = getattr(REGISTRY, "_names_to_collectors", {}).get(
      "budget_exceeded_total"
    )
    if isinstance(existing, Counter):
      budget_counter = existing
    else:
      raise exc
  def _on_exceeded(tenant_id: str) -> None:
    budget_counter.labels(tenant_id=tenant_id).inc()
  return BudgetManager(
    cache=cache,
    daily_limit=settings.TENANT_DAILY_BUDGET,
    on_budget_exceeded=_on_exceeded,
  )
@lru_cache
def get_token_manager() -> TokenManagerPort:
  return LiteLLMTokenManager(
    context_limits=settings.MODEL_CONTEXT_LIMITS,
  )
@lru_cache
def get_cost_calculator() -> CostCalculatorPort:
  return LiteLLMCostCalculator()
@lru_cache
def get_dynamic_config_service(
  cache: Annotated[CachePort, Depends(get_cache_port)],
) -> DynamicConfigService:
  return DynamicConfigService(cache=cache)
def get_semantic_cache_service(
  cache: Annotated[CachePort, Depends(get_cache_port)],
  db: Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)],
) -> SemanticCachePort:
  from app.infrastructure.vector_search import (
    SqlAlchemyVectorSearch,
  )
  return SemanticCacheService(
    cache=cache,
    vector_search=SqlAlchemyVectorSearch(db),
    lookback_days=settings.SEMANTIC_CACHE_LOOKBACK_DAYS,
  )
def get_search_engine(
  db: Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)],
) -> SearchEnginePort:
  return SqlAlchemySearchEngine(session=db)
@lru_cache
def get_semantic_classifier(
  provider: Annotated[LLMProvider, Depends(get_llm_provider)],
  prompt_manager: Annotated[PromptManager, Depends(get_prompt_manager)],
) -> SemanticClassifier:
  return SemanticClassifier(
    llm_provider=provider,
    prompt_manager=prompt_manager,
    classification_model=settings.DEFAULT_FAST_MODEL,
  )
@lru_cache
def get_query_expander(
  provider: Annotated[LLMProvider, Depends(get_llm_provider)],
) -> QueryExpander:
  return QueryExpander(
    llm_provider=provider,
    expansion_model=settings.DEFAULT_FAST_MODEL,
  )
def get_interaction_logger(
  event_bus: Annotated[EventBusPort, Depends(get_event_bus_port)],
) -> InteractionLoggerPort:
  return ResilientInteractionLogger(event_bus=event_bus)
def get_retrieval_pipeline(
  provider: Annotated[LLMProvider, Depends(get_llm_provider)],
  search_engine: Annotated[SearchEnginePort, Depends(get_search_engine)],
  cache: Annotated[CachePort, Depends(get_cache_port)],
  expander: Annotated[QueryExpander, Depends(get_query_expander)],
  reranker: Annotated[RerankerPort, Depends(get_reranker)],
  token_manager: Annotated[TokenManagerPort, Depends(get_token_manager)],
  prompt_manager: Annotated[PromptManager, Depends(get_prompt_manager)],
  runner: Annotated[BackgroundRunner, Depends(get_background_runner)],
  singleflight: Annotated[AsyncSingleFlight, Depends(get_singleflight)],
) -> RetrievalPipeline:
  return RetrievalPipeline(
    llm_provider=provider,
    search_engine=search_engine,
    cache=cache,
    expander=expander,
    reranker=reranker,
    token_manager=token_manager,
    prompt_manager=prompt_manager,
    background_runner=runner,
    singleflight=singleflight,
    embedding_model=settings.EMBEDDING_MODEL,
    output_reserve=settings.MODEL_OUTPUT_RESERVE,
  )
def get_adaptive_router(
  provider: Annotated[LLMProvider, Depends(get_llm_provider)],
  runner: Annotated[BackgroundRunner, Depends(get_background_runner)],
  semantic_cache: Annotated[SemanticCachePort, Depends(get_semantic_cache_service)],
  classifier: Annotated[SemanticClassifier, Depends(get_semantic_classifier)],
  retrieval_pipeline: Annotated[RetrievalPipeline, Depends(get_retrieval_pipeline)],
  budget_manager: Annotated[BudgetManager, Depends(get_budget_manager)],
  token_manager: Annotated[TokenManagerPort, Depends(get_token_manager)],
  cost_calculator: Annotated[CostCalculatorPort, Depends(get_cost_calculator)],
  security_service: Annotated[SecurityPort, Depends(get_security_service)],
  interaction_logger: Annotated[
    InteractionLoggerPort, Depends(get_interaction_logger)
  ],
  singleflight: Annotated[AsyncSingleFlight, Depends(get_singleflight)],
  dspy_router_policy: Annotated[
    AdaptiveRouterDSPyPolicy | None, Depends(get_adaptive_router_dspy_policy)
  ],
) -> AdaptiveRouter:
  return AdaptiveRouter(
    llm_provider=provider,
    background_runner=runner,
    semantic_cache=semantic_cache,
    classifier=classifier,
    retrieval_pipeline=retrieval_pipeline,
    budget_manager=budget_manager,
    token_manager=token_manager,
    cost_calculator=cost_calculator,
    security_service=security_service,
    interaction_logger=interaction_logger,
    singleflight=singleflight,
    dspy_router_policy=dspy_router_policy,
    fast_model=settings.DEFAULT_FAST_MODEL,
    smart_model=settings.DEFAULT_SMART_MODEL,
    embedding_model=settings.EMBEDDING_MODEL,
    output_reserve=settings.MODEL_OUTPUT_RESERVE,
  )
def get_system_monitor(
  db: Annotated[DatabasePort, Depends(get_database_port)],
  cache: Annotated[CachePort, Depends(get_cache_port)],
  queue: Annotated[type[QueueManager], Depends(get_queue_manager)],
  reranker: Annotated[RerankerPort, Depends(get_reranker)],
) -> HealthService:
  return HealthService(db=db, cache=cache, queue=queue, reranker=reranker)
def get_pii_detector_port() -> IPiiDetectorPort:
  global _pii_detector_cache
  if settings.TESTING or settings.MOCK_LLM:
    return NoOpPiiDetectorAdapter()
  snap = runtime_tunable_config.get_runtime_tunable_snapshot()
  key = (snap.dlp_model_path, snap.dlp_max_context)
  with _pii_detector_lock:
    if _pii_detector_cache is not None and _pii_detector_cache[0] == key:
      return _pii_detector_cache[1]
    port: IPiiDetectorPort = SemanticKernelPiiDetectorAdapter(
      model_path=key[0],
      n_ctx=key[1],
    )
    _pii_detector_cache = (key, port)
    return port
def get_sanitize_prompt_use_case(
  detector: Annotated[IPiiDetectorPort, Depends(get_pii_detector_port)],
) -> SanitizePromptUseCase:
  return SanitizePromptUseCase(detector=detector)
RouterDependency = Annotated[AdaptiveRouter, Depends(get_adaptive_router)]
DbDependency = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)]
```

```py
import os
import structlog
logger = structlog.get_logger()
def get_secret(key: str, default: str = "") -> str:  # pragma: no mutate
  value = os.getenv(key, default)
  if not value:
    logger.warning(
      "secret_missing",
      key=key,
      hint="Consider using a managed secret store",
    )
  return value
```

### `src/app/security/pii_presidio_singleton.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import threading
from typing import Any
import structlog
logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)
_analyzer_lock = threading.Lock()
_presidio_analyzer: Any | None = None
_PRESIDIO_FAILED = False
def _create_presidio_analyzer_engine() -> Any:
  from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
  from presidio_analyzer.nlp_engine import NlpEngineProvider
  _spacy_non_pii_labels = [
    "CARDINAL",
    "ORDINAL",
    "QUANTITY",
    "PERCENT",
    "FAC",
    "PRODUCT",
    "EVENT",
    "WORK_OF_ART",
    "LAW",
    "LANGUAGE",
  ]
  nlp_configuration = {
    "nlp_engine_name": "spacy",
    "models": [{"lang_code": "en", "model_name": "en_core_web_sm"}],
    "ner_model_configuration": {
      "labels_to_ignore": _spacy_non_pii_labels,
    },
  }
  provider = NlpEngineProvider(nlp_configuration=nlp_configuration)
  nlp_engine = provider.create_engine()
  return AnalyzerEngine(
    nlp_engine=nlp_engine,
    supported_languages=["en"],
  )
def _get_presidio_analyzer() -> Any | None:
  global _presidio_analyzer, _PRESIDIO_FAILED  # pragma: no mutate
  if _PRESIDIO_FAILED:  # pragma: no mutate
    return None  # pragma: no mutate
  if _presidio_analyzer is not None:  # pragma: no mutate
    return _presidio_analyzer  # pragma: no mutate
  with _analyzer_lock:  # pragma: no mutate
    if _presidio_analyzer is not None:  # pragma: no mutate
      return _presidio_analyzer  # pragma: no mutate
    try:
      _presidio_analyzer = _create_presidio_analyzer_engine()  # pragma: no mutate
      logger.info("presidio_analyzer_initialized")  # pragma: no mutate
    except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
      _PRESIDIO_FAILED = True  # pragma: no mutate
      logger.critical(  # pragma: no mutate
        "presidio_analyzer_unavailable",
        error=str(exc),
        action_required=(
          "Fix NLP dependencies immediately to prevent PII leaks "
          "to external LLMs."
        ),
      )
      return None  # pragma: no mutate
  return _presidio_analyzer  # pragma: no mutate
```

### `src/app/security/pii_tokenizer.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import re
import structlog
from app.security.pii_presidio_singleton import _get_presidio_analyzer
logger = structlog.get_logger()
_DEFAULT_ENTITY_KIND = "ENTITY"
_TOKEN_FORMAT = "<{kind}_{n}>"  # noqa: S105  # nosec B105
_PRESIDIO_LANGUAGE = "en"
def _entity_kind(entity_type: str) -> str:
  safe = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", entity_type).strip("_").upper()
  assert isinstance(safe, str), f"safe must be str, got {type(safe)}"
  return safe or _DEFAULT_ENTITY_KIND
class PIITokenizerService:
  _EMAIL_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b[a-zA-Z0-9._%+-]+(@| AT )[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+(\.| DOT )[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"
  )
  _PHONE_RE = re.compile(r"\+?1?\s*\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}")
  _SSN_RE = re.compile(r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b")
  _CARD_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:\d[ -]*?){13,19}\b")
  def __init__(self, *, prefer_presidio: bool = True) -> None:
    self._mapping: dict[str, str] = {}
    self._reverse_mapping: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    self._counters: dict[str, int] = {}
    self._prefer_presidio = prefer_presidio
  def _remember_token(self, token: str, original: str) -> str:
    self._mapping[token] = original
    self._reverse_mapping.setdefault(original, []).append(token)
    return token
  def _token_for_original(self, kind: str, original: str, *, seen: set[str]) -> str:
    if original not in seen:
      existing = self._reverse_mapping.get(original)
      if existing:
        token = existing[0]
        seen.add(original)
        return token
    token = self._next_token(kind)
    seen.add(original)
    self._mapping[token] = original
    self._reverse_mapping.setdefault(original, []).append(token)
    return token
  def _next_token(self, kind: str) -> str:
    n = self._counters.get(kind, 0) + 1
    assert n > 0, f"n must be positive, got {n}"
    self._counters[kind] = n
    token = _TOKEN_FORMAT.format(kind=kind, n=n)
    assert isinstance(token, str), f"token must be str, got {type(token)}"
    return token
  def _apply_regex_pass(self, text: str) -> str:
    result = text
    seen_in_call: set[str] = set()
    for pattern, kind in [
      (self._EMAIL_RE, "EMAIL"),
      (self._PHONE_RE, "PHONE"),
      (self._SSN_RE, "SSN"),
      (self._CARD_RE, "CARD"),
    ]:
      def _replace(match: re.Match[str], *, kind: str = kind) -> str:
        original = match.group(0)
        return self._token_for_original(kind, original, seen=seen_in_call)
      result = pattern.sub(_replace, result)
    return result
  def _tokenize_presidio(self, text: str) -> str:
    analyzer = _get_presidio_analyzer()
    if analyzer is None:
      return text
    try:
      results = analyzer.analyze(text=text, language=_PRESIDIO_LANGUAGE)
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.warning("presidio_analyze_failed", error=str(exc))
      return text
    result = text
    seen_in_call: set[str] = set()
    for r in sorted(results, key=lambda x: x.start, reverse=True):
      start, end = int(r.start), int(r.end)
      if start < 0 or end > len(result) or start >= end:
        continue
      original = result[start:end]
      if not original.strip():
        continue
      kind = _entity_kind(str(r.entity_type))
      assert len(original) > 0, "original must not be empty"
      token = self._token_for_original(kind, original, seen=seen_in_call)
      result = result[:start] + token + result[end:]
    return result
  def tokenize(self, text: str) -> str:
    if not text or not text.strip():
      return text
    text = self._apply_regex_pass(text)
    if self._prefer_presidio:
      text = self._tokenize_presidio(text)
    return self._apply_regex_pass(text)
  def detokenize(self, text: str) -> str:
    result = text
    for token, original in self._mapping.items():
      result = result.replace(token, original)
    return result
  def clear_mapping(self) -> None:
    self._mapping.clear()
    self._reverse_mapping.clear()
    self._counters.clear()
```

### `src/app/security/feedback_privacy.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import base64
import binascii
from typing import Any
import structlog
from cryptography.fernet import InvalidToken
from sqlalchemy.types import String, TypeDecorator
from app.security.encryption import decrypt_bytes, encrypt_bytes, hmac_sha256_hex
logger = structlog.get_logger()
_FERNET_MIN_B64_LEN = 80
_FERNET_MIN_PAYLOAD_LEN = 57
_FERNET_VERSION_BYTE = 0x80
_PADDING_DIVISOR = 4
def _looks_like_fernet_ciphertext(value: str) -> bool:
  if len(value) < _FERNET_MIN_B64_LEN:
    return False
  try:
    raw_ascii = value.encode("ascii")
  except UnicodeEncodeError:
    return False
  pad = (-len(raw_ascii)) % _PADDING_DIVISOR
  to_decode = raw_ascii + (b"=" * pad if pad else b"")
  try:
    raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(to_decode)
  except binascii.Error:
    return False
  if len(raw) < _FERNET_MIN_PAYLOAD_LEN:
    return False
  assert len(raw) > 0, "raw must not be empty"
  return raw[0] == _FERNET_VERSION_BYTE
def looks_like_encrypted_feedback_text(value: str | None) -> bool:
  if value is None:
    return False
  return _looks_like_fernet_ciphertext(value)
def encrypt_feedback_text(value: str | None) -> str | None:
  if value is None:
    return None
  return encrypt_bytes(value.encode("utf-8")).decode("ascii")  # pragma: no mutate
def decrypt_feedback_text(value: str | None) -> str | None:
  if value is None:
    return None
  if not _looks_like_fernet_ciphertext(value):
    return value
  try:
    return decrypt_bytes(value.encode("ascii")).decode("utf-8")  # pragma: no mutate
  except (InvalidToken, binascii.Error, UnicodeDecodeError, UnicodeEncodeError):
    logger.warning(
      "feedback_log_decrypt_failed",
      fallback="returning_raw_value",
    )
    return value
def fingerprint_feedback_text(value: str | None, *, context: str) -> str | None:
  if value is None:
    return None
  return hmac_sha256_hex(value, context=context)  # pragma: no mutate
class EncryptedString(TypeDecorator[str | None]):
  impl = String
  cache_ok = True
  def process_bind_param(
    self, value: str | None, _dialect: Any
  ) -> str | None:  # pragma: no mutate
    return encrypt_feedback_text(value)  # pragma: no mutate
  def process_result_value(
    self, value: str | None, _dialect: Any
  ) -> str | None:  # pragma: no mutate
    return decrypt_feedback_text(value)  # pragma: no mutate
```

### `src/app/security/billing_privacy.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import binascii
from decimal import Decimal, InvalidOperation
from typing import Any
import structlog
from cryptography.fernet import InvalidToken
from sqlalchemy.types import Text, TypeDecorator
from app.security.encryption import decrypt_bytes, encrypt_bytes
logger = structlog.get_logger()
def _encrypt(value: str) -> str:
  return encrypt_bytes(value.encode("utf-8")).decode("ascii")
def _decrypt(value: str) -> str | None:
  try:
    return decrypt_bytes(value.encode("ascii")).decode("utf-8")
  except (
    InvalidToken,
    binascii.Error,
    UnicodeDecodeError,
    UnicodeEncodeError,
  ) as exc:
    logger.warning(
      "billing_ale_decrypt_failed",
      error_type=type(exc).__name__,
      fallback="returning_raw_value",
    )
    return value
class EncryptedDecimal(TypeDecorator[Decimal | None]):
  impl = Text
  cache_ok = True
  def process_bind_param(self, value: Decimal | None, dialect: Any) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
      return None
    return _encrypt(str(value))
  def process_result_value(self, value: str | None, dialect: Any) -> Decimal | None:
    if value is None:
      return None
    plaintext = _decrypt(value)
    if plaintext is None:
      return None
    try:
      return Decimal(plaintext)
    except InvalidOperation:
      logger.error(
        "billing_ale_decimal_parse_failed",
        raw=plaintext[:40],
      )
      return None
class EncryptedBillingString(TypeDecorator[str | None]):
  impl = Text
  cache_ok = True
  def process_bind_param(self, value: str | None, dialect: Any) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
      return None
    return _encrypt(value)
  def process_result_value(self, value: str | None, dialect: Any) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
      return None
    return _decrypt(value)
```

### `src/app/security/jwt_auth.py`
```py
from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
from typing import Any, cast
import jwt
import structlog
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, Request, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
from app.config import settings
logger = structlog.get_logger()
def _resolve_secret_key() -> str:
  if settings.SECRET_KEY:
    return settings.SECRET_KEY.get_secret_value()
  if settings.DEBUG:
    logger.warning(
      "jwt_using_dev_key",
      hint="Set SECRET_KEY for production deployments",
    )
    return "dev-only-unsafe-key-not-for-production"
  raise RuntimeError(
    "SECRET_KEY is not configured. Set the SECRET_KEY environment variable."
  )
SECRET_KEY: str = _resolve_secret_key()
ALGORITHM = "HS256"
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 30
oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(
  tokenUrl=f"{settings.API_V1_STR}/auth/token",
  scopes={
    "read": "Read data",
    "write": "Modify data",
    "admin": "Admin access",
  },
)
def create_access_token(
  data: dict[str, Any],
  expires_delta: timedelta | None = None,
) -> str:
  to_encode = data.copy()
  expire = datetime.now(UTC) + (
    expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)
  )
  to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
  return jwt.encode(
    to_encode,
    SECRET_KEY,
    algorithm=ALGORITHM,
  )
async def _optional_bearer_token(request: Request) -> str | None:
  try:
    return await oauth2_scheme(request)
  except HTTPException:
    return None
def get_current_user_scopes(
  token: str | None = Depends(_optional_bearer_token),
) -> list[str]:
  credentials_exception = HTTPException(
    status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
    detail="Could not validate credentials",
    headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
  )
  if token is None:
    raise credentials_exception
  if settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
    return ["admin", "read", "write"]
  try:
    payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
    if payload.get("sub") == "schemathesis-contract-user":
      return ["admin", "read", "write"]
    scopes_payload = payload.get("scopes", [])
    if isinstance(scopes_payload, str):
      return scopes_payload.split()
    return cast(list[str], scopes_payload)
  except jwt.PyJWTError as e:
    raise credentials_exception from e
```

### `src/app/security/pii_scrubber.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import asyncio
import os
import re
import threading
from typing import Any, ClassVar
import structlog
from app.domain.exceptions import PIIScrubbingError
from app.security.pii_tokenizer import PIITokenizerService
logger = structlog.get_logger()
__all__ = ["PIIScrubber", "PIITokenizerService"]
def _pii_regex_only_mode() -> bool:  # pragma: no mutate
  return os.environ.get(
    "PII_SCRUBBER_REGEX_ONLY", ""
  ).strip().lower() in (  # pragma: no mutate
    "1",  # pragma: no mutate
    "true",  # pragma: no mutate
    "yes",  # pragma: no mutate
  )  # pragma: no mutate
class PIIScrubber:
  _nlp: ClassVar[Any | None] = None
  _init_lock: ClassVar[threading.Lock] = threading.Lock()
  _PATTERNS: ClassVar[list[tuple[re.Pattern[str], str]]] = [
    (
      re.compile(
        r"\b[a-zA-Z0-9._%+-]+(@| AT )[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+(\.| DOT )[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b",
        re.IGNORECASE,
      ),
      "[EMAIL]",
    ),
    (re.compile(r"\+?1?\s*\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}"), "[PHONE]"),
    (re.compile(r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b"), "[SSN]"),
    (re.compile(r"\b(?:\d[ -]*?){13,16}\b"), "[CARD]"),
  ]
  @classmethod
  def _get_nlp(cls) -> Any:
    if cls._nlp is not None:
      return cls._nlp
    with cls._init_lock:
      if cls._nlp is not None:
        return cls._nlp
      try:
        import spacy
        cls._nlp = spacy.load(
          "en_core_web_sm",
          disable=["parser", "tagger", "lemmatizer"],
        )
        logger.info("Spacy model 'en_core_web_sm' loaded successfully.")
      except (ImportError, OSError) as e:
        logger.critical("spacy_model_load_failed", error=str(e))
        raise PIIScrubbingError("NLP Engine unavailable") from e
    return cls._nlp
  @classmethod
  async def warmup(cls) -> None:
    try:
      await asyncio.to_thread(cls._get_nlp)
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error("pii_scrubber_warmup_failed", error=str(e))
  @classmethod
  def _scrub_sync(cls, text: str, strict: bool = True) -> str:
    redacted = text
    for pattern, replacement in cls._PATTERNS:
      redacted = pattern.sub(replacement, redacted)  # pragma: no mutate
    if _pii_regex_only_mode():
      if redacted != text:
        logger.info("PII detected and scrubbed", strict_mode=strict)
      return redacted  # pragma: no mutate
    try:
      nlp = cls._get_nlp()  # pragma: no mutate
      doc = nlp(redacted)  # pragma: no mutate
      for ent in reversed(doc.ents):  # pragma: no mutate
        if ent.label_ in ["PERSON", "GPE", "ORG"]:
          redacted = (  # pragma: no mutate
            redacted[: ent.start_char]
            + f"[{ent.label_}]"
            + redacted[ent.end_char :]
          )
    except Exception as e:
      logger.error(
        "nlp_scrubbing_failed",
        error=str(e),
        strict_mode=strict,
      )
      if strict:
        raise PIIScrubbingError(
          "Security mechanism failed. Request blocked to prevent data leak."
        ) from e
      redacted += " [WARNING: PII SCAN INCOMPLETE]"
    if redacted != text:
      logger.info("PII detected and scrubbed", strict_mode=strict)
    return redacted
  @classmethod
  async def scrub_async(cls, text: str, strict: bool = True) -> str:
    if not text:
      return text
    return await asyncio.to_thread(cls._scrub_sync, text, strict)
  @classmethod
  def scrub(cls, text: str, strict: bool = True) -> str:
    return cls._scrub_sync(text, strict)
```

### `src/app/security/guardrails.py`
```py
import asyncio
import re
from typing import ClassVar
import structlog
logger = structlog.get_logger()
class PromptGuard:
  INJECTION_PATTERNS: ClassVar[list[tuple[str, float]]] = [
    (r"ignore (all|previous) instructions", 1.0),
    (r"system override", 1.0),
    (r"bypass mode", 1.0),
    (r"delete all data", 1.0),
    (r"drop table", 1.0),
    (r"you are now a", 0.8),  # DAN (Do Anything Now) attempts
    (r"act as a", 0.5),  # Potential persona hijacking
    (r"reveal your system prompt", 1.0),
    (r"developer mode", 1.0),
    (r"print previous", 1.0),
    (r"output initialization", 1.0),
    (r"forget everything", 1.0),
  ]
  _COMPILED_PATTERNS: ClassVar[list[tuple[re.Pattern[str], float]]] = [
    (re.compile(p, re.IGNORECASE), score) for p, score in INJECTION_PATTERNS
  ]
  THRESHOLD: float = 0.9
  MAX_PROMPT_LENGTH: ClassVar[int] = 10000  # ~2.5k tokens max to prevent DoS
  @classmethod
  def _validate_sync(cls, prompt: str) -> bool:
    if len(prompt) > cls.MAX_PROMPT_LENGTH:
      logger.warning(
        "Security Guardrail Triggered (Prompt too long)",
        length=len(prompt),
        limit=cls.MAX_PROMPT_LENGTH,
      )
      return False
    risk_score = 0.0
    violations = []
    for pattern, score in cls._COMPILED_PATTERNS:
      if pattern.search(prompt):
        risk_score += score
        violations.append(pattern.pattern)
        if risk_score >= cls.THRESHOLD:
          logger.warning(
            "Security Guardrail Triggered (Fail Fast)",
            risk_score=risk_score,
            violations=violations,
            prompt_snippet=prompt[:50],
          )
          return False
    return True
  @classmethod
  async def validate_async(cls, prompt: str) -> bool:
    return await asyncio.to_thread(cls._validate_sync, prompt)
  @classmethod
  def validate(cls, prompt: str) -> bool:
    return cls._validate_sync(prompt)
```

### `src/app/security/oauth2.py`
```py
from typing import Any
import jwt
import structlog
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
from app.config import settings
logger = structlog.get_logger()
oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="/token")
_ALGORITHMS = ["HS256"]
def verify_oauth2_token(token: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
  if not settings.SECRET_KEY:
    raise RuntimeError("SECRET_KEY must be set for token verification")
  secret = settings.SECRET_KEY.get_secret_value()
  payload: dict[str, Any] = jwt.decode(token, secret, algorithms=_ALGORITHMS)
  logger.debug(
    "oauth2_token_verified",
    user_id=payload.get("sub", "unknown"),
  )
  return payload
```

### `src/app/security/rbac.py`
```py
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import SecurityScopes
from app.security.jwt_auth import get_current_user_scopes
class RoleChecker:
  def __init__(self, required_scopes: list[str]) -> None:
    self.required_scopes = set(required_scopes)  # pragma: no mutate
  def __call__(
    self,
    security_scopes: SecurityScopes,
    token_scopes: list[str] = Depends(get_current_user_scopes),
  ) -> bool:
    if not self.required_scopes.issubset(set(token_scopes)):
      raise HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
        detail="Not enough permissions",
        headers={
          "WWW-Authenticate": f'Bearer scope="{security_scopes.scope_str}"'
        },
      )
    return True
admin_required = RoleChecker(["admin", "read", "write"])
reader_required = RoleChecker(["read"])
writer_required = RoleChecker(["write"])
```
