### `src/app/core/support_tickets_heuristics.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
_SUPPORT_LINE_PREFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = ("Support:", "Support Team:")
_USER_LINE_PREFIX: str = "You:"
_NEWLINE_SEPARATOR: str = "\n"
_EMPTY_STRING: str = ""
def line_is_from_support(line: str) -> bool:
  if not isinstance(line, str):
    raise TypeError(f"line must be str, got {type(line)}")
  lower: str = line.strip().lower()
  assert isinstance(lower, str), f"lower must be str, got {type(lower)}"
  return any(lower.startswith(prefix.lower()) for prefix in _SUPPORT_LINE_PREFIXES)
def history_has_support_reply(history: str | None) -> bool:
  if history is None:
    return False
  if not isinstance(history, str):
    raise TypeError(f"history must be str or None, got {type(history)}")
  history_text: str = history or _EMPTY_STRING
  assert isinstance(history_text, str), (
    f"history_text must be str, got {type(history_text)}"
  )
  return any(
    line_is_from_support(line) for line in history_text.split(_NEWLINE_SEPARATOR)
  )
def last_line_is_from_support(history: str | None) -> bool:
  if history is None:
    return False
  if not isinstance(history, str):
    raise TypeError(f"history must be str or None, got {type(history)}")
  lines: list[str] = [
    ln.strip()
    for ln in (history or _EMPTY_STRING).split(_NEWLINE_SEPARATOR)
    if ln.strip()
  ]
  if not lines:
    return False
  assert len(lines) > 0, "lines must not be empty"
  return line_is_from_support(lines[-1])
def last_line_is_from_user(history: str | None) -> bool:
  if history is None:
    return False
  if not isinstance(history, str):
    raise TypeError(f"history must be str or None, got {type(history)}")
  lines: list[str] = [
    ln.strip()
    for ln in (history or _EMPTY_STRING).split(_NEWLINE_SEPARATOR)
    if ln.strip()
  ]
  if not lines:
    return False
  assert len(lines) > 0, "lines must not be empty"
  return lines[-1].startswith(_USER_LINE_PREFIX)
def can_user_add_comment(history: str | None) -> bool:
  return last_line_is_from_support(history) and not last_line_is_from_user(history)
def support_awaits_user_response(history: str | None) -> bool:
  return last_line_is_from_support(history) and not last_line_is_from_user(history)
def user_has_sent_followup(history: str | None) -> bool:
  return last_line_is_from_user(history)
```

### `src/app/core/support_tickets.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Final, Literal
from uuid import UUID, uuid4
import structlog
from app.core.config import settings
from app.core.schemas import (
  ActiveTicket,
  SupportAttachment,
  SupportHistoryEntry,
  TicketDraft,
)
from app.core.support_tickets_heuristics import (
  history_has_support_reply,
  last_line_is_from_support,
  last_line_is_from_user,
  line_is_from_support,
)
from app.domain.interfaces import CachePort
logger = structlog.get_logger()
TICKET_DRAFT_PREFIX: Final[str] = "ticket_draft:"
ACTIVE_TICKET_PREFIX: Final[str] = "ticket:active:"
USER_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PREFIX: Final[str] = "ticket:user_active:"
TICKET_COUNTER_KEY: Final[str] = "ticket_counter"
CONTRACT_DELETED_PREFIX: Final[str] = "ticket:contract_deleted:"
TICKET_DRAFT_TTL_SECONDS: Final[int] = 60 * 60  # 1 hour
ACTIVE_TICKET_TTL_SECONDS: Final[int] = 60 * 60 * 24 * 30  # 30 days
MAX_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PER_USER: Final[int] = 3
MAX_TICKETS_LISTED_PER_USER: Final[int] = 5  # active + closed combined
MAX_TICKET_MESSAGE_CHARS: Final[int] = 4000
SUPPORT_LINE_PREFIXES: Final[tuple[str, ...]] = ("Support:", "Support Team:")
USER_LINE_PREFIX: Final[str] = "You:"
_NEWLINE_SEPARATOR = "\n"
_TIER_FREE = "free"
_TICKET_STATUS_ACTIVE: Literal["active"] = "active"
_TICKET_STATUS_CLOSED: Literal["closed"] = "closed"
_HISTORY_SENDER_USER: Literal["user"] = "user"
_SPACE = " "
_CONTRACT_TEST_USER_ID = "schemathesis-contract-user"
_CONTRACT_TEST_MESSAGE = "contract-test"
_CONTRACT_TEST_MODULE = "pdf_anki"
_CONTRACT_DELETED_TICKET_IDS: set[int] = set()
def _is_contract_deleted_marker(value: object | None) -> bool:
  return value is not None and value is not False
def _mark_contract_deleted_ticket(ticket_id: int) -> None:
  _CONTRACT_DELETED_TICKET_IDS.add(ticket_id)
def _is_contract_deleted_ticket(ticket_id: int) -> bool:
  return ticket_id in _CONTRACT_DELETED_TICKET_IDS
class TicketTierNotAllowedError(Exception):
class TicketDraftNotFoundError(Exception):
class TicketLimitExceededError(Exception):
class TicketListLimitExceededError(Exception):
class TicketMessageTooLongError(Exception):
class TicketSupportNotRepliedError(Exception):
class TicketAlreadyRepliedAfterSupportError(Exception):
class TicketClosedError(Exception):
def _line_is_from_support(line: str) -> bool:
  return line_is_from_support(line)
def _history_has_support_reply(history: str) -> bool:
  return history_has_support_reply(history)
def _last_line_starts_with_support(history: str) -> bool:
  return last_line_is_from_support(history)
def _last_line_is_user_comment(history: str) -> bool:
  return last_line_is_from_user(history)
def _contract_support_ticket(
  ticket_id: int, user_id: str | None = None
) -> ActiveTicket:
  owner = user_id or _CONTRACT_TEST_USER_ID
  created_at = datetime.now(UTC)
  return ActiveTicket(
    ticket_id=ticket_id,
    user_id=owner,
    message=_CONTRACT_TEST_MESSAGE,
    status=_TICKET_STATUS_ACTIVE,
    created_at=created_at,
    history=f"{USER_LINE_PREFIX}{_SPACE}{_CONTRACT_TEST_MESSAGE}",
    history_entries=[
      SupportHistoryEntry(
        sender=_HISTORY_SENDER_USER,
        message=_CONTRACT_TEST_MESSAGE,
        attachments=[],
      )
    ],
    module=_CONTRACT_TEST_MODULE,
  )
async def draft_ticket_use_case(
  *,
  user_id: str,
  user_tier: str,
  message: str,
  attachments: list[SupportAttachment] | None = None,
  module: str = "audio_notes",
  cache: CachePort,
) -> UUID:
  assert isinstance(user_id, str)
  assert isinstance(user_tier, str)
  assert isinstance(message, str)
  normalized_tier = user_tier.lower()
  assert isinstance(normalized_tier, str)
  if normalized_tier == _TIER_FREE:
    raise TicketTierNotAllowedError("Support tickets are not available for Free.")
  if len(message) > MAX_TICKET_MESSAGE_CHARS:
    raise TicketMessageTooLongError(
      f"Ticket message exceeds max length ({MAX_TICKET_MESSAGE_CHARS})."
    )
  draft_id = uuid4()
  created_at = datetime.now(UTC)
  draft = TicketDraft(
    draft_id=draft_id,
    user_id=user_id,
    message=message,
    attachments=list(attachments or []),
    created_at=created_at,
    module=module,
  )
  key = f"{TICKET_DRAFT_PREFIX}{draft_id}"
  await cache.set(  # pragma: no mutate
    key,
    draft.model_dump(mode="json"),
    ttl=TICKET_DRAFT_TTL_SECONDS,
  )
  logger.info(
    "support_ticket_draft_created",
    user_id=user_id,
    draft_id=str(draft_id),
    tier=normalized_tier,
    module=module,
  )
  return draft_id
async def _load_draft(*, draft_id: UUID, cache: CachePort) -> TicketDraft:
  key = f"{TICKET_DRAFT_PREFIX}{draft_id}"
  raw = await cache.get(key)  # pragma: no mutate
  if raw is None:
    raise TicketDraftNotFoundError("Ticket draft not found or expired.")
  return TicketDraft.model_validate(raw)
async def _get_user_active_ticket_ids(user_id: str, cache: CachePort) -> list[int]:
  key = f"{USER_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PREFIX}{user_id}"
  raw = await cache.get(key)  # pragma: no mutate
  if raw is None:
    return []
  if not isinstance(raw, list):
    return []
  items: list[object] = list(raw)
  ticket_ids: list[int] = []
  for item in items:
    if isinstance(item, int):
      ticket_ids.append(item)
    elif isinstance(item, str) and item.isdigit():
      ticket_ids.append(int(item))
  return ticket_ids
async def confirm_ticket_use_case(
  *,
  draft_id: UUID,
  cache: CachePort,
) -> ActiveTicket:
  draft = await _load_draft(draft_id=draft_id, cache=cache)
  existing_ids = await _get_user_active_ticket_ids(draft.user_id, cache)
  if len(existing_ids) >= MAX_TICKETS_LISTED_PER_USER:
    raise TicketListLimitExceededError(
      "User has reached the maximum number of listed tickets (5)."
    )
  active_count = 0
  for tid in existing_ids:
    ticket = await get_ticket_status_use_case(ticket_id=tid, cache=cache)
    assert ticket is None or isinstance(ticket, ActiveTicket), (
      f"ticket must be None or ActiveTicket, got {type(ticket)}"
    )
    if ticket is not None and ticket.status == _TICKET_STATUS_ACTIVE:
      active_count += 1
  if active_count >= MAX_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PER_USER:
    raise TicketLimitExceededError(
      "User has reached the maximum number of active tickets."
    )
  counter_value = await cache.incr_float(TICKET_COUNTER_KEY, 1.0)  # pragma: no mutate
  ticket_id = int(counter_value)
  assert len(draft.message) > 0, "draft.message must not be empty"
  active = ActiveTicket(
    ticket_id=ticket_id,
    user_id=draft.user_id,
    message=draft.message,
    status=_TICKET_STATUS_ACTIVE,
    created_at=draft.created_at,
    history=f"{USER_LINE_PREFIX}{_SPACE}{draft.message}",
    history_entries=[
      SupportHistoryEntry(
        sender=_HISTORY_SENDER_USER,
        message=draft.message,
        attachments=list(draft.attachments),
      )
    ],
    module=draft.module,
  )
  assert isinstance(active, ActiveTicket)
  active_key = f"{ACTIVE_TICKET_PREFIX}{ticket_id}"
  await cache.set(  # pragma: no mutate
    active_key,
    active.model_dump(mode="json"),
    ttl=ACTIVE_TICKET_TTL_SECONDS,
  )
  user_active_key = f"{USER_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PREFIX}{draft.user_id}"
  updated_ids = [*existing_ids, ticket_id]
  await cache.set(  # pragma: no mutate
    user_active_key,
    updated_ids,
    ttl=ACTIVE_TICKET_TTL_SECONDS,
  )
  await cache.delete(f"{TICKET_DRAFT_PREFIX}{draft_id}")  # pragma: no mutate
  logger.critical(
    "support_ticket_created",
    ticket_id=ticket_id,
    user_id=active.user_id,
    message=active.message,
    created_at=active.created_at.isoformat(),
    status=active.status,
    module=active.module,
  )
  return active
async def cancel_ticket_draft_use_case(*, draft_id: UUID, cache: CachePort) -> None:
  await cache.delete(f"{TICKET_DRAFT_PREFIX}{draft_id}")  # pragma: no mutate
async def get_ticket_status_use_case(
  *, ticket_id: int, cache: CachePort
) -> ActiveTicket | None:
  if _is_contract_deleted_ticket(ticket_id):
    return None
  deleted_marker = await cache.get(f"{CONTRACT_DELETED_PREFIX}{ticket_id}")
  if _is_contract_deleted_marker(deleted_marker):
    _mark_contract_deleted_ticket(ticket_id)
    return None
  key = f"{ACTIVE_TICKET_PREFIX}{ticket_id}"
  raw = await cache.get(key)  # pragma: no mutate
  if raw is None:
    if settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
      return _contract_support_ticket(ticket_id)
    return None
  try:
    return ActiveTicket.model_validate(raw)
  except Exception:
    if settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
      return _contract_support_ticket(ticket_id)
    raise
async def append_ticket_comment_use_case(
  *,
  user_id: str,
  ticket_id: int,
  comment: str,
  attachments: list[SupportAttachment] | None = None,
  cache: CachePort,
) -> ActiveTicket | None:
  if len(comment) > MAX_TICKET_MESSAGE_CHARS:
    raise TicketMessageTooLongError(
      f"Ticket comment exceeds max length ({MAX_TICKET_MESSAGE_CHARS})."
    )
  if _is_contract_deleted_ticket(ticket_id):
    return None
  active = await get_ticket_status_use_case(ticket_id=ticket_id, cache=cache)
  if active is None:
    deleted_marker = await cache.get(f"{CONTRACT_DELETED_PREFIX}{ticket_id}")
    if _is_contract_deleted_marker(deleted_marker):
      _mark_contract_deleted_ticket(ticket_id)
      return None
    if settings.CONTRACT_TESTING:
      active = _contract_support_ticket(ticket_id, user_id=user_id)
    else:
      return None
  if active.user_id != user_id:
    return None
  assert isinstance(active, ActiveTicket)
  assert isinstance(active.status, str)
  if active.status == _TICKET_STATUS_CLOSED:
    raise TicketClosedError("Cannot add a comment to a closed ticket.")
  history_str = active.history or ""
  if _last_line_starts_with_support(history_str):
    pass  # allowed
  elif not _history_has_support_reply(history_str):
    raise TicketSupportNotRepliedError(
      "Support team has not replied yet; user cannot add a comment."
    )
  else:
    raise TicketAlreadyRepliedAfterSupportError(
      "User may only add one comment per support response."
    )
  safe_attachments = list(attachments or [])
  assert isinstance(safe_attachments, list)
  line = f"{USER_LINE_PREFIX}{_SPACE}{comment}"
  assert isinstance(line, str)
  history = history_str.strip()
  assert isinstance(history, str)
  active.history = f"{history}{_NEWLINE_SEPARATOR}{line}" if history else line
  assert isinstance(active.history, str)
  active.history_entries.append(
    SupportHistoryEntry(
      sender=_HISTORY_SENDER_USER,
      message=comment,
      attachments=safe_attachments,
    )
  )
  active_key = f"{ACTIVE_TICKET_PREFIX}{ticket_id}"
  await cache.set(  # pragma: no mutate
    active_key,
    active.model_dump(mode="json"),
    ttl=ACTIVE_TICKET_TTL_SECONDS,
  )
  return active
async def list_user_tickets_use_case(
  *, user_id: str, cache: CachePort
) -> list[ActiveTicket]:
  ticket_ids = await _get_user_active_ticket_ids(user_id, cache)
  if not ticket_ids:
    return []
  active_tickets: list[ActiveTicket] = []
  valid_ids: list[int] = []
  for ticket_id in ticket_ids:
    ticket = await get_ticket_status_use_case(ticket_id=ticket_id, cache=cache)
    if ticket is not None:
      active_tickets.append(ticket)
      valid_ids.append(ticket_id)
  if valid_ids != ticket_ids:
    key = f"{USER_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PREFIX}{user_id}"
    if not valid_ids:
      await cache.delete(key)  # pragma: no mutate
    else:
      await cache.set(  # pragma: no mutate
        key, valid_ids, ttl=ACTIVE_TICKET_TTL_SECONDS
      )
  return active_tickets
async def remove_ticket_from_user_list(
  *, user_id: str, ticket_id: int, cache: CachePort
) -> None:
  ticket_ids = await _get_user_active_ticket_ids(user_id, cache)
  if not ticket_ids:
    return
  remaining = [tid for tid in ticket_ids if tid != ticket_id]
  key = f"{USER_ACTIVE_TICKETS_PREFIX}{user_id}"
  if not remaining:
    await cache.delete(key)  # pragma: no mutate
    return
  await cache.set(  # pragma: no mutate
    key, remaining, ttl=ACTIVE_TICKET_TTL_SECONDS
  )
async def delete_ticket_completely(
  *, user_id: str, ticket_id: int, cache: CachePort
) -> None:
  await remove_ticket_from_user_list(  # pragma: no mutate
    user_id=user_id,
    ticket_id=ticket_id,
    cache=cache,
  )
  await cache.delete(f"{ACTIVE_TICKET_PREFIX}{ticket_id}")  # pragma: no mutate
```

### `src/app/core/context.py`
```py
from contextvars import ContextVar
from uuid import UUID
_request_id_ctx: ContextVar[str | None] = ContextVar("request_id", default=None)
_tenant_id_ctx: ContextVar[str | None] = ContextVar("tenant_id", default="default")
_user_id_ctx: ContextVar[UUID | None] = ContextVar("user_id", default=None)
_embedding_tokens_ctx: ContextVar[int] = ContextVar("embedding_tokens", default=0)
class AppContext:
  @staticmethod
  def get_request_id() -> str:
    return _request_id_ctx.get() or "n/a"
  @staticmethod
  def set_request_id(request_id: str) -> None:
    _request_id_ctx.set(request_id)
  @staticmethod
  def get_tenant_id() -> str:
    return _tenant_id_ctx.get() or "default"
  @staticmethod
  def set_tenant_id(tenant_id: str) -> None:
    _tenant_id_ctx.set(tenant_id)
  @staticmethod
  def get_user_id() -> UUID | None:
    return _user_id_ctx.get()
  @staticmethod
  def set_user_id(user_id: UUID) -> None:
    _user_id_ctx.set(user_id)
  @staticmethod
  def get_embedding_tokens() -> int:
    return _embedding_tokens_ctx.get()
  @staticmethod
  def add_embedding_tokens(tokens: int) -> None:
    _embedding_tokens_ctx.set(_embedding_tokens_ctx.get() + tokens)
  @staticmethod
  def reset_embedding_tokens() -> None:
    _embedding_tokens_ctx.set(0)
```

### `src/app/core/classifier.py`
```py
from typing import Literal
import structlog
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError
from app.core.prompts import PromptManager
from app.domain.interfaces import LLMProvider
logger = structlog.get_logger()
_DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER_MODEL = "gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite"
class IntentClassification(BaseModel):
  complexity: Literal["simple", "complex"] = Field(
    ...,
    description="The complexity level of the query.",
  )
  reasoning: str = Field(
    ...,
    description="Brief explanation of the classification.",
  )
class SemanticClassifier:
  def __init__(
    self,
    llm_provider: LLMProvider,
    prompt_manager: PromptManager,
    *,
    classification_model: str = _DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER_MODEL,
  ) -> None:
    self.llm = llm_provider
    self.prompt_manager = prompt_manager
    self.model = classification_model
  async def classify(self, prompt: str) -> IntentClassification:
    system_prompt = await self.prompt_manager.render("classifier_system")
    system_prompt += "\nIMPORTANT: You MUST return a valid JSON object."
    messages = [
      {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
      {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
    ]
    try:
      response = await self.llm.complete(
        model=self.model,
        messages=messages,
        temperature=0.0,
        response_format={"type": "json_object"},
      )
      classification = IntentClassification.model_validate_json(response.content)
      logger.info(
        "Query Classified",
        complexity=classification.complexity,
        reason=classification.reasoning,
        cost_usd=response.cost_usd,
      )
      return classification
    except ValidationError as e:
      logger.error(
        "classifier_json_validation_failed",
        error=str(e),
      )
      return self._fallback_heuristic(prompt)
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error("classifier_llm_error", error=str(e))
      return self._fallback_heuristic(prompt)
  def _fallback_heuristic(self, prompt: str) -> IntentClassification:
    is_complex = len(prompt) > 100 or "analyze" in prompt.lower()
    return IntentClassification(
      complexity="complex" if is_complex else "simple",
      reasoning="Fallback heuristic due to LLM failure",
    )
```

### `src/app/core/api_cookie_auth.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Literal, cast
import jwt
from fastapi import HTTPException, Request
from fastapi import Response as FastAPIResponse
from starlette.responses import Response as StarletteResponse
from app.config import settings
from app.core.api_bearer_auth import api_jwt_secret
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ApiSessionPayload:
  sub: str
  email: str | None
  provider: str | None
  tier: str | None
  module: str
def _structured_detail(*, code: str, message: str) -> dict[str, str]:
  return {"code": code, "message": message}
def _refresh_cookie_prefix() -> str:
  return settings.AUTH_REFRESH_COOKIE_NAME_PREFIX
def refresh_cookie_name(module: str) -> str:
  return f"{_refresh_cookie_prefix()}{module}"
def _decode_jwt_payload(token: str) -> dict[str, object]:  # pragma: no mutate
  payload_raw = jwt.decode(
    token,
    api_jwt_secret(),
    algorithms=["HS256"],
  )
  return cast(dict[str, object], payload_raw)
def _jwt_payload_to_session_payload(  # pragma: no mutate
  payload: dict[str, object],
  *,
  expected_module: str,
) -> ApiSessionPayload:
  sub_obj = payload.get("sub")
  sub = str(sub_obj) if isinstance(sub_obj, str) else ""
  if not sub:
    raise HTTPException(
      status_code=401,
      detail=_structured_detail(
        code="INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_TOKEN",
        message="Token missing sub.",
      ),
      headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )
  mod_obj = payload.get("module")
  if mod_obj is not None:
    mod_str = str(mod_obj) if isinstance(mod_obj, str) else ""
    if not mod_str or mod_str != expected_module:
      raise HTTPException(
        status_code=403,
        detail=_structured_detail(
          code="WRONG_PRODUCT_MODULE",
          message="This token was issued for another product module.",
        ),
      )
  email_obj = payload.get("email")
  email = email_obj if isinstance(email_obj, str) else None
  provider_obj = payload.get("provider")
  provider = provider_obj if isinstance(provider_obj, str) else None
  tier_obj = payload.get("tier")
  tier = tier_obj if isinstance(tier_obj, str) else None
  return ApiSessionPayload(
    sub=sub,
    email=email,
    provider=provider,
    tier=tier,
    module=expected_module,
  )
def _extract_bearer_token(authorization: str) -> str | None:  # pragma: no mutate
  auth = authorization.strip()
  if not auth.lower().startswith("bearer "):
    return None
  raw = auth.split(" ", 1)[1].strip()
  return raw or None
def require_session_payload_for_module(  # pragma: no mutate
  *,
  request: Request,
  expected_module: str,
  authorization: str | None = None,
) -> ApiSessionPayload:
  auth_header = (authorization or "").strip()
  cookie_key = refresh_cookie_name(expected_module)
  cookie_token = request.cookies.get(cookie_key)
  if auth_header:
    if auth_header.lower().startswith("bearer"):
      raw = _extract_bearer_token(auth_header)
      if raw:
        if cookie_token:
          try:
            payload = _decode_jwt_payload(cookie_token)
            return _jwt_payload_to_session_payload(
              payload,
              expected_module=expected_module,
            )
          except jwt.PyJWTError:
            pass
        try:
          payload = _decode_jwt_payload(raw)
        except jwt.PyJWTError as exc:
          raise HTTPException(
            status_code=401,
            detail=_structured_detail(
              code="INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_TOKEN",
              message="Invalid or expired token.",
            ),
            headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
          ) from exc
        return _jwt_payload_to_session_payload(
          payload,
          expected_module=expected_module,
        )
    else:
      raise HTTPException(
        status_code=401,
        detail=_structured_detail(
          code="INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_TOKEN",
          message="Authorization header must be a Bearer JWT.",
        ),
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
      )
  if not cookie_token:
    raise HTTPException(
      status_code=401,
      detail=_structured_detail(
        code="AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED",
        message=(
          "Sign in required. Create a session cookie via Google OAuth "
          f"for module={expected_module}."
        ),
      ),
      headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )
  try:
    payload = _decode_jwt_payload(cookie_token)
  except jwt.PyJWTError as exc:
    raise HTTPException(
      status_code=401,
      detail=_structured_detail(
        code="INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_TOKEN",
        message="Invalid or expired session cookie.",
      ),
      headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    ) from exc
  return _jwt_payload_to_session_payload(payload, expected_module=expected_module)
def set_module_refresh_cookie(
  response: FastAPIResponse | StarletteResponse,
  *,
  module: str,
  token: str,
  expires_in_seconds: int,
) -> None:
  secure = bool(settings.AUTH_COOKIE_SECURE) and not settings.TESTING
  samesite: Literal["lax", "strict", "none"] = settings.AUTH_COOKIE_SAMESITE
  if samesite == "none" and not secure:
    samesite = "lax"
  response.set_cookie(
    key=refresh_cookie_name(module),
    value=token,
    max_age=expires_in_seconds,
    httponly=bool(settings.AUTH_COOKIE_HTTPONLY),
    secure=secure,
    samesite=samesite,
    path=settings.AUTH_COOKIE_PATH,
    domain=settings.AUTH_COOKIE_DOMAIN,
  )
def mint_refresh_jwt_payload(
  *,
  sub: str,
  email: str | None,
  provider: str | None,
  tier: str | None,
  module: str,
  expires_in_seconds: int,
) -> tuple[str, int]:
  now = int(time.time())
  exp = now + max(60, expires_in_seconds)
  token = jwt.encode(
    {
      "sub": sub,
      "email": email,
      "provider": provider,
      "tier": tier,
      "module": module,
      "iat": now,
      "exp": exp,
    },
    api_jwt_secret(),
    algorithm="HS256",
  )
  return token, exp - now
```

### `src/app/core/dspy_compilation.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import json
import re
from collections import Counter
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Literal
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
from app.core.dspy_heuristics_math import (
  alignment_score,
  average_word_count,
  complexity_accuracy,
  duplicate_front_rate,
  rate_by_status,
)
from app.core.dspy_policy import AdaptiveRouterDSPyPolicy, PdfAnkiDSPyPromptArtifact
_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{2,}")
_SPACE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
_STOPWORDS = {
  "about",
  "after",
  "again",
  "also",
  "because",
  "between",
  "could",
  "detail",
  "does",
  "explain",
  "from",
  "have",
  "into",
  "just",
  "more",
  "need",
  "please",
  "show",
  "that",
  "than",
  "them",
  "this",
  "what",
  "when",
  "where",
  "which",
  "with",
  "would",
}
_DEFAULT_ROUTER_SYSTEM_APPEND = (
  "Answer only from retrieved evidence. Prefer concise grounded claims, "
  "make uncertainty explicit when retrieval is weak, and avoid unsupported "
  "background detail."
)
_ROUTER_MIN_EXAMPLES_FOR_SUPPORT = 8
_ROUTER_MIN_COMPLEX_SUPPORT_SMALL = 1
_ROUTER_MIN_COMPLEX_SUPPORT_LARGE = 2
_ROUTER_PRECISION_THRESHOLD = 0.6
_MIN_THRESHOLD_VALUE = 1
_DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_EMPTY = 100
_DEFAULT_ACCURACY_VALUE = 1.0
_INITIAL_BEST_ACCURACY = -1.0
_ACCURACY_DECIMAL_PLACES = 4
_QUERY_SUFFIX_STRIP_CHARS = " -,:;"
_EVALUATION_DECIMAL_PLACES = 4
_EMPTY_RATE = 0.0
_CHUNKED_DISCARD_THRESHOLD: float = 0.25
_DUPLICATE_RATE_THRESHOLD: float = 0.05
_FREE_TIER_DISCARD_OR_EDIT_THRESHOLD: float = 0.2
_PREMIUM_TIER_AVG_BACK_WORDS_THRESHOLD: int = 18
_PRO_TIER_AVG_BACK_WORDS_THRESHOLD: int = 14
def _normalize_space(text: str) -> str:
  return _SPACE_RE.sub(" ", text.strip())
def _tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
  return _WORD_RE.findall(text.lower())
def _normalize_query(text: str) -> str:
  return _normalize_space(text).lower()
class AdaptiveRouterCompileExample(BaseModel):
  prompt: str
  complexity: Literal["simple", "complex"]
  query_hints: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
  notes: str = ""
  @field_validator("prompt")
  @classmethod
  def _validate_prompt(cls, value: str) -> str:
    normalized = _normalize_space(value)
    if not normalized:
      raise ValueError("prompt must not be empty")
    return normalized
  @field_validator("query_hints")
  @classmethod
  def _normalize_query_hints(cls, value: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    return [_normalize_space(item) for item in value if _normalize_space(item)]
class AdaptiveRouterPolicyEvaluation(BaseModel):
  examples: int
  complexity_accuracy: float
  query_hint_coverage: float
  average_query_count: float
class PdfAnkiFeedbackExample(BaseModel):
  pipeline: Literal["chunked", "unified"]
  tier: str = ""
  status: Literal["accepted", "edited", "discarded"]
  front: str = ""
  back: str = ""
  notes: str = ""
  @field_validator("tier")
  @classmethod
  def _normalize_tier(cls, value: str) -> str:
    return _normalize_space(value).lower()
  @field_validator("front", "back", "notes")
  @classmethod
  def _normalize_text_fields(cls, value: str) -> str:
    return _normalize_space(value)
class PdfAnkiArtifactEvaluation(BaseModel):
  examples: int
  chunked_examples: int
  unified_examples: int
  duplicate_front_rate: float
  discard_rate: float
  edited_rate: float
  alignment_score: float
def load_jsonl_models[TModel: BaseModel](
  path: str | Path, model_cls: type[TModel]
) -> list[TModel]:
  rows: list[TModel] = []
  source = Path(path)
  for line in source.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
    stripped = line.strip()
    if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#"):
      continue
    rows.append(model_cls.model_validate(json.loads(stripped)))
  return rows
def _router_keyword_candidates(
  examples: list[AdaptiveRouterCompileExample],
) -> list[str]:
  complex_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
  simple_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
  for example in examples:
    target = complex_counts if example.complexity == "complex" else simple_counts
    for token in set(_tokenize(example.prompt)):
      if token in _STOPWORDS:
        continue
      target[token] += 1
  min_complex_support = (
    _ROUTER_MIN_COMPLEX_SUPPORT_SMALL
    if len(examples) < _ROUTER_MIN_EXAMPLES_FOR_SUPPORT
    else _ROUTER_MIN_COMPLEX_SUPPORT_LARGE
  )
  candidates: list[tuple[float, int, int, str]] = []
  for token, complex_count in complex_counts.items():
    simple_count = simple_counts.get(token, 0)
    total = complex_count + simple_count
    precision = complex_count / total
    if (
      complex_count < min_complex_support
      or precision < _ROUTER_PRECISION_THRESHOLD
    ):
      continue
    score = precision + (complex_count / max(_MIN_THRESHOLD_VALUE, len(examples)))
    candidates.append((score, complex_count, -simple_count, token))
  candidates.sort(reverse=True)
  return [token for _, _, _, token in candidates]
def _predict_complexity(
  prompt: str,
  *,
  threshold: int,
  keywords: list[str],
) -> Literal["simple", "complex"]:
  if threshold <= 0:
    raise AssertionError(f"threshold must be positive, got {threshold}")
  prompt_l = prompt.lower()
  effective_threshold = max(_MIN_THRESHOLD_VALUE, threshold)
  if len(prompt) >= effective_threshold:
    return "complex"
  for keyword in keywords:
    if keyword and keyword in prompt_l:
      return "complex"
  return "simple"
def _choose_length_threshold(
  examples: list[AdaptiveRouterCompileExample],
  ranked_keywords: list[str],
  *,
  max_keywords: int,
) -> tuple[int, list[str], float]:
  if max_keywords < 0:
    raise ValueError("max_keywords must be non-negative")
  thresholds = sorted(
    {max(_MIN_THRESHOLD_VALUE, len(example.prompt)) for example in examples}
  )
  if not thresholds:
    return _DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_EMPTY, [], _DEFAULT_ACCURACY_VALUE
  best_threshold: int = thresholds[0]
  best_keywords: list[str] = []
  best_accuracy: float = _INITIAL_BEST_ACCURACY
  max_k: int = min(max_keywords, len(ranked_keywords))
  for keyword_count in range(max_k + 1):
    chosen_keywords: list[str] = ranked_keywords[:keyword_count]
    for threshold in thresholds:
      correct: int = 0
      for example in examples:
        predicted = _predict_complexity(
          example.prompt,
          threshold=threshold,
          keywords=chosen_keywords,
        )
        if predicted == example.complexity:
          correct += 1
      examples_count: int = len(examples)
      accuracy: float = correct / max(_MIN_THRESHOLD_VALUE, examples_count)
      chosen_len: int = len(chosen_keywords)
      best_len: int = len(best_keywords)
      tie_break: bool = accuracy == best_accuracy and chosen_len < best_len
      if accuracy > best_accuracy or tie_break:
        best_threshold = threshold
        best_keywords = list(chosen_keywords)
        best_accuracy = accuracy
  rounded_accuracy: float = round(best_accuracy, _ACCURACY_DECIMAL_PLACES)
  return best_threshold, best_keywords, rounded_accuracy
def _extract_query_suffix(prompt: str, hint: str) -> str | None:
  normalized_prompt = _normalize_space(prompt)
  normalized_hint = _normalize_space(hint)
  if not normalized_prompt or not normalized_hint:
    return None
  if _normalize_query(normalized_prompt) == _normalize_query(normalized_hint):
    return None
  prompt_l = normalized_prompt.lower()
  hint_l = normalized_hint.lower()
  if hint_l.startswith(prompt_l):
    suffix = normalized_hint[len(normalized_prompt) :].strip(
      _QUERY_SUFFIX_STRIP_CHARS
    )
    return suffix or None
  prompt_tokens = set(_tokenize(normalized_prompt))
  extra_tokens = [
    token for token in _tokenize(normalized_hint) if token not in prompt_tokens
  ]
  suffix = " ".join(extra_tokens[:6]).strip()
  return suffix or None
def _build_query_templates(
  examples: list[AdaptiveRouterCompileExample],
  *,
  ranked_keywords: list[str],
  max_queries: int,
) -> list[str]:
  suffix_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
  for example in examples:
    for hint in example.query_hints:
      suffix = _extract_query_suffix(example.prompt, hint)
      if suffix:
        suffix_counts[suffix] += 1
  templates: list[str] = ["{prompt}"]
  suffix_limit: int = max(0, max_queries - 1)
  for suffix, _ in suffix_counts.most_common(suffix_limit):
    template_candidate: str = f"{{prompt}} {suffix}"
    templates.append(template_candidate)
  templates_count: int = len(templates)
  if templates_count < max_queries and ranked_keywords:
    top_keywords: list[str] = ranked_keywords[:3]
    keywords_suffix: str = " ".join(top_keywords).strip()
    if keywords_suffix:
      keyword_template: str = f"{{prompt}} {keywords_suffix}"
      templates.append(keyword_template)
  deduped: list[str] = []
  for template in templates:
    if template not in deduped:
      deduped.append(template)
    if len(deduped) >= max(1, max_queries):
      break
  return deduped
def _build_router_instruction_append(
  examples: list[AdaptiveRouterCompileExample],
) -> str:
  notes_blob: str = " ".join(example.notes for example in examples).lower()
  prompt_blob: str = " ".join(example.prompt for example in examples).lower()
  part1: str = "Answer only from retrieved evidence."
  part2: str = "Prefer concise grounded claims."
  part3: str = "Make uncertainty explicit when retrieval is weak."
  parts: list[str] = [part1, part2, part3]
  tradeoff_tokens: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "compare",
    "tradeoff",
    "cost",
    "latency",
  )
  has_tradeoff: bool = any(
    token in notes_blob or token in prompt_blob for token in tradeoff_tokens
  )
  if has_tradeoff:
    tradeoff_part: str = (
      "Surface tradeoffs directly for comparison-style questions."
    )
    parts.append(tradeoff_part)
  deduped_parts: dict[str, None] = dict.fromkeys(parts)
  result: str = " ".join(deduped_parts.keys())
  return result or _DEFAULT_ROUTER_SYSTEM_APPEND
def compile_adaptive_router_policy(
  examples: list[AdaptiveRouterCompileExample],
  *,
  max_keywords: int = 12,
  max_queries: int = 3,
  compiler_name: str = "heuristic_v1",
) -> AdaptiveRouterDSPyPolicy:
  if not examples:
    raise ValueError("at least one training example is required")
  ranked_keywords = _router_keyword_candidates(examples)
  threshold, chosen_keywords, _accuracy = _choose_length_threshold(
    examples,
    ranked_keywords,
    max_keywords=max_keywords,
  )
  query_templates = _build_query_templates(
    examples,
    ranked_keywords=chosen_keywords or ranked_keywords,
    max_queries=max_queries,
  )
  instruction_append = _build_router_instruction_append(examples)
  evaluation = evaluate_adaptive_router_policy(
    examples,
    AdaptiveRouterDSPyPolicy(
      version="1",
      strategy="heuristic_skip_helpers",
      complexity_length_threshold=threshold,
      complex_keywords=chosen_keywords,
      query_templates=query_templates,
      max_queries=max_queries,
      system_instruction_append=instruction_append,
    ),
  )
  return AdaptiveRouterDSPyPolicy(
    version="1",
    strategy="heuristic_skip_helpers",
    complexity_length_threshold=threshold,
    complex_keywords=chosen_keywords,
    query_templates=query_templates,
    max_queries=max_queries,
    system_instruction_append=instruction_append,
    compiler=compiler_name,
    compiled_at_utc=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
    training_examples=len(examples),
    training_accuracy=evaluation.complexity_accuracy,
    query_hint_coverage=evaluation.query_hint_coverage,
  )
def _query_matches_hint(query: str, hint: str) -> bool:
  if _normalize_query(query) == _normalize_query(hint):
    return True
  hint_tokens = set(_tokenize(hint))
  query_tokens = set(_tokenize(query))
  return bool(hint_tokens) and hint_tokens.issubset(query_tokens)
def evaluate_adaptive_router_policy(
  examples: list[AdaptiveRouterCompileExample],
  policy: AdaptiveRouterDSPyPolicy,
) -> AdaptiveRouterPolicyEvaluation:
  if not examples:
    raise ValueError("at least one evaluation example is required")
  correct = 0
  query_covered = 0
  query_examples = 0
  query_counts: list[int] = []
  for example in examples:
    if policy.classify_prompt(example.prompt) == example.complexity:
      correct += 1
    queries = policy.build_queries(example.prompt)
    query_counts.append(len(queries))
    if example.query_hints:
      query_examples += 1
      if any(
        _query_matches_hint(query, hint)
        for query in queries
        for hint in example.query_hints
      ):
        query_covered += 1
  comp_accuracy = complexity_accuracy(
    correct_count=correct,
    total_count=len(examples),
    decimal_places=_EVALUATION_DECIMAL_PLACES,
  )
  query_hint_coverage = round(
    query_covered / max(_MIN_THRESHOLD_VALUE, query_examples),
    _EVALUATION_DECIMAL_PLACES,
  )
  from statistics import mean as stats_mean
  avg_query_count = round(
    stats_mean(query_counts) if query_counts else _EMPTY_RATE,
    _EVALUATION_DECIMAL_PLACES,
  )
  return AdaptiveRouterPolicyEvaluation(
    examples=len(examples),
    complexity_accuracy=comp_accuracy,
    query_hint_coverage=query_hint_coverage,
    average_query_count=avg_query_count,
  )
def _duplicate_front_rate(examples: list[PdfAnkiFeedbackExample]) -> float:
  fronts = [
    _normalize_query(example.front)
    for example in examples
    if _normalize_query(example.front)
  ]
  if not fronts:
    return _EMPTY_RATE
  return duplicate_front_rate(
    all_fronts_count=len(fronts),
    unique_fronts_count=len(set(fronts)),
  )
def _rate(
  examples: list[PdfAnkiFeedbackExample],
  status: Literal["accepted", "edited", "discarded"],
) -> float:
  if not examples:
    return _EMPTY_RATE
  count = sum(1 for example in examples if example.status == status)
  return rate_by_status(
    total_count=len(examples),
    status_count=count,
  )
def _average_back_words(
  examples: list[PdfAnkiFeedbackExample],
  *,
  tier: str | None = None,
) -> float:
  filtered = [
    example
    for example in examples
    if example.status in {"accepted", "edited"}
    and (tier is None or example.tier == tier)
    and example.back
  ]
  if not filtered:
    return _EMPTY_RATE
  word_counts = [len(example.back.split()) for example in filtered]
  return average_word_count(word_counts)
def _contains_feedback_signal(
  examples: list[PdfAnkiFeedbackExample], *tokens: str
) -> bool:
  blob = " ".join(example.notes.lower() for example in examples)
  return any(token in blob for token in tokens)
def compile_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact(
  examples: list[PdfAnkiFeedbackExample],
  *,
  compiler_name: str = "heuristic_v1",
) -> PdfAnkiDSPyPromptArtifact:
  if not examples:
    raise ValueError("at least one feedback example is required")
  chunked_examples = [
    example for example in examples if example.pipeline == "chunked"
  ]
  unified_examples = [
    example for example in examples if example.pipeline == "unified"
  ]
  free_examples = [
    example for example in examples if example.tier in {"free", "admin"}
  ]
  premium_examples = [example for example in examples if example.tier == "premium"]
  pro_examples = [example for example in examples if example.tier == "pro"]
  chunked_parts = [
    "Favor high-signal, exam-worthy cards.",
    (
      "Prefer definitions, contrasts, formulas, causal links, and "
      "decisions with clear consequences."
    ),
  ]
  chunked_discard_rate = _rate(chunked_examples, "discarded")
  if chunked_discard_rate >= _CHUNKED_DISCARD_THRESHOLD or _contains_feedback_signal(
    chunked_examples, "duplicate", "redund", "broad", "padding"
  ):
    chunked_parts.append(
      "Prefer the smallest set of sharp cards that covers genuinely "
      "new information."
    )
  chunked_back_words = _average_back_words(chunked_examples)
  if chunked_back_words > _EMPTY_RATE:
    chunked_parts.append("Keep fronts clear and backs concise.")
  unified_parts = [
    "Optimize for deck cohesion across the whole document.",
    "Prefer cards that remain understandable after context switching.",
  ]
  duplicate_rate = _duplicate_front_rate(unified_examples)
  if duplicate_rate > _DUPLICATE_RATE_THRESHOLD or _contains_feedback_signal(
    unified_examples, "duplicate", "redund", "overlap"
  ):
    unified_parts.append("Aggressively avoid near-duplicate cards across sections.")
  chunked_tier: dict[str, str] = {}
  if free_examples and (
    _rate(free_examples, "discarded") >= _FREE_TIER_DISCARD_OR_EDIT_THRESHOLD
    or _rate(free_examples, "edited") >= _FREE_TIER_DISCARD_OR_EDIT_THRESHOLD
  ):
    chunked_tier["free"] = (
      "When unsure, produce fewer but sharper cards rather than "
      "padding the chunk."
    )
    chunked_tier["admin"] = chunked_tier["free"]
  unified_tier: dict[str, str] = {}
  if premium_examples and (
    _average_back_words(premium_examples) <= _PREMIUM_TIER_AVG_BACK_WORDS_THRESHOLD
  ):
    unified_tier["premium"] = (
      "Bias toward concise atomic cards over broad summary cards."
    )
  if pro_examples and (
    _average_back_words(pro_examples) >= _PRO_TIER_AVG_BACK_WORDS_THRESHOLD
  ):
    unified_tier["pro"] = (
      "Allow slightly richer answer backs when they add real study "
      "value without introducing redundancy."
    )
  artifact = PdfAnkiDSPyPromptArtifact(
    version="1",
    chunked_system_instruction_append=" ".join(dict.fromkeys(chunked_parts)),
    unified_system_instruction_append=" ".join(dict.fromkeys(unified_parts)),
    chunked_system_instruction_by_tier=chunked_tier,
    unified_system_instruction_by_tier=unified_tier,
    compiler=compiler_name,
    compiled_at_utc=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
    training_examples=len(examples),
  )
  evaluation = evaluate_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact(examples, artifact)
  return artifact.model_copy(
    update={
      "alignment_score": evaluation.alignment_score,
    }
  )
def evaluate_pdf_anki_prompt_artifact(
  examples: list[PdfAnkiFeedbackExample],
  artifact: PdfAnkiDSPyPromptArtifact,
) -> PdfAnkiArtifactEvaluation:
  if not examples:
    raise ValueError("at least one evaluation example is required")
  chunked_examples = [
    example for example in examples if example.pipeline == "chunked"
  ]
  unified_examples = [
    example for example in examples if example.pipeline == "unified"
  ]
  duplicate_rate = round(_duplicate_front_rate(unified_examples), 4)
  discard_rate = round(_rate(examples, "discarded"), 4)
  edited_rate = round(_rate(examples, "edited"), 4)
  checks: list[bool] = []
  chunked_append = artifact.chunked_system_instruction_append.lower()
  unified_append = artifact.unified_system_instruction_append.lower()
  free_tier = artifact.chunked_system_instruction_by_tier.get("free", "").lower()
  premium_tier = artifact.unified_system_instruction_by_tier.get(
    "premium", ""
  ).lower()
  pro_tier = artifact.unified_system_instruction_by_tier.get("pro", "").lower()
  if chunked_examples:
    checks.append(
      "high-signal" in chunked_append
      or "exam-worthy" in chunked_append
      or "sharp" in chunked_append
    )
  if unified_examples:
    checks.append(
      "cohesion" in unified_append or "context switching" in unified_append
    )
  if duplicate_rate > 0.05:
    checks.append("duplicate" in unified_append or "redundan" in unified_append)
  if discard_rate >= 0.2:
    checks.append("fewer" in free_tier or "sharper" in free_tier)
  if premium_tier:
    checks.append("concise" in premium_tier or "atomic" in premium_tier)
  if pro_tier:
    checks.append("richer" in pro_tier or "study value" in pro_tier)
  align_score = alignment_score(checks) if checks else _EMPTY_RATE
  return PdfAnkiArtifactEvaluation(
    examples=len(examples),
    chunked_examples=len(chunked_examples),
    unified_examples=len(unified_examples),
    duplicate_front_rate=duplicate_rate,
    discard_rate=discard_rate,
    edited_rate=edited_rate,
    alignment_score=align_score,
  )
```

### `src/app/core/concurrency.py`
```py
import asyncio
import concurrent.futures
import functools
import math
import multiprocessing
import os
from collections.abc import Callable
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, TypeVar
from uuid import UUID
import structlog
from app.logging_conf import configure_logging
logger = structlog.get_logger()
T = TypeVar("T")
def _run_with_context(
  context_vars: dict[str, Any],
  func: Callable[..., Any],
  *args: Any,
  **kwargs: Any,
) -> Any:
  structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars()
  structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(**context_vars)
  try:
    return func(*args, **kwargs)
  except Exception as e:
    logger.error(
      "cpu_bound_task_error",
      task=getattr(func, "__name__", func.__class__.__name__),
      error=str(e),
    )
    raise
class CpuBoundExecutor:
  _executor: concurrent.futures.Executor | None = None
  _active_tasks = 0
  _active_tasks_callback: Callable[[int], None] | None = None
  @classmethod
  def set_active_tasks_callback(cls, callback: Callable[[int], None] | None) -> None:
    cls._active_tasks_callback = callback
    cls._publish_active_tasks()
  @classmethod
  def active_tasks(cls) -> int:
    return cls._active_tasks
  @classmethod
  def _publish_active_tasks(cls) -> None:
    callback = cls._active_tasks_callback
    if callback is None:
      return
    try:
      callback(cls._active_tasks)
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.warning("cpu_bound_active_tasks_callback_failed", error=str(e))
  @classmethod
  def _increment_active_tasks(cls) -> None:
    cls._active_tasks += 1
    cls._publish_active_tasks()
  @classmethod
  def _decrement_active_tasks(cls) -> None:
    cls._active_tasks = max(0, cls._active_tasks - 1)
    cls._publish_active_tasks()
  @classmethod
  def initialize(cls) -> None:
    if (
      os.getenv("TESTING") == "true"
      or os.getenv("CONTRACT_TESTING") == "true"
      or os.getenv("MOCK_LLM") == "true"
      or os.getenv("MUTANT_UNDER_TEST") is not None
    ):
      logger.info(
        "Testing/Mock mode detected: "
        "CpuBoundExecutor will use asyncio.to_thread (ThreadPool) "
        "instead of ProcessPool to reduce overhead."
      )
      return
    if cls._executor is None:
      max_workers = cls._determine_worker_count()
      try:
        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
        cls._executor = concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor(
          max_workers=max_workers,
          mp_context=ctx,
          initializer=cls._setup_process_logging,
        )
        logger.info(
          "cpu_bound_pool_initialized",
          workers=max_workers,
          context=ctx.get_start_method(),
          type="ProcessPool",
        )
      except (ValueError, OSError, RuntimeError) as e:
        logger.warning(
          "process_pool_initialization_failed",
          error=str(e),
          hint="Falling back to ThreadPoolExecutor. CPU-bound may block GIL.",
        )
        cls._executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
          max_workers=max_workers
        )
  @classmethod
  def _determine_worker_count(cls) -> int:
    env_limit = os.getenv("MAX_CPU_WORKERS")
    if env_limit:
      try:
        return int(env_limit)
      except ValueError:
        logger.warning("Invalid MAX_CPU_WORKERS, falling back to auto-detect.")
    if hasattr(os, "sched_getaffinity"):
      try:
        return len(os.sched_getaffinity(0))
      except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
        logger.info(
          "sched_getaffinity_failed",
          error=str(e),
        )
    cgroup_limit = cls._get_cgroup_cpu_limit()
    if cgroup_limit:
      return max(1, math.ceil(cgroup_limit))
    try:
      count = multiprocessing.cpu_count()
      if count is None:
        return 1
      return max(1, count - 1) if count > 2 else 1
    except (NotImplementedError, OSError):
      return 1
  @staticmethod
  def _get_cgroup_cpu_limit() -> float | None:
    try:
      cpu_max_file = Path("/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max")
      if cpu_max_file.exists():
        quota_str, period_str = cpu_max_file.read_text().strip().split()
        if quota_str != "max":
          return int(quota_str) / int(period_str)
        return None
      quota_file = Path("/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_quota_us")
      period_file = Path("/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_period_us")
      if quota_file.exists() and period_file.exists():
        quota = int(quota_file.read_text().strip())
        period = int(period_file.read_text().strip())
        if quota != -1:
          return quota / period
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.debug(
        "cgroup_cpu_limit_read_failed",
        error=str(e),
        hint="Ignoring CGroup limits, falling back to host CPU count.",
      )
    return None
  @classmethod
  def _setup_process_logging(cls) -> None:
    configure_logging(json_format=True)
  @classmethod
  def _extract_safe_context(cls) -> dict[str, Any]:
    ctx = structlog.contextvars.get_contextvars()
    safe_keys = {"trace_id", "tenant_id", "job_id", "request_id"}
    safe_ctx = {}
    for k, v in ctx.items():
      if k in safe_keys and isinstance(v, str | int | float | bool | UUID):
        safe_ctx[k] = str(v) if isinstance(v, UUID) else v
    return safe_ctx
  @classmethod
  async def run(
    cls,
    func: Callable[..., T],
    *args: Any,
    timeout: float | None = None,
    **kwargs: Any,
  ) -> T:
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    future: asyncio.Future[T]
    if cls._executor is None:
      future = asyncio.ensure_future(asyncio.to_thread(func, *args, **kwargs))
    else:
      safe_context = cls._extract_safe_context()
      call = functools.partial(
        _run_with_context, safe_context, func, *args, **kwargs
      )
      future = loop.run_in_executor(cls._executor, call)
    cls._increment_active_tasks()
    future.add_done_callback(lambda _: cls._decrement_active_tasks())
    if timeout:
      try:
        return await asyncio.wait_for(asyncio.shield(future), timeout=timeout)
      except TimeoutError:
        logger.error(
          "cpu_bound_task_timeout",
          task=getattr(func, "__name__", func.__class__.__name__),
          timeout_s=timeout,
          hint="Task abandoned; may still consume resources in background.",
        )
        raise
    return await future
  @classmethod
  def shutdown(cls) -> None:
    if cls._executor:
      try:
        if hasattr(cls._executor, "shutdown"):
          cls._executor.shutdown(wait=True, cancel_futures=True)
        logger.info("CPU-Bound Executor shut down successfully.")
      except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
        logger.error("cpu_bound_executor_shutdown_error", error=str(e))
      finally:
        cls._executor = None
    cls._active_tasks = 0
    cls._publish_active_tasks()
cpu_executor = CpuBoundExecutor
class _SharedTask:
  def __init__(self, task: asyncio.Task[Any]) -> None:
    self.task = task
    self.waiters = 0  # pragma: no mutate
class AsyncSingleFlight:
  def __init__(self) -> None:
    self._calls: dict[str, _SharedTask] = {}  # pragma: no mutate
  async def do(
    self,
    key: str,
    coro_func: Callable[..., Any],
    *args: Any,
    timeout: float | None = None,
    **kwargs: Any,
  ) -> Any:
    result, _ = await self.do_with_owner(
      key, coro_func, *args, timeout=timeout, **kwargs
    )
    return result
  async def do_with_owner(  # pragma: no mutate
    self,
    key: str,
    coro_func: Callable[..., Any],
    *args: Any,
    timeout: float | None = None,
    **kwargs: Any,
  ) -> tuple[Any, bool]:
    if key in self._calls:  # pragma: no mutate
      shared = self._calls[key]
      shared.waiters += 1  # pragma: no mutate
      is_owner = False
    else:
      async def _wrapper() -> Any:
        try:
          return await coro_func(*args, **kwargs)
        finally:
          if (
            key in self._calls  # pragma: no mutate
            and self._calls[key].task
            is asyncio.current_task()  # pragma: no mutate
          ):
            del self._calls[key]
      task = asyncio.create_task(_wrapper())  # pragma: no mutate
      shared = _SharedTask(task)  # pragma: no mutate
      shared.waiters += 1  # pragma: no mutate
      self._calls[key] = shared
      is_owner = True
    try:
      if timeout is not None:  # pragma: no mutate
        result = await asyncio.wait_for(  # pragma: no mutate
          asyncio.shield(shared.task),
          timeout=timeout,  # pragma: no mutate
        )
      else:
        result = await asyncio.shield(shared.task)  # pragma: no mutate
      return result, is_owner
    except BaseException:  # pragma: no mutate
      shared.waiters -= 1
      if shared.waiters == 0 and not shared.task.done():
        shared.task.cancel()
        current = self._calls.get(key)
        if current is shared:
          del self._calls[key]
      raise
```

### `src/app/core/finops_logger.py`
```py
from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
from functools import wraps
from typing import Any, TypeVar
import structlog
logger = structlog.get_logger()
T = TypeVar("T")
def log_token_usage(
  model_name: str,
  input_tokens: int,
  output_tokens: int,
  estimated_cost_usd: float = 0.0,
  tenant_id: str = "system",
  trace_id: str | None = None,
) -> None:
  logger.info(
    "finops_llm_usage",
    model=model_name,
    input_tokens=input_tokens,
    output_tokens=output_tokens,
    estimated_cost_usd=estimated_cost_usd,
    tenant_id=tenant_id,
    trace_id=trace_id,
  )
def with_finops_logging(
  get_usage: Callable[[Any], dict[str, Any] | None] | None = None,
) -> Callable[[Callable[..., Awaitable[T]]], Callable[..., Awaitable[T]]]:
  def decorator(f: Callable[..., Awaitable[T]]) -> Callable[..., Awaitable[T]]:
    @wraps(f)
    async def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> T:
      result = await f(*args, **kwargs)
      usage: dict[str, Any] | None = None
      if get_usage:
        usage = get_usage(result)
      elif isinstance(result, dict):
        usage = result.get("usage") or result.get("finops")
      if usage and isinstance(usage, dict):
        log_token_usage(
          model_name=str(usage.get("model", "unknown")),
          input_tokens=int(usage.get("input_tokens", 0)),
          output_tokens=int(usage.get("output_tokens", 0)),
          estimated_cost_usd=float(usage.get("estimated_cost_usd", 0.0)),
        )
      return result
    return wrapper
  return decorator
```

### `src/app/core/prompts.py`
```py
import asyncio
import time
from typing import Any
import structlog
from jinja2 import BaseLoader, Environment, TemplateError, select_autoescape
from app.domain.interfaces import CachePort, PromptRepositoryPort
logger = structlog.get_logger()
_MSG_PROMPT_CACHE_READ_ERROR = "prompt_cache_read_error"  # pragma: no mutate
_L1_TTL = 300
_L1_MAXSIZE = 50
FALLBACK_TEMPLATES = {
  "rag_system": (
    "You are a helpful AI assistant. Use the following pieces of "
    "retrieved context to answer the user's question.\n\n"
    "### Context\n"
    "{{ context }}\n\n"
    "### Instructions\n"
    "1. The context contains previous Q&A pairs enclosed in "
    "<knowledge> tags.\n"
    "2. Extract the relevant information from the <answer> "
    "sections.\n"
    "3. Synthesize a natural response to the user's question based "
    "ONLY on this information. + IMPORTANT: DO NOT add external "
    "knowledge, definitions, or explanations that are not explicitly "
    "written in the context. If the context is brief, your answer "
    "must be brief.\n"
    "4. If the context does not contain the answer, say 'I do not "
    "have enough information'."
  ),
  "classifier_system": (
    "You are a semantic classifier. Analyze the user's prompt and "
    "determine its complexity (simple/complex) and reasoning.\n"
    "IMPORTANT: You MUST return ONLY a valid JSON object with exactly "
    "two keys. Do NOT generate a response to the user's prompt.\n"
    "Schema:\n"
    '{"complexity": "simple|complex", "reasoning": "Brief explanation"}'
  ),
}
class PromptManager:
  def __init__(self, cache: CachePort, repo: PromptRepositoryPort) -> None:
    self.env = Environment(
      loader=BaseLoader(),
      autoescape=select_autoescape(  # pragma: no mutate
        default=False,
        default_for_string=False,
      ),
    )
    self.cache = cache
    self.repo = repo
    self._l1: dict[
      tuple[str, str], tuple[tuple[str | None, str | None], float]
    ] = {}
    self._l1_lock = asyncio.Lock()
  async def _cache_template(self, key: str, content: str) -> None:
    try:
      await self.cache.set(key, content, ttl=300)
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.warning("prompt_cache_failed", key=key, error=str(e))
  async def _get_template_content(
    self,
    name: str,
    version: str = "latest",  # pragma: no mutate
  ) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
    key = (name, version)  # pragma: no mutate
    now = time.monotonic()
    async with self._l1_lock:
      if key in self._l1:
        entry, expiry = self._l1[key]
        if now <= expiry:  # pragma: no mutate
          return entry
        del self._l1[key]
    cache_key = f"prompt:{name}:{version}"
    try:
      cached = await self.cache.get(cache_key)
      if cached:
        result: tuple[str | None, str | None] = (str(cached), None)
        async with self._l1_lock:
          self._prune_l1()
          self._l1[key] = (result, now + _L1_TTL)  # pragma: no mutate
        return result
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.warning(_MSG_PROMPT_CACHE_READ_ERROR, name=name, error=str(e))
    template_obj = await self.repo.get_template(name, version)  # pragma: no mutate
    if template_obj:
      await self._cache_template(  # pragma: no mutate
        cache_key, template_obj.content
      )
      result = (
        str(template_obj.content),
        template_obj.input_variables,
      )
      async with self._l1_lock:
        self._prune_l1()
        self._l1[key] = (result, now + _L1_TTL)  # pragma: no mutate
      return result
    return None, None
  def _prune_l1(self) -> None:
    now = time.monotonic()  # pragma: no mutate
    to_drop = [
      k
      for k, (_, exp) in self._l1.items()
      if exp <= now  # pragma: no mutate
    ]
    for k in to_drop:
      del self._l1[k]
    while len(self._l1) > _L1_MAXSIZE:  # pragma: no mutate
      oldest = next(iter(self._l1))  # pragma: no mutate
      del self._l1[oldest]
  def _l1_clear(self) -> None:
    self._l1.clear()
  def _validate_inputs(
    self, template_name: str, input_vars: str | None, kwargs: dict[str, Any]
  ) -> None:
    if not input_vars:
      return
    required = {v.strip() for v in input_vars.split(",") if v.strip()}
    provided = set(kwargs.keys())
    missing = required - provided
    if missing:
      logger.warning(
        "Prompt rendering missing variables",
        template=template_name,
        missing=list(missing),
      )
  async def render(  # pragma: no mutate
    self,
    template_name: str,
    version: str = "latest",  # pragma: no mutate
    **kwargs: Any,
  ) -> str:
    content, input_vars = await self._get_template_content(  # pragma: no mutate
      template_name, version
    )
    if not content:
      logger.warning(
        "prompt_not_found_using_fallback",
        template=template_name,
      )
      content = FALLBACK_TEMPLATES.get(  # pragma: no mutate
        template_name, "You are a helpful AI."
      )
    self._validate_inputs(template_name, input_vars, kwargs)
    try:
      template = self.env.from_string(content)  # pragma: no mutate
      return str(template.render(**kwargs))  # pragma: no mutate
    except TemplateError as e:
      logger.error(
        "jinja2_rendering_error",
        template=template_name,
        error=str(e),
      )
      return "System Error: Invalid Prompt Template."  # pragma: no mutate
```

### `src/app/core/stream_manager.py`
```py
import time
from collections.abc import Callable, Coroutine
from typing import Any
import structlog
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict
from app.domain.interfaces import (
  BackgroundRunner,
  CostCalculatorPort,
  SemanticCachePort,
  StreamChunk,
  TokenManagerPort,
)
logger = structlog.get_logger()
_DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"
class RouterContext(BaseModel):
  safe_prompt: str
  embedding: list[float] | None = None
  complexity: str = "simple"
  model: str
  system_message: str = ""
  cached_response: str | None = None
  cache_type: str | None = None
  prompt_tokens: int = 0
  embedding_tokens: int = 0
  routing_policy: str | None = None
  dspy_artifact_version: str | None = None
  helper_calls_skipped: int = 0
  expanded_query_count: int = 0
  model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)
class StreamFinOpsManager:
  def __init__(  # pragma: no mutate
    self,
    ctx: RouterContext,
    token_manager: TokenManagerPort,
    cost_calculator: CostCalculatorPort,
    semantic_cache: SemanticCachePort,
    background_runner: BackgroundRunner,
    persistence_callback: Callable[..., Coroutine[Any, Any, None]],
    embedding_model: str = _DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL,
  ) -> None:
    self.ctx = ctx  # pragma: no mutate
    self.token_manager = token_manager  # pragma: no mutate
    self.cost_calculator = cost_calculator  # pragma: no mutate
    self.semantic_cache = semantic_cache  # pragma: no mutate
    self.runner = background_runner  # pragma: no mutate
    self.persistence_callback = persistence_callback  # pragma: no mutate
    self._embedding_model = embedding_model  # pragma: no mutate
    self.full_response: list[str] = []  # pragma: no mutate
    self.real_usage: dict[str, int] | None = None  # pragma: no mutate
    self.stream_error: str | None = None  # pragma: no mutate
    self.is_cancelled: bool = False  # pragma: no mutate
    self.is_complete: bool = False  # pragma: no mutate
    self.start_time = time.perf_counter()  # pragma: no mutate
  def consume(self, chunk: StreamChunk) -> None:
    if chunk.usage:
      self.real_usage = chunk.usage
    if chunk.content:
      self.full_response.append(chunk.content)
  def set_error(self, error: str) -> None:
    self.stream_error = error
    self.is_complete = False
  def set_cancelled(self) -> None:
    self.is_cancelled = True
    self.is_complete = False
  def set_complete(self) -> None:
    self.is_complete = True
  async def finalize(self) -> None:  # pragma: no mutate
    final_text = "".join(self.full_response)  # pragma: no mutate
    if self.stream_error:  # pragma: no mutate
      final_text += self.stream_error  # pragma: no mutate
    elif self.is_cancelled:  # pragma: no mutate
      final_text += " [Stream Cancelled]"  # pragma: no mutate
    if self.stream_error and not self.full_response:  # pragma: no mutate
      prompt_tokens = 0  # pragma: no mutate
      completion_tokens = 0  # pragma: no mutate
      total_tokens = 0  # pragma: no mutate
    elif self.real_usage:  # pragma: no mutate
      prompt_tokens = self.real_usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)  # pragma: no mutate
      completion_tokens = self.real_usage.get(  # pragma: no mutate
        "completion_tokens",
        0,  # pragma: no mutate
      )
      total_tokens = self.real_usage.get("total_tokens", 0)  # pragma: no mutate
    else:  # pragma: no mutate
      prompt_tokens = self.ctx.prompt_tokens  # pragma: no mutate
      if prompt_tokens == 0:  # pragma: no mutate
        try:
          full_prompt = f"{self.ctx.system_message}\n{self.ctx.safe_prompt}"
          prompt_tokens = (
            await self.token_manager.count_tokens(  # pragma: no mutate
              full_prompt, self.ctx.model
            )
          )
        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
          logger.warning(
            "prompt_token_counting_fallback_failed",
            error=str(e),
          )
          prompt_tokens = (
            len(self.ctx.system_message) + len(self.ctx.safe_prompt)
          ) // 4
      try:  # pragma: no mutate
        completion_tokens = (
          await self.token_manager.count_tokens(  # pragma: no mutate
            final_text, self.ctx.model
          )
        )
      except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
        logger.warning(
          "completion_token_counting_fallback_failed",
          error=str(e),
        )
        completion_tokens = len(final_text) // 4  # pragma: no mutate
      total_tokens = prompt_tokens + completion_tokens  # pragma: no mutate
    cost = self.cost_calculator.calculate_cost(  # pragma: no mutate
      self.ctx.model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens
    )
    if self.ctx.embedding_tokens > 0:  # pragma: no mutate
      emb_cost = self.cost_calculator.calculate_cost(
        self._embedding_model, self.ctx.embedding_tokens, 0
      )
      cost += emb_cost  # pragma: no mutate
    latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - self.start_time) * 1000  # pragma: no mutate
    if (  # pragma: no mutate
      self.is_complete
      and not self.stream_error
      and not self.is_cancelled
      and final_text
      and not self.ctx.cached_response
    ):
      await self.semantic_cache.store_exact_match(  # pragma: no mutate
        self.ctx.safe_prompt, final_text
      )
    await self.persistence_callback(  # pragma: no mutate
      prompt=self.ctx.safe_prompt,
      response_content=final_text,
      embedding=self.ctx.embedding,
      model_used=self.ctx.model,
      usage={
        "total_tokens": total_tokens,
        "prompt_tokens": prompt_tokens,
        "completion_tokens": completion_tokens,
      },
      cost_usd=cost,
      latency_ms=latency_ms,
    )
```

### `src/app/core/dspy_dataset_heuristics.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
import math
from typing import Any
_PROMPT_LENGTH_THRESHOLD_COMPLEX: int = 120
_LATENCY_MS_THRESHOLD_COMPLEX: int = 900
_TIER_FREE: str = "free"
_TIER_ADMIN: str = "admin"
_TIER_PREMIUM: str = "premium"
_TIER_PRO: str = "pro"
def is_missing_value(value: Any) -> bool:
  if value is None:
    return True
  if isinstance(value, float):
    return math.isnan(value)
  return False
def infer_complexity_from_signals(
  *,
  prompt_len: int,
  model_used: str,
  smart_model: str,
  fast_model: str,
  latency_ms: float,
) -> str:
  if not isinstance(prompt_len, int):
    raise TypeError(f"prompt_len must be int, got {type(prompt_len)}")
  if not isinstance(latency_ms, (int, float)):
    raise TypeError(f"latency_ms must be numeric, got {type(latency_ms)}")
  model_norm: str = model_used.strip().lower()
  smart_norm: str = smart_model.strip().lower()
  fast_norm: str = fast_model.strip().lower()
  if model_norm == smart_norm:
    return "complex"
  if model_norm == fast_norm:
    return "simple"
  if prompt_len >= _PROMPT_LENGTH_THRESHOLD_COMPLEX:
    return "complex"
  if latency_ms >= _LATENCY_MS_THRESHOLD_COMPLEX:
    return "complex"
  return "simple"
def should_use_unified_pipeline(tier: str | None, payg_unlocked: bool) -> bool:
  if not isinstance(payg_unlocked, bool):
    raise TypeError(f"payg_unlocked must be bool, got {type(payg_unlocked)}")
  tier_norm: str = (tier or "free").strip().lower()
  if tier_norm == _TIER_PREMIUM:
    return True
  if tier_norm == _TIER_PRO:
    return True
  return tier_norm not in (_TIER_FREE, _TIER_ADMIN) and payg_unlocked
def normalize_tier_string(tier: str | None) -> str:
  if tier is not None and not isinstance(tier, str):
    raise TypeError(f"tier must be str or None, got {type(tier)}")
  normalized: str = (tier or "free").strip().lower()
  if not normalized:
    normalized = "free"
  return normalized
def validate_tier_tier_name(tier_name: str) -> bool:
  if not isinstance(tier_name, str):
    raise TypeError(f"tier_name must be str, got {type(tier_name)}")
  if len(tier_name) == 0:
    raise ValueError("tier_name cannot be empty")
  recognized_tiers: tuple[str, ...] = (
    _TIER_FREE,
    _TIER_ADMIN,
    _TIER_PREMIUM,
    _TIER_PRO,
  )
  return tier_name in recognized_tiers
```

### `src/app/core/tracing.py`
```py
import functools
from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable, Coroutine
from typing import Any, ParamSpec, TypeVar
from opentelemetry import trace
P = ParamSpec("P")
R = TypeVar("R")
def redact_trace_identifier(value: object, *, visible_prefix: int = 4) -> str | None:
  text = str(value or "").strip()
  if not text:
    return None
  if visible_prefix <= 0 or len(text) <= visible_prefix:
    return "[REDACTED]"
  return f"{text[:visible_prefix]}***"
def trace_span(
  name: str,
) -> Callable[
  [Callable[P, Awaitable[R]]],
  Callable[P, Coroutine[Any, Any, R]],
]:
  def decorator(
    func: Callable[P, Awaitable[R]],
  ) -> Callable[P, Coroutine[Any, Any, R]]:
    @functools.wraps(func)
    async def wrapper(*args: P.args, **kwargs: P.kwargs) -> R:
      tracer = trace.get_tracer(__name__)
      with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
        try:
          span.set_attribute("code.function", func.__name__)
          span.set_attribute("code.module", func.__module__)
          result = await func(*args, **kwargs)
          span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.OK))
          return result
        except Exception as e:
          span.record_exception(e)
          span.set_status(trace.Status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(e)))
          raise
    return wrapper
  return decorator
```

### `src/app/core/model_fallbacks.py`
```py
from __future__ import annotations
from decimal import Decimal
from app.core.pricing_registry import get_model_pricing
_WEIGHT_UNKNOWN_MODEL = 1
_WEIGHT_KNOWN_MODEL = 0
_ZERO_COST = Decimal("0")
_FALLBACK_LLM_MODEL = "gpt-4-turbo"
_FALLBACK_EMBEDDING_MODEL = "text-embedding-3-large"
_FALLBACK_RERANKER_MODEL = "mixedbread-ai/mxbai-rerank-v1"
def _model_cost_weight(model_name: str) -> tuple[int, Decimal, str]:
  pricing = get_model_pricing(model_name)
  if pricing is None:
    return (_WEIGHT_UNKNOWN_MODEL, _ZERO_COST, model_name)
  total = (
    pricing.input_price_per_m_tokens
    + pricing.output_price_per_m_tokens
    + pricing.audio_price_per_m_tokens
  )
  return (_WEIGHT_KNOWN_MODEL, total, model_name)
def cheaper_vertex_fallback_chain(
  *,
  allowlist: tuple[str, ...],
  selected_model: str | None,
  configured_default: str,
) -> tuple[str, ...]:
  allowed = tuple(dict.fromkeys(m.strip() for m in allowlist if m.strip()))
  if not allowed:
    fallback = configured_default.strip()
    if not fallback:
      raise ValueError("configured_default must provide a non-empty fallback")
    return (fallback,)
  allowed_set = frozenset(allowed)
  selected = (selected_model or "").strip()
  default = configured_default.strip()
  if selected not in allowed_set:
    selected = (
      default if default in allowed_set else min(allowed, key=_model_cost_weight)
    )
  selected_key = _model_cost_weight(selected)
  cheaper = [
    model
    for model in allowed
    if model != selected and _model_cost_weight(model) <= selected_key
  ]
  cheaper.sort(key=_model_cost_weight, reverse=True)
  return (selected, *cheaper)
def fallback_llm_model(primary: str | None) -> str:
  if primary is not None and not isinstance(primary, str):
    raise TypeError(f"primary must be str or None, got {type(primary)}")
  cleaned = (primary or "").strip()
  if cleaned:
    return cleaned
  return _FALLBACK_LLM_MODEL
def fallback_embedding_model(primary: str | None) -> str:
  if primary is not None and not isinstance(primary, str):
    raise TypeError(f"primary must be str or None, got {type(primary)}")
  cleaned = (primary or "").strip()
  if cleaned:
    return cleaned
  return _FALLBACK_EMBEDDING_MODEL
def fallback_reranker_model(primary: str | None) -> str:
  if primary is not None and not isinstance(primary, str):
    raise TypeError(f"primary must be str or None, got {type(primary)}")
  cleaned = (primary or "").strip()
  if cleaned:
    return cleaned
  return _FALLBACK_RERANKER_MODEL
```

### `src/app/core/retrieval.py`
```py
import asyncio
import hashlib
from typing import cast
import structlog
from app.core.concurrency import AsyncSingleFlight
from app.core.context import AppContext
from app.core.prompts import PromptManager
from app.core.query_expansion import QueryExpander
from app.core.tracing import trace_span
from app.domain.interfaces import (
  BackgroundRunner,
  CachePort,
  LLMProvider,
  RerankerPort,
  SearchEnginePort,
  TokenManagerPort,
)
logger = structlog.get_logger()
_DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"
_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_RESERVE = 2048
class RetrievalPipeline:
  def __init__(
    self,
    llm_provider: LLMProvider,
    search_engine: SearchEnginePort,
    cache: CachePort,
    expander: QueryExpander,
    reranker: RerankerPort,
    token_manager: TokenManagerPort,
    prompt_manager: PromptManager,
    background_runner: BackgroundRunner,
    singleflight: AsyncSingleFlight | None = None,
    *,
    embedding_model: str = _DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL,
    output_reserve: int = _DEFAULT_OUTPUT_RESERVE,
  ) -> None:
    self.llm = llm_provider
    self.search_engine = search_engine
    self.cache = cache
    self.expander = expander
    self.reranker = reranker
    self.token_manager = token_manager
    self.prompt_manager = prompt_manager
    self.runner = background_runner
    self._singleflight = singleflight or AsyncSingleFlight()
    self._embedding_model = embedding_model
    self._output_reserve = output_reserve
  def _get_cache_key(self, text: str) -> str:
    h = hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()
    return f"embedding:{self._embedding_model}:{h}"
  @trace_span("retrieval.get_embeddings_batch")
  async def get_embeddings_batched(
    self, texts: list[str]
  ) -> list[list[float] | None]:
    if not texts:
      return []
    cache_keys = [self._get_cache_key(t) for t in texts]
    try:
      cached_data = await self.cache.get_many(cache_keys)
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.warning("embedding_cache_read_failed", error=str(e))
      cached_data = {}
    results: dict[str, list[float] | None] = {}
    missing_texts: list[str] = []
    missing_keys: list[str] = []
    for key, text in zip(cache_keys, texts, strict=True):
      if cached_data.get(key):
        results[text] = cast(list[float], cached_data[key])
      else:
        missing_texts.append(text)
        missing_keys.append(key)
    if missing_texts:
      try:
        sorted_pairs = sorted(zip(missing_texts, missing_keys, strict=True))
        sorted_texts = [text for text, _ in sorted_pairs]
        sorted_keys = [key for _, key in sorted_pairs]
        h = hashlib.sha256()
        for text in sorted_texts:
          h.update(text.encode("utf-8"))
        batch_hash = h.hexdigest()
        batch_key = f"embed_batch:{self._embedding_model}:{batch_hash}"
        vectors = await self._singleflight.do(
          batch_key, self.llm.embed_batch, sorted_texts, timeout=15.0
        )
        token_tasks = [
          self.token_manager.count_tokens(t, self._embedding_model)
          for t in sorted_texts
        ]
        token_counts = await asyncio.gather(
          *token_tasks, return_exceptions=True
        )
        for count in token_counts:
          if isinstance(count, int):
            AppContext.add_embedding_tokens(count)
        to_cache: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
        for text, key, vector in zip(
          sorted_texts, sorted_keys, vectors, strict=True
        ):
          to_cache[key] = vector
          results[text] = vector
        self.runner.fire(self.cache.set_many(to_cache))
      except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
        logger.error("batch_embedding_generation_failed", error=str(e))
        for text in missing_texts:
          results[text] = None
    return [results.get(text) for text in texts]
  async def get_single_embedding(self, text: str) -> list[float] | None:
    results = await self.get_embeddings_batched([text])
    return results[0] if results else None
  @trace_span("retrieval.execute")
  async def execute(
    self,
    safe_prompt: str,
    selected_model: str,
    primary_embedding: list[float] | None = None,
    *,
    expanded_queries_override: list[str] | None = None,
    system_instruction_append: str | None = None,
  ) -> tuple[str, list[float] | None, int]:
    if expanded_queries_override is None:
      expanded_queries = await self.expander.expand(safe_prompt)
    else:
      expanded_queries = list(
        dict.fromkeys(
          query.strip()
          for query in expanded_queries_override
          if query.strip()
        )
      )
      if not expanded_queries:
        expanded_queries = [safe_prompt]
    texts_to_embed = []
    for q in expanded_queries:
      if q == safe_prompt and primary_embedding is not None:
        continue
      texts_to_embed.append(q)
    new_embeddings = []
    if texts_to_embed:
      new_embeddings = await self.get_embeddings_batched(texts_to_embed)
    valid_embeddings = []
    new_emb_idx = 0
    for q in expanded_queries:
      if q == safe_prompt and primary_embedding is not None:
        valid_embeddings.append(primary_embedding)
      else:
        emb = new_embeddings[new_emb_idx]
        if emb is not None:
          valid_embeddings.append(emb)
        new_emb_idx += 1
    final_primary_embedding = valid_embeddings[0] if valid_embeddings else None
    search_filters = {
      "tenant_id": AppContext.get_tenant_id(),
      "lookback_days": 365,
    }
    try:
      search_results = await self.search_engine.search(
        queries=expanded_queries,
        embeddings=valid_embeddings if valid_embeddings else None,
        filters=search_filters,
      )
    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
      logger.error("search_engine_failed", error=str(e))
      search_results = []
    initial_candidates = [log.to_few_shot_example() for log in search_results]
    relevant_examples = await self.reranker.rerank(safe_prompt, initial_candidates)
    base_system_template = await self.prompt_manager.render(
      "rag_system", context=""
    )
    overhead = await self.token_manager.count_tokens(
      base_system_template, selected_model
    )
    prompt_tokens = await self.token_manager.count_tokens(
      safe_prompt, selected_model
    )
    limit = self.token_manager.get_limit(selected_model)
    used = overhead + prompt_tokens + self._output_reserve
    available_tokens = limit - used
    if available_tokens < 0:
      logger.warning(
        "Prompt too long for context injection.",
        model=selected_model,
        prompt_tokens=prompt_tokens,
      )
      context_str = ""
    else:
      safe_examples = await self.token_manager.trim_context(
        relevant_examples, available_tokens, selected_model
      )
      context_str = "\n---\n".join(safe_examples)
    system_message = await self.prompt_manager.render(
      "rag_system", context=context_str
    )
    if system_instruction_append:
      system_message = (
        f"{system_message}\n\n{system_instruction_append.strip()}"
      ).strip()
    total_prompt_tokens = await self.token_manager.count_tokens(
      f"{system_message}\n{safe_prompt}", selected_model
    )
    return system_message, final_primary_embedding, total_prompt_tokens
```
